title: Aadi Baklawа
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- paste as No. 86
- wheat starch
- fresh butter
- pounded almonds, pistachios, or filberts
- half a pound or a little more of scalded fresh butter
- a pound of nice honey or loaf-sugar
- two pints of water
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Make sufficient paste as No. 86
2. Divide it into fifty pieces the size of walnuts, sprinkle a little wheat starch under and over, and roll them out with a rolling-pin as thin as tissue-paper, quite round, and the size of the baking-tin you intend to lay them in
3. Butter the tin, in which lay five of the pieces of paste one over the other
4. Sprinkle a little fresh butter over the top one, and lay five more pieces over
5. Sprinkle either pounded almonds, pistachios, or filberts, all over, and lay five more pieces of paste over
6. Butter again, and lay five more pieces of paste
7. Sprinkle either of the above ingredients, and lay five more pieces of paste over, butter again, and so forth, until you have laid all the pieces of paste
8. Cut it up in diamonds, pour about half a pound or a little more of scalded fresh butter all over, and bake it a nice colour in a hot oven
9. While it is baking, put a pound of nice honey or loaf-sugar into a saucepan, with two pints of water, and set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
10. When the paste is nicely browned, pour it gently all over, and let it remain for five minutes or so
11. Dish up in pyramid, sprinkle some powdered sugar over, and serve hot or cold

title: Aadi Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five eggs
- salt
- pepper
- two ounces of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Break four or five eggs in a basin
2. Add a little salt and pepper
3. Beat them well up with a fork
4. Put into a very clean frying-pan about two ounces of fresh butter
5. Put it on the fire
6. When hot, pour in the eggs
7. Keep stirring till all is nicely set
8. Let it slip to the edge of the pan, laying hold by the handle and raising it slantwise, which will give an elongated shape to the omelette
9. Turn in the edges
10. Let it set a moment
11. Turn it over on to a dish
12. Serve

title: Aadi Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pints of water
- a pound of the best rice, well washed
- sufficient salt
- half a pound of fresh butter
- a wet cloth

## Directions

1. Pour two pints of water in a saucepan, and put it on the fire
2. When boiling, throw in a pound of the best rice, well washed, and sufficient salt, and boil it until the whole of the liquid is absorbed
3. Put in a saucepan half a pound of fresh butter, and set it on the fire until it boils
4. Pour it all at once over the rice, put the cover over the pan, wrap a wet cloth round it, to prevent the steam from escaping, and keep it on the hob for half an hour
5. Take the lid off and stir it once round with a fork
6. Dish up in the shape of a dome, and serve hot

title: Aadi Sherub
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of loaf sugar
- two pints of cold water
- the white of an egg
- a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or other essence

## Directions

1. Put three pounds of loaf sugar in a clean stewpan, with two pints of cold water
2. Whip up the white of an egg, and add it to the sugar
3. Stir well with a wooden spoon, and set it to boil until it becomes a little thickish
4. Skim it well, and just before you take it off, add a few drops of the essence of orange-flower, or any other essence you choose
5. Pass it through a clean cloth

title: Aadi Yaprah-Dolmdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vine leaves
- raw mutton
- one or two onions
- three or four tablespoonfuls of rice
- salt
- pepper
- water
- broth

## Directions

1. Prepare some vine leaves as No. 111
2. Chop up fine a sufficient quantity of raw mutton with one or two onions
3. Add three or four tablespoonfuls of rice well washed, a little salt and pepper
4. Mix them well together with a little water to make it like a paste
5. Lay the vine leaves open and put in each one a teaspoonful of this stuffing
6. Wrap them up about the length and the thickness of your thumb
7. Squeeze them gently with your hand one by one so that they may retain the stuffing while cooking
8. Place them in a saucepan without any water and set it on the fire with the cover over
9. When their own moisture is exhausted, add some of the broth
10. Let it simmer until it forms a nice gravy at the bottom
11. Take one out and try if the meat and the rice are done
12. If not, add some more broth
13. When the rice and the meat are quite tender, dish up and serve

title: Aadi Yäpräh-Dolmessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vine leaves
- raw mutton
- one or two onions
- three or four tablespoonfuls of rice well washed
- salt
- pepper
- water
- broth

## Directions

1. Prepare some vine leaves
2. Chop up fine a sufficient quantity of raw mutton with one or two onions
3. Add three or four tablespoonfuls of rice well washed, a little salt and pepper, and mix them well together with a little water, to make it like a paste
4. Lay the vine leaves open, put in each one a teaspoonful of this stuffing, and wrap them up about the length and the thickness of your thumb
5. Squeeze them gently with your hand one by one, that they may retain the stuffing while cooking
6. Place them in a saucepan without any water, and set it on the fire with the cover over
7. When their own moisture is exhausted, add some of the broth, and let it simmer until it forms a nice gravy at the bottom
8. Take one out and try if the meat and the rice are done; if not, add some more broth
9. When the rice and the meat are quite tender, dish up, and serve

title: Agdi Tel Kadciyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- water
- three-quarters of a pound of scalded fresh butter
- a pound of white sugar
- two pints of water

## Directions

1. Procure a tin pint pot, the shape of a tumbler, insert in the bottom eight or ten fine tubes
2. Put some flour in a basin, which mix with water to form a smooth batter
3. Put a portion of the batter in the pot, let it run through the tubes, by passing the pot up and down on a very hot crumpet-stove, which is done in a few minutes in long threads like vermicelli
4. Take them up, and spread them on a tray, and continue in this way till you have made about three pounds
5. Lay the vermicelli even in a buttered baking-tin, pour three-quarters of a pound of scalded fresh butter over
6. Bake it a nice brown on both sides in the oven, or on a charcoal fire
7. Put a pound of white sugar in a stewpan, with two pints of water, and set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
8. Skim, and keep it hot
9. When the vermicelli is done, pour the syrup all over
10. If you wish it to be crisp, leave it uncovered; if soft, cover it the moment you have poured the syrup over, and let it remain for fifteen minutes or so
11. Cut it in four crossways, or any other shape you fancy, dish up
12. If any syrup is left in the baking tin, pour it over, and serve hot or cold

title: Agdi Tel Kâdâyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- water
- three-quarters of a pound of scalded fresh butter

## Directions

1. Procure a tin pint pot, the shape of a tumbler, insert in the bottom eight or ten fine tubes
2. Put some flour in a basin, which mix with water to form a smooth batter
3. Put a portion of the batter in the pot, let it run through the tubes, by passing the pot up and down on a very hot crumpet-stove, which is done in a few minutes in long threads like vermicelli
4. Take them up, and spread them on a tray, and continue in this way till you have made about three pounds
5. Lay the vermicelli even in a buttered baking-tin, pour three-quarters of a pound of scalded fresh butter over, and bake it a nice brown on both sides in the oven, or on a charcoal fire
6. Put a pound of white sugar in a stewpan, with two pints of water, and set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
7. Skim, and keep it hot
8. When the vermicelli is done, pour the syrup all over
9. If you wish it to be crisp, leave it uncovered; if soft, cover it the moment you have poured the syrup over, and let it remain for fifteen minutes or so
10. Cut it in four crossways, or any other shape you fancy, dish up
11. If any syrup is left in the baking tin, pour it over, and serve hot or cold

title: Ajem Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of nice mutton
- three or four ounces of fresh butter
- three or four finely chopped onions
- one or two handfuls of pistachios
- one or two handfuls of currants
- a teaspoonful of mixed spice
- two pounds of the best rice, well washed
- two quarts of cold water
- salt
- flour paste

## Directions

1. Cut three pounds of nice mutton in pieces about the size of walnuts
2. Place them in a stewpan
3. If the meat is not fat, add three or four ounces of fresh butter
4. Put the pan on a charcoal fire, and let the meat stew till quite brown, but not burnt, and the fat is as clear as oil
5. Take out the pieces of meat with a hand strainer, and put them in a basin
6. Put three or four finely chopped onions in the remaining fat, and fry them a nice brown
7. Lay the pieces of meat over
8. Add one or two handfuls of pistachios, the same of currants, a teaspoonful of mixed spice, two pounds of the best rice, well washed
9. Pour gently two quarts of cold water over
10. Add sufficient salt
11. Put the cover over the pan, and cement round it with flour paste, so as to keep the steam in
12. Put the pan on the fire, and let it boil gently until the whole of the liquid is absorbed
13. Take off the cover, and turn the contents of the pan carefully on to a hot dish, and serve

title: Ajem Pilawi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of nice mutton
- three or four ounces of fresh butter
- three or four finely chopped onions
- one or two handfuls of pistachios
- one or two handfuls of currants
- a teaspoonful of mixed spice
- two pounds of the best rice, well washed
- two quarts of cold water
- sufficient salt
- flour paste

## Directions

1. Cut three pounds of nice mutton in pieces about the size of walnuts, place them in a stewpan
2. If the meat is not fat, add three or four ounces of fresh butter
3. Put the pan on a charcoal fire, and let the meat stew till quite brown, but not burnt, and the fat is as clear as oil
4. Take out the pieces of meat with a hand strainer, and put them in a basin
5. Put three or four finely chopped onions in the remaining fat, and fry them a nice brown
6. Lay the pieces of meat over, add one or two handfuls of pistachios, the same of currants, a teaspoonful of mixed spice, two pounds of the best rice, well washed
7. Pour gently two quarts of cold water over, add sufficient salt, put the cover over the pan, and cement round it with flour paste, so as to keep the steam in
8. Put the pan on the fire, and let it boil gently until the whole of the liquid is absorbed
9. Take off the cover, and turn the contents of the pan carefully on to a hot dish, and serve

title: Assude Helwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of wheat-starch
- water
- a pound of the best white honey
- half a pound of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put in a clean stewpan a pound of wheat-starch, a pint of water, a pound of the best white honey, and half a pound of fresh butter melted
2. Put it on the fire and commence stirring until the butter begins to appear on the surface of the mixture
3. Keep stirring from the bottom of the pan and pressing it with the back of the spoon for a few minutes longer
4. Dish up like a dome and serve

title: Assude Helwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of wheat-starch
- a pint of water
- a pound of the best white honey
- half a pound of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put in a clean stewpan a pound of wheat-starch, a pint of water, a pound of the best white honey, and half a pound of fresh butter, melted
2. Put it on the fire and commence stirring until the butter begins to appear on the surface of the mixture
3. Keep stirring from the bottom of the pan and pressing it with the back of the spoon for a few minutes longer
4. Dish up like a dome and serve

title: Aywa Murebbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 pounds quinces
- white of 1 egg
- 3 pounds powdered sugar
- 6 pints of water
- alum the size of half a pea
- prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces and cut each one in quarters
2. Remove the cores
3. Beat up the white of an egg in a stewpan
4. Add 3 pounds of powdered sugar and 6 pints of water
5. Put it on the fire
6. When boiling, skim it
7. Add the fruit and boil it till reduced to a pulp
8. Take it off and pass it through a clean cloth into another stewpan
9. Add a piece of alum the size of half a pea
10. Set it again on the fire and let it boil gently for a minute or two
11. Skim and pass it again through a close muslin into the pan
12. Add a few drops of prepared cochineal
13. Put it again on the fire and let it boil until it becomes a thick syrup
14. Take it off and fill the preserve jars or glass bowls
15. When cold, cover them and keep them in a cool place

title: Aywa Murebbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- about fourteen pounds of quinces
- the white of an egg
- three pounds of powdered sugar
- six pints of water
- a piece of alum the size of half a pea
- a few drops of prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Procure about fourteen pounds of quinces, peel them, cut each one in quarters, and remove the cores.
2. Beat up the white of an egg in a stewpan and add three pounds of powdered sugar and six pints of water.
3. Put it on the fire.
4. When boiling, skim it.
5. Add the fruit and boil it till reduced to a pulp.
6. Take it off and pass it through a clean cloth into another stewpan.
7. Add a piece of alum the size of half a pea.
8. Set it again on the fire and let it boil gently for a minute or two.
9. Skim and pass it again through a close muslin into the pan.
10. Add a few drops of prepared cochineal.
11. Put it again on the fire and let it boil until it becomes a thick syrup.
12. Take it off and fill the preserve jars or glass bowls.
13. When cold, cover them and keep them in a cool place.

title: Aywa Tatlusi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six quinces
- syrup
- prepared cochineal
- cloves

## Directions

1. Peel five or six quinces
2. Remove the cores with a scoop
3. Ornament the exterior with a sharp instrument
4. Put them in cold water as you finish them
5. Scald them when all is ready
6. Take them out and arrange them in another stewpan
7. Pour over a pint of syrup or a little more
8. Put the cores with the pips in a small saucepan with part of the water in which the quinces are boiled
9. Put it on the fire until reduced to a pulp
10. Pass it through a sieve and add it to the fruit with a few drops of prepared cochineal and a few cloves
11. Put the pan on the fire and let it simmer gently until the fruit is tender
12. Take it off and when cold, dish it up with the syrup and serve

title: Aywa Tdtlussi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- Five or six quinces
- A pint of syrup
- Prepared cochineal
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Peel five or six quinces
2. Remove the cores with a scoop
3. Ornament the exterior with a sharp instrument
4. As you finish them put them in cold water
5. When all is ready, scald them
6. Take them out and arrange them in another stewpan
7. Pour over a pint of syrup
8. Put the cores with the pips in a small saucepan with part of the water in which the quinces are boiled
9. Put it on the fire until reduced to a pulp
10. Pass it through a sieve and add it to the fruit with a few drops of prepared cochineal and a few cloves
11. Put the pan on the fire and let it simmer gently until the fruit is tender
12. Take it off
13. When cold, dish it up with the syrup and serve

title: Badem Ghardbiyyessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds and three-quarters of skinned almonds
- two pounds and three-quarters of powdered sugar
- whites of four eggs
- one ounce of powdered gum-mastic
- powdered cinnamon
- musk (optional)
- rose-water (optional)
- lemon-peel (optional)
- cochineal (optional)

## Directions

1. Put two pounds and three-quarters of skinned almonds in a stone mortar with the same quantity of powdered sugar
2. Pound them together until very fine
3. Add the whites of four eggs, an ounce of powdered gum-mastic, and a little powdered cinnamon
4. Pound them for a few minutes longer
5. When like a paste, make it into small cakes the size of macaroons, or any size and shape you please
6. Lay on the bottom of a baking-tin some paper
7. Arrange the cakes over
8. Cover them with paper
9. Turn up the edges of the papers that are on the bottom of the tin and fix them to the top ones, so as to prevent the cakes from burning
10. Bake for about an hour in a rather hot oven (Sometimes a little musk mixed with rose-water is used instead of the white of egg, and sometimes a little lemon-peel and a little cochineal is added)

title: Badem Ghardbiyyessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds and three-quarters of skinned almonds
- two pounds and three-quarters of powdered sugar
- whites of four eggs
- an ounce of powdered gum-mastic
- powdered cinnamon
- musk mixed with rose-water (sometimes used instead of egg white)
- lemon-peel (sometimes added)
- cochineal (sometimes added)

## Directions

1. Put two pounds and three-quarters of skinned almonds in a stone mortar, with the same quantity of powdered sugar
2. Pound them together until very fine
3. Add the whites of four eggs, an ounce of powdered gum-mastic, and a little powdered cinnamon
4. Pound them for a few minutes longer
5. When like a paste, make it into small cakes the size of macaroons, or any size and shape you please
6. Lay on the bottom of a baking-tin some paper and arrange the cakes over
7. Cover them with paper and turn up the edges of the papers that are on the bottom of the tin and fix them to the top ones, so as to prevent the cakes from burning
8. Bake for about an hour in a rather hot oven

title: Badem Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of sweet almonds
- three drachms of bitter almonds
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- another three-quarters of a pint of water
- two pounds and three-quarters of powdered loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Scald three-quarters of a pound of sweet almonds, and three drachms of bitter almonds
2. Skin and pound them in a stone mortar until in pulp
3. Mix with three-quarters of a pint of water, and pass it through a close muslin into a basin
4. Put the residue in the mortar and pound it again, which mix with another three-quarters of a pint of water, pass it again through the muslin into the basin
5. Put in a stewpan two pounds and three-quarters of powdered loaf sugar, add the liquor
6. Stir well with a wooden spoon, set it on a charcoal fire, and let it boil for one minute
7. Take it off, and pass it through the muslin again, bottle, and cork it up till wanted

title: Badem Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of sweet almonds
- three drachms of bitter almonds
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- two pounds and three-quarters of powdered loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Scald three-quarters of a pound of sweet almonds, and three drachms of bitter almonds
2. Skin and pound them in a stone mortar until in pulp
3. Mix with three-quarters of a pint of water, and pass it through a close muslin into a basin
4. Put the residue in the mortar and pound it again
5. Mix with another three-quarters of a pint of water, pass it again through the muslin into the basin
6. Put in a stewpan two pounds and three-quarters of powdered loaf sugar
7. Add the liquor, stir well with a wooden spoon
8. Set it on a charcoal fire, and let it boil for one minute, then take it off
9. Pass it through the muslin again, bottle, and cork it up till wanted

title: Badinjan Dolmasi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven egg-plants
- one or two pounds of raw mutton
- a handful of well-washed rice
- two onions
- salt
- pepper
- five or six eggs
- fresh butter
- rib-bones of mutton
- broth or water
- juice of one or two lemons

## Directions

1. Procure six or seven egg-plants and remove the stalks
2. Cut off the tops as large as a two-shilling piece
3. Take out the insides without making a hole at the other end and throw them in salt water for ten or fifteen minutes
4. Mince fine one or two pounds of raw mutton
5. Add a handful of well-washed rice, two onions chopped very fine, sufficient salt and pepper and mix them together with your hands
6. Stuff the egg-plants with these ingredients and cover them with the tops contrariwise
7. Beat up five or six eggs in a basin and dip the egg-plants in it
8. Fry them in hot fresh butter
9. Prick them here and there with a fork
10. Place them in a stewpan the bottom of which you have previously covered with rib-bones of mutton
11. Cover them either with broth or water and add the juice of one or two lemons
12. Set it on a charcoal fire and let it simmer till the liquor is nearly absorbed or until it forms a nice thick gravy at the bottom
13. Dish them up carefully and serve hot
14. If you fancy, beat up two or three eggs with the gravy and the juice of half a lemon in a saucepan
15. Place it on a very slow fire and keep stirring
16. When it begins to thicken, pour over the egg-plants and then serve

title: Badinjan Dolmássi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven egg-plants
- one or two pounds of raw mutton
- a handful of well-washed rice
- two onions
- salt
- pepper
- five or six eggs
- fresh butter
- rib-bones of mutton
- broth or water
- juice of one or two lemons

## Directions

1. Procure six or seven egg-plants and remove the stalks
2. Cut off the tops as large as a two-shilling piece
3. Take out the insides without making a hole at the other end and throw them in salt water for ten or fifteen minutes
4. Mince fine one or two pounds of raw mutton
5. Add a handful of well-washed rice, two onions chopped very fine, sufficient salt and pepper, and mix them together with your hands
6. Stuff the egg-plants with these ingredients and cover them with the tops contrariwise
7. Beat up five or six eggs in a basin and dip the egg-plants in them
8. Fry them in hot fresh butter
9. Prick them here and there with a fork and place them in a stewpan, the bottom of which you have previously covered with rib-bones of mutton
10. Cover them either with broth or water and add the juice of one or two lemons
11. Set it on a charcoal fire and let it simmer till the liquor is nearly absorbed or until it forms a nice thick gravy at the bottom
12. Dish them up carefully and serve hot, or beat up two or three eggs with the gravy and the juice of half a lemon in a saucepan, place it on a very slow fire and keep stirring, and when it begins to thicken, pour over the egg-plants and serve

title: Badinjan Mujmeri
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven egg plants
- water
- salt
- three or four onions
- fresh butter
- four or five tablespoonfuls of fried minced meat
- raw mutton
- parsley
- pepper
- two or three eggs

## Directions

1. Procure six or seven egg plants, cut them in two lengthways, take out the insides, and boil them a little in water, moderately salt
2. take them out, and put them in a basin, add three or four finely chopped onions, previously fried a nice brown in fresh butter, four or five tablespoonfuls of fried minced meat (prepared of raw mutton), a handful of parsley, chopped fine, sufficient salt and pepper, and two or three eggs well beaten up, and mix them well together
3. butter a baking-pan, in which arrange half of the egg-plants, and stuff them with the above mixture
4. cover them with the remaining half, and bake them in a rather hot oven
5. When the egg-plants are shrunk and soft, they are cooked
6. Dish up, and serve

title: Badinjan Pachassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- egg-plants
- salt water
- flour
- fresh butter
- three or four cloves of garlic
- salt
- a small teacup of wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut as many egg-plants as are wanted in slices a quarter of an inch thick, and put them in salt water for about ten minutes
2. Dip them in flour, fry them a nice brown in fresh butter, and arrange them in a baking-dish
3. Well pound three or four cloves of garlic in a mortar, add a little salt, and a small teacup of wine vinegar; stir it well with a wooden spoon, and pour it over the egg-plants
4. Place the dish on a moderate charcoal fire until the liquor is absorbed, then take it off, and serve

title: Badinjan Pachassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- egg-plants
- salt water
- flour
- fresh butter
- three or four cloves of garlic
- a little salt
- a small teacup of wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut as many egg-plants as are wanted in slices a quarter of an inch thick, and put them in salt water for about ten minutes
2. Dip them in flour, fry them a nice brown in fresh butter, and arrange them in a baking-dish
3. Well pound three or four cloves of garlic in a mortar, add a little salt, and a small teacup of wine vinegar
4. Stir it well with a wooden spoon, and pour it over the egg-plants
5. Place the dish on a moderate charcoal fire until the liquor is absorbed, then take it off, and serve

title: Badinjdn Mujmeri
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven egg-plants
- water
- salt
- three or four onions
- fresh butter
- four or five tablespoonfuls of fried minced meat of raw mutton
- parsley chopped fine
- pepper
- two or three eggs

## Directions

1. Procure six or seven egg-plants, cut them in two lengthways, take out the insides
2. Boil them a little in water, moderately salt
3. Take them out, and put them in a basin
4. Add three or four finely chopped onions, previously fried a nice brown in fresh butter, four or five tablespoonfuls of fried minced meat (prepared of raw mutton), a handful of parsley chopped fine, sufficient salt and pepper, and two or three eggs well beaten up
5. Mix them well together
6. Butter a baking-pan, in which arrange half of the egg-plants, and stuff them with the above mixture
7. Cover them with the remaining half, and bake them in a rather hot oven
8. When the egg-plants are shrunk and soft, they are cooked
9. Dish up, and serve

title: Balik Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six pounds of salmon or swordfish
- salt
- olive-oil
- three pints of wine vinegar
- half a teacup of saffron-water
- one or two tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- eight or ten cloves of garlic
- bay-leaves
- myrtle-leaves
- mixed spice
- washed peppercorns
- skinned pistachios

## Directions

1. Cut fish in slices three-quarters of an inch thick
2. Wash and dry them well in a cloth
3. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt
4. Fry them a nice brown in olive-oil
5. Pour three pints of wine vinegar in a stewpan with half a teacup of saffron-water and one or two tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
6. Stir it round and partly boil it
7. Peel eight or ten cloves of garlic and cut them in pieces the size of pistachios
8. Lay some bay-leaves and myrtle-leaves on the bottom of a stone jar
9. Place some of the slices of fish over
10. Sprinkle some mixed spice over them with some pieces of garlic, washed peppercorns, and skinned pistachios
11. Lay bay-leaves and myrtle-leaves, then fish, then spice, garlic, pistachios, and peppercorns until the jar is full with bay-leaves and myrtle-leaves on the top
12. Pour the boiled vinegar until the fish is covered
13. Place a piece of clean board or a plate over with an iron weight on top to press it
14. Cover the jar and let it remain for three days before using

title: Balık Dolmesi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four middle-sized mackerel, or whitings, or any other fish
- half a teacup of olive oil
- three or four finely-chopped onions
- two or three ounces of pistachios, skinned
- one or two tablespoonfuls of currants
- a little pepper and salt
- a little mixed spice
- flour
- egg
- hot olive oil

## Directions

1. Get three or four middle-sized mackerel, or whitings, or any other fish you may fancy, well clean them, and take out the gills
2. Break the bones of the tails, and skin them, without tearing the skin, in one piece, and the head must be attached to the skin
3. Remove the flesh from the bones, and chop it up
4. Put half a teacup of olive oil in a frying pan, place it on the fire
5. When hot, put in three or four finely-chopped onions, and stir them with a wooden spoon until nicely browned
6. Add the fish, with two or three ounces of pistachios, skinned, one or two tablespoonfuls of currants, a little pepper and salt, and a little mixed spice, and keep stirring until the fish is nearly done
7. Stuff the fish skins with it, dip them in flour, and then in egg, and fry them in hot olive oil till a nice colour, turning them over
8. When done, take them out with a slice carefully, let them drain
9. Dish up on a white napkin, and serve

title: Bamiya-Basstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pounds of mutton
- salt
- water
- five or six sliced onions
- fresh butter
- one or two hundred okras
- juice of one or two lemons
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut three or four pounds of mutton into pieces about the size of walnuts, wash and place them in a saucepan, with sufficient salt
2. Cover them with water, and set the pan on the fire
3. When boiling, skim and simmer till the meat is half done
4. Fry partly brown five or six sliced onions in fresh butter
5. Peel the stalky parts, and cut off the ends of one or two hundred okras
6. Place the pieces of meat and the okras, together with the onions in layers
7. Add the liquor in which the meat was boiled; if not enough, add some of broth and a little salt
8. Put the cover over the pan, and set it on a charcoal fire until the meat and the okras are tender, and a nice gravy is formed at the bottom
9. Just five minutes before removing it from the fire, add the juice of one or two lemons
10. When done, dish up, sprinkle a little pepper and cinnamon over, and serve

title: Bamiya-Bdsstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pounds of mutton
- salt
- water
- five or six sliced onions
- fresh butter
- one or two hundred okras
- broth
- juice of one or two lemons
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut three or four pounds of mutton into pieces about the size of walnuts
2. Wash and place them in a saucepan with sufficient salt
3. Cover them with water, and set the pan on the fire
4. When boiling, skim and simmer till the meat is half done
5. Fry partly brown five or six sliced onions in fresh butter
6. Peel the stalky parts, and cut off the ends of one or two hundred okras
7. Place the pieces of meat and the okras, together with the onions in layers
8. Add the liquor in which the meat was boiled; if not enough, add some of broth and a little salt
9. Put the cover over the pan, and set it on a charcoal fire until the meat and the okras are tender, and a nice gravy is formed at the bottom
10. Just five minutes before removing it from the fire, add the juice of one or two lemons
11. When done, dish up, sprinkle a little pepper and cinnamon over, and serve

title: Band, Etindhi Papas Yahnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six pounds of loin of veal
- three or four onions
- five or six heads of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- cummin
- a teacup of wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut five or six pounds of a loin of veal in pieces the size of eggs
2. Place them in rather deep earthen stewpan, with three or four sliced onions, five or six heads of garlic, salt and pepper, a little cummin, and a teacup of wine vinegar, without water
3. Put the cover over the pan air tight, or paste it round with paper, so that no steam shall escape
4. Put it in an oven, or simmer it on a moderate fire for about three or four hours
5. When done, dish it up tastefully, and serve
6. Be particular in pasting round the cover, and the fire must not be fierce, or the meat may burn

title: Band, Etindhi Papas Yáhnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six pounds of loin of veal
- onions
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- cummin
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut five or six pounds of a loin of veal in pieces the size of eggs
2. Place them in rather deep earthen stewpan, with three or four sliced onions, five or six heads of garlic, salt and pepper, a little cummin, and a teacup of wine vinegar, without water
3. Put the cover over the pan air tight, or paste it round with paper, so that no steam shall escape
4. Put it in an oven, or simmer it on a moderate fire for about three or four hours
5. When done, dish it up tastefully, and serve

title: Bash Pilawi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three lambs' or sheeps' heads
- water
- sufficient salt
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- a little pepper
- ground cinnamon
- half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic
- a pound and a half of rice, well washed

## Directions

1. Get two or three lambs' or sheeps' heads, wash well, and place them in a stewpan nearly full of water, add sufficient salt, and put the pan on the fire.
2. When boiling, skim and simmer until the meat comes off the heads.
3. Cut them open, take out the brains, put them in a basin, and beat them up with a little broth of the heads to the thickness of a thick cream.
4. Pass the broth through a sieve, measure three pints of it into a saucepan, add a quarter of a pound of fresh butter if it is not rich, a little pepper, ground cinnamon, and half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic, and set the pan on the fire.
5. As soon as boiling commences, add a pound and a half of rice well washed, and let it simmer until the liquor is absorbed.
6. Take it off, and place it on the hob for ten minutes or so, then dish it up in the shape of a dome, spread the brain all over, then brown it nicely in the oven, and serve.

title: Bash Pilbwi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three lambs' or sheeps' heads
- water
- sufficient salt
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- pepper
- ground cinnamon
- half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic
- a pound and a half of rice, well washed

## Directions

1. Get two or three lambs' or sheeps' heads, wash well, and place them in a stewpan nearly full of water, add sufficient salt, and put the pan on the fire.
2. When boiling, skim and simmer until the meat comes off the heads.
3. Cut them open, take out the brains, put them in a basin, and beat them up with a little broth of the heads to the thickness of a thick cream.
4. Pass the broth through a sieve, measure three pints of it into a saucepan.
5. Add a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, a little pepper, ground cinnamon, and half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic, and set the pan on the fire.
6. As soon as boiling commences, add a pound and a half of rice well washed, and let it simmer until the liquor is absorbed.
7. Take it off, and place it on the hob for ten minutes or so.
8. Dish it up in the shape of a dome, spread the brain all over, then brown it nicely in the oven, and serve.

title: Bdlik Dolmetssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four middle-sized mackerel, whitings, or other fish
- half a teacup of olive oil
- three or four finely-chopped onions
- two or three ounces of pistachios, skinned
- one or two tablespoonfuls of currants
- pepper
- salt
- mixed spice
- flour
- egg
- hot olive oil

## Directions

1. Get three or four middle-sized mackerel, or whitings, or any other fish you may fancy, well clean them
2. Take out the gills
3. Break the bones of the tails, and skin them, without tearing the skin, in one piece, and the head must be attached to the skin
4. Remove the flesh from the bones, and chop it up
5. Put half a teacup of olive oil in a frying pan, place it on the fire
6. When hot, put in three or four finely-chopped onions, and stir them with a wooden spoon until nicely browned
7. Add the fish, with two or three ounces of pistachios, skinned, one or two tablespoonfuls of currants, a little pepper and salt, and a little mixed spice
8. Keep stirring until the fish is nearly done
9. Stuff the fish skins with it
10. Dip them in flour, and then in egg, and fry them in hot olive oil till a nice colour, turning them over
11. When done, take them out with a slice carefully, let them drain
12. Dish up on a white napkin, and serve

title: Bdndinjan Yaldnji Dolmdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven egg-plants
- salt
- a teacupful of olive oil
- half a pound of onions
- a pound of well-washed rice
- pepper
- a tablespoonful of finely-chopped mint
- a teacup of water

## Directions

1. Prepare six or seven egg-plants as No. 107
2. Sprinkle some salt over the insides of the egg-plants
3. After a few minutes squeeze them well with your hands so as to be free from bitter water
4. Chop them up
5. Put a teacupful of olive oil in a saucepan and place it on the fire
6. When hot, put in half a pound of onions chopped fine and stir till they begin to brown
7. Add the insides of the egg-plants and stir for three minutes or so
8. Add a pound of well-washed rice, sufficient salt and pepper, a tablespoonful of finely-chopped mint, and a teacup of water
9. Keep stirring until the moisture is absorbed
10. Stuff the egg-plants moderately with it and put the lids over
11. Prick them here and there with a fork
12. Place them in a saucepan and nearly cover them with water
13. Set it on the fire with the cover over and let it boil gently until the liquor is absorbed
14. Dish up and serve hot or cold

title: Beidingan Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- egg-plants
- parsley
- celery
- one or two dozen cloves of garlic
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Procure as many egg-plants as are required and take the stalks off
2. Split each one in three or four lengthways without separating from the bottom
3. Scald them
4. Place them in a wicker-basket one over the other
5. Put a piece of board or a plate over them with an iron weight on top
6. Let them remain for eight or ten hours to draw the bitter water from them
7. Chop up some parsley and celery
8. Mix with one or two dozen cloves of garlic, each cut in two
9. Stuff between each slice of the egg-plants with the ingredients
10. Tie up each of the egg-plants with the stalks of celery to prevent the stuffing from falling out
11. Arrange them in a stone jar one over the other
12. Cover them with wine vinegar
13. Place a piece of clean board over and an iron weight on the top to press it
14. Let it remain for about three weeks before using

title: Beingan Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- egg-plants
- parsley
- celery
- one or two dozen cloves of garlic
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Procure as many egg-plants as are required
2. Take the stalks off
3. Split each one in three or four lengthways, without separating from the bottom
4. Scald them
5. Place them in a wicker-basket one over the other
6. Put a piece of board or a plate over them
7. Place an iron weight over
8. Let them remain for eight or ten hours, so as to draw the bitter water from them
9. Chop up some parsley and celery
10. Mix with one or two dozen of cloves of garlic, each cut in two
11. Stuff between each slice of the egg-plants with the ingredients
12. Tie up each of the egg-plants with the stalks of celery, to prevent the stuffing from falling out
13. Arrange them in a stone jar one over the other
14. Cover them with wine vinegar
15. Place a piece of clean board over
16. Put an iron weight on the top to press it
17. Let it remain for about three weeks

title: Belamiinjanli Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four egg-plants
- salt water
- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- one or two chopped onions
- two pints of hot or cold water
- one pint of broth
- a little salt and pepper
- a few pieces of gum-mastic
- a small quantity of saffron
- a pound and a half of rice, well cleaned

## Directions

1. Procure three or four egg-plants, cut them in four crossways, take out the insides if seeded, and cut them in slices about an inch thick.
2. Scald them in salt water, and drain them.
3. Put three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter in a saucepan with one or two chopped onions; put it on the fire, and let the onions stew.
4. When nearly brown, add the pieces of egg-plants, and fry them partly together with the onions.
5. Add two pints of hot or cold water, and one of broth, a little salt and pepper, a few pieces of gum-mastic, and a small quantity of saffron.
6. As soon as it boils, add a pound and a half of rice, well cleaned, and let it boil slowly until the liquor is absorbed.
7. Take it off, and place it on the hob for ten minutes or so.
8. Dish it up, and serve.

title: Beldmjanli Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four egg-plants
- salt water
- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- one or two chopped onions
- two pints of hot or cold water
- one pint of broth
- salt and pepper
- a few pieces of gum-mastic
- a small quantity of saffron
- a pound and a half of rice, well cleaned

## Directions

1. Procure three or four egg-plants, cut them in four crossways, take out the insides if seeded, and cut them in slices about an inch thick.
2. Scald them in salt water, and drain them.
3. Put three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter in a saucepan with one or two chopped onions and put it on the fire.
4. Let the onions stew, and when nearly brown, add the pieces of egg-plants, and fry them partly together with the onions.
5. Add two pints of hot or cold water, and one of broth, a little salt and pepper, a few pieces of gum-mastic, and a small quantity of saffron.
6. As soon as it boils, add a pound and a half of rice, well cleaned, and let it boil slowly until the liquor is absorbed.
7. Take it off, and place it on the hob for ten minutes or so.
8. Dish it up, and serve.

title: Beshidiye Helwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fresh butter
- half a pound of wheat-starch
- water
- a pound of white sugar
- rose-water
- powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put on the fire a stewpan with half a pound of fresh butter
2. When it is melted, take it off
3. Add half a pound of wheat-starch which you have previously dissolved in a pint of water
4. Add a pound of white sugar previously melted in a little water
5. Set it on the fire and keep stirring it
6. When it becomes crummy, stir it a few minutes longer
7. Take it off
8. Dish up
9. Sprinkle a little rose-water over with a little powdered cinnamon and serve

title: Beshidiye Helwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fresh butter
- half a pound of wheat-starch
- a pint of water
- a pound of white sugar
- water
- rose-water
- powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put on the fire a stewpan with half a pound of fresh butter
2. When it is melted, take it off
3. Add half a pound of wheat-starch, which you have previously dissolved in a pint of water
4. Add a pound of white sugar, previously melted in a little water
5. Set it on the fire and keep stirring it
6. When it becomes crummy, stir it a few minutes longer
7. Take it off, dish up
8. Sprinkle a little rose-water over with a little powdered cinnamon and serve

title: Betlik Chorbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of very fresh grey mullet
- salt
- six ounces of the best olive oil
- one pound and four ounces of finely-chopped onions
- handful of washed parsley
- handful of mint
- one pint of the best wine vinegar
- two or three pints of hot or cold water
- yolks of three or four eggs
- juice of one or two lemons
- half a teacup of water
- about two drachms of saffron
- cinnamon
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Procure three pounds of very fresh grey mullet, clean it well, and sprinkle a little salt over it
2. Put six ounces of the best olive oil in a stewpan, with one pound and four ounces of finely-chopped onions, and sufficient salt
3. Put the pan on the fire till the onions are partly browned
4. Add a handful of washed parsley, and the same of mint, both chopped fine, and fry them a little together with the onions
5. Add one pint of the best wine vinegar, and two or three pints of hot or cold water, and boil it for twenty or thirty minutes
6. Pass the liquor through a cloth or sieve into another stewpan, and place the fish in it
7. Put it on the fire and let it simmer till the fish is done
8. Take it out and extract all the bones, cut the fish in pieces and put them into the tureen
9. Pass the liquor again through a cloth into a stewpan
10. Put in a basin the yolks of three or four eggs, the juice of one or two lemons, and half a teacup of water previously coloured with about two drachms of saffron
11. Beat up all together, and add it gradually to the liquor
12. Set it on a moderate fire for ten or fifteen minutes, stirring it all the time with a wooden spoon, and without permitting it to boil
13. Pour it over the fish, sprinkle a little cinnamon and a pinch of cayenne pepper over, and serve

title: Beyaz Yalmi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton from leg or other part
- salt
- pepper
- water
- half a pint of chick-peas
- two or three onions
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of a leg or any other part of mutton in pieces the size of walnuts or eggs
2. Wash and place them in a stewpan with a little salt and pepper
3. Cover them with water
4. Put it on a charcoal fire, and let it boil for about twenty minutes
5. Skim it well
6. Add half a pint of chick-peas previously scalded in water, and two or three onions sliced
7. Let it simmer until it forms a nice thick gravy at the bottom of the pan and the meat and peas are tender
8. Dish it up with two or three slices of toasted bread on the bottom of the dish
9. Serve whilst hot

title: Beyaz Ydhmi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg or any other part of mutton
- salt
- pepper
- water
- chick-peas
- onions
- toasted bread

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of a leg or any other part of mutton in pieces the size of walnuts or eggs
2. Wash and place them in a stewpan with a little salt and pepper
3. Cover them with water and put it on a charcoal fire
4. Let it boil for about twenty minutes and skim it well
5. Add half a pint of chick-peas previously scalded in water, and two or three onions sliced
6. Let it simmer until it forms a nice thick gravy at the bottom of the pan and the meat and peas are tender
7. Dish it up with two or three slices of toasted bread on the bottom of the dish
8. Serve whilst hot

title: Bhadinjan Khyghanassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- egg-plant
- six eggs
- tablespoonful of currants
- two of skinned pistachios
- pepper
- powdered cinnamon
- four or five ounces of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Peel an egg-plant, take out the inside, if seeded, and grate it or chop it up fine
2. Scald it in moderately-salt water, strain and squeeze from it all the water in a cloth
3. Break in a basin six eggs, beat them well up
4. Add the egg-plants, with a tablespoonful of currants, two of skinned pistachios, a little pepper and powdered cinnamon, mix well all together
5. Put in the frying-pan four or five ounces of fresh butter, put it on the fire till hot
6. Pour in the mixture and fry it a nice brown on both sides
7. When done, turn it over carefully on to a dish, and serve

title: Bhdinjan Khyghanassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- an egg-plant
- six eggs
- a tablespoonful of currants
- two tablespoonfuls of skinned pistachios
- pepper
- powdered cinnamon
- four or five ounces of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Peel an egg-plant, take out the inside, if seeded, and grate it or chop it up fine
2. Scald it in moderately-salt water, strain and squeeze from it all the water in a cloth
3. Break in a basin six eggs, beat them well up, and add the egg-plants, with a tablespoonful of currants, two of skinned pistachios, a little pepper and powdered cinnamon, mix well all together
4. Put in the frying-pan four or five ounces of fresh butter, put it on the fire till hot, then pour in the mixture and fry it a nice brown on both sides
5. When done, turn it over carefully onto a dish, and serve

title: Bhlik Pilayi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a teacup and a half of nice olive-oil
- two pints of water
- a little salt
- a pound of well-washed rice
- any fish
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put a teacup and a half of nice olive-oil in a stewpan, and set it on the fire
2. As soon as it begins to bubble, pour into it two pints of water, with a little salt
3. When boiling, add a pound of well-washed rice
4. Lay over any fish you choose, previously well cleaned and seasoned with salt
5. Put the cover over the pan, and let it simmer until the liquor is absorbed
6. Take it off, and place it on the hob for fifteen minutes or so
7. Dish up the rice, and place the fish over tastefully, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve hot

title: Bhlik Piláwi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a teacup and a half of nice olive-oil
- two pints of water
- salt
- a pound of well-washed rice
- fish
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put a teacup and a half of nice olive-oil in a stewpan, and set it on the fire
2. As soon as it begins to bubble, pour into it two pints of water, with a little salt
3. When boiling, add a pound of well-washed rice
4. Lay over any fish you choose, previously well cleaned and seasoned with salt
5. Put the cover over the pan, and let it simmer until the liquor is absorbed
6. Take it off, and place it on the hob for fifteen minutes or so
7. Dish up the rice, and place the fish over tastefully, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve hot

title: Bibar Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven dozen young capsicums
- parsley
- mint
- strong wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Procure six or seven dozen young capsicums
2. Cut the stalks off and scald them
3. Place them in a wicker basket
4. Place a piece of clean board or a plate over them
5. Put an iron weight on the top
6. Let it remain for one night so the bitter water may drain from them
7. Place them in a stone jar with some parsley and mint between them
8. Cover them with strong wine vinegar
9. Cover the jar and let it remain in the pantry for ten or fifteen days before using
10. If you wish them to be kept for any length of time, change the vinegar occasionally

title: Bidar Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven dozen young capsicums
- parsley
- mint
- strong wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Procure six or seven dozen young capsicums
2. Cut the stalks off
3. Scald them
4. Place them in a wicker basket
5. Place a piece of clean board or a plate over them
6. Put an iron weight on the top
7. Let it remain for one night, so as the bitter water may drain from them
8. Place them in a stone jar
9. Put some parsley and mint between them
10. Cover them with strong wine vinegar
11. Cover the jar
12. Let it remain in the pantry for ten or fifteen days before using
13. If you wish them to be kept for any length of time, change the vinegar occasionally

title: Birydn, or Orman Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- whole sheep or lamb
- onion-juice
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Get a whole sheep or a lamb, already cleaned, rub it all over with onion-juice, sufficient salt and pepper
2. Pass it on a pole sufficiently long, and wrap the sheep up in sheets of paper
3. Make a large wood or charcoal fire, thrust in the ground at each end of the fire, half a yard distant, a stake, with fangs sufficiently large and strong for the pole to rest on with the sheep
4. Place the pole on them, and keep turning it gently
5. Put under the sheep a dripping-pan to catch the gravy, and baste the sheep with a bunch of feathers, or a piece of clean linen tied at the top of a stick
6. When half done, remove the papers and turn it quickly
7. When nicely browned all over, take out the pole, dish it up, sprinkle some cinnamon over, and serve

title: Birydn, or Orman Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a whole sheep or a lamb, cleaned
- onion-juice
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Rub the sheep or lamb all over with onion-juice, sufficient salt and pepper
2. Pass it on a pole sufficiently long
3. Wrap the sheep up in sheets of paper
4. Make a large wood or charcoal fire
5. Thrust in the ground at each end of the fire, half a yard distant, a stake with fangs sufficiently large and strong for the pole to rest on
6. Place the pole on them and keep turning it gently
7. Put under the sheep a dripping-pan to catch the gravy
8. Baste the sheep with a bunch of feathers or a piece of clean linen tied at the top of a stick
9. When half done, remove the papers and turn it quickly
10. When nicely browned all over, take out the pole
11. Sprinkle some cinnamon over and serve

title: Botlik Yimurtassi Tereturu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- botargo (fish-eggs)
- olive oil
- lemon-juice
- pistachios or almonds, skinned

## Directions

1. Procure a pound of botargo
2. Pound it well in a stone mortar
3. Add a teaspoonful of the best olive oil and the same of lemon-juice, and pound it again
4. Add another teaspoonful of oil and lemon-juice, and beat it up well
5. Continue in this way until you bring it to the consistency of a very thick cream
6. Pour it in a dish or plate
7. Ornament the top with some skinned pistachios or almonds, and serve

title: Botlik Yumurtası Terethuru
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- botargo (fish-eggs)
- olive oil
- lemon juice
- pistachios or almonds

## Directions

1. Procure a pound of botargo and pound it well in a stone mortar
2. Add a teaspoonful of the best olive oil and the same of lemon-juice and pound it again
3. Add another teaspoonful of oil and lemon-juice and beat it up well
4. Continue in this way until you bring it to the consistency of a very thick cream
5. Pour it in a dish or plate and ornament the top with some skinned pistachios or almonds
6. Serve

title: Bâdinjan-Tâvâssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- egg-plants
- salt
- flour
- water
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Procure as many egg-plants as are wanted, cut them in slices about half an inch thick
2. Sprinkle them with salt, and let them remain about an hour
3. When the bitter juice is drawn from them, put two or three ounces of flour into a basin, which mix with water to form a smooth batter, not too thick
4. Dip the egg-plants in it, and fry them in fresh butter till a nice colour, turning them over
5. When done, take them out with a slice, let them drain, dish up, and serve

title: Bändinjan Yaldnji Dolmessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven egg-plants
- salt
- a teacupful of olive oil
- half a pound of onions
- a pound of well-washed rice
- pepper
- a tablespoonful of finely-chopped mint
- a teacup of water

## Directions

1. Prepare six or seven egg-plants
2. Sprinkle some salt over the insides of the egg-plants
3. After a few minutes squeeze them well with your hands, so as to be free from bitter water, and chop them up
4. Put a teacupful of olive oil in a saucepan, place it on the fire
5. When hot, put in half a pound of onions chopped fine, and stir till they begin to brown
6. Add the insides of the egg-plants, and stir for three minutes or so
7. Add a pound of well-washed rice, sufficient salt and pepper, a tablespoonful of finely-chopped mint, and a teacup of water
8. Keep stirring until the moisture is absorbed
9. Stuff the egg-plants moderately with it, put the lids over
10. Prick them here and there with a fork
11. Place them in a saucepan, and nearly cover them with water
12. Set it on the fire with the cover over, and let it boil gently until the liquor is absorbed
13. Dish up, and serve hot or cold

title: Bādinğān-Tāvāssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- egg-plants
- salt
- two or three ounces of flour
- water
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Procure as many egg-plants as are wanted, cut them in slices about half an inch thick, sprinkle them with salt, and let them remain about an hour.
2. When the bitter juice is drawn from them, put two or three ounces of flour into a basin, which mix with water to form a smooth batter, not too thick.
3. Dip the egg-plants in it, and fry them in fresh butter till a nice colour, turning them over.
4. When done, take them out with a slice, let them drain, dish up, and serve.

title: Chaghli Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- unripe fruit such as peaches, nectarines, almonds, apricots, cherries, apples, plums, pears
- very strong wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Gather any unripe fruit that may have fallen from the trees, such as peaches, nectarines, almonds, apricots, cherries, apples, plums, pears
2. Mix all together
3. Place them in bottles or in a stone jar
4. Cover them with very strong wine vinegar
5. Tie up the mouths of the bottles with a piece of leather
6. Let them remain for thirty or forty days before using

title: Chaghlι Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- unripe fruit such as peaches, nectarines, almonds, apricots, cherries, apples, plums, pears
- very strong wine vinegar
- leather

## Directions

1. Gather any unripe fruit that may have fallen from the trees, such as peaches, nectarines, almonds, apricots, cherries, apples, plums, pears
2. Mix all together
3. Place them in bottles or in a stone jar
4. Cover them with very strong wine vinegar
5. Tie up the mouths of the bottles with a piece of leather
6. Let them remain for thirty or forty days before using

title: Chekirdeksiz Uzum Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of sultana raisins
- a pint of water
- sufficient loaf sugar
- a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or rose
- a few small pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Get a pound of sultana raisins and take the stalks off
2. Well wash them and put them in a stewpan with a pint of water
3. Set it to boil until they are swollen up
4. Take it off and pass the liquor through a sieve into a saucepan
5. Add sufficient loaf sugar and boil until it becomes thickish
6. Remove it and let it cool
7. Pour it in a glass bowl and moderate the sweetness with cold water
8. Put the boiled raisins in with a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or rose and a few small pieces of ice
9. Serve

title: Chekirdeksiz Uzum Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of sultana raisins
- a pint of water
- loaf sugar
- a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or rose
- a few small pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Get a pound of sultana raisins, take the stalks off and well wash them
2. Put them in a stewpan with a pint of water, and set it to boil until they are swollen up
3. Take it off, and pass the liquor through a sieve into a saucepan
4. Add sufficient loaf sugar, and boil until it becomes thickish, then remove it
5. When cold, pour it in a glass bowl, and moderate the sweetness with cold water
6. Put the boiled raisins in, with a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or rose, and a few small pieces of ice, and serve

title: Chilek Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- about three pounds of crushed sugar
- two pints of water
- white of an egg
- a pound and a quarter of fresh gathered strawberries

## Directions

1. Put about three pounds of crushed sugar in a stewpan with two pints of water, and the white of an egg well beaten up
2. Set it on the fire
3. When boiling, skim and simmer until it begins to thicken
4. Remove it, and immediately add a pound and a quarter of fresh gathered strawberries, well-cleaned and the stalks taken off
5. Put the cover over the pan and let it remain for two hours
6. Take out the strawberries with a strainer, and pass the syrup through a clean cloth
7. Bottle and cork it up until required

title: Chilek Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of crushed sugar
- two pints of water
- white of an egg
- one pound and a quarter of fresh gathered strawberries

## Directions

1. Put about three pounds of crushed sugar in a stewpan with two pints of water, and the white of an egg well beaten up
2. Set it on the fire
3. When boiling, skim and simmer until it begins to thicken
4. Remove it, and immediately add a pound and a quarter of fresh gathered strawberries, well-cleaned and the stalks taken off
5. Put the cover over the pan and let it remain for two hours
6. Take out the strawberries with a strainer, and pass the syrup through a clean cloth
7. Bottle and cork it up until required

title: Churek Kadciyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- about half a pound of scalded fresh butter
- hot syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare as much vermicelli as is required, as No. 149
2. Twist it like a rope, twice the thickness of your thumb, and make it into cakes the size of a Bath bun
3. Lay them in a buttered baking-tin, pour in about half a pound of scalded fresh butter
4. Bake it on both sides a nice brown
5. When done, pour some hot syrup all over
6. Let it remain for ten minutes or so, dish up, and serve hot or cold

title: Churek Kaddyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- butter
- syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare vermicelli as No. 149
2. Twist it like a rope, twice the thickness of your thumb, and make it into cakes the size of a Bath bun
3. Lay them in a buttered baking-tin
4. Pour in about half a pound of scalded fresh butter
5. Bake it on both sides a nice brown
6. When done, pour some hot syrup all over
7. Let it remain for ten minutes or so
8. Dish up and serve hot or cold

title: Churek Kâdâyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- half a pound of scalded fresh butter
- hot syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare as much vermicelli as is required
2. Twist it like a rope, twice the thickness of your thumb, and make it into cakes the size of a Bath bun
3. Lay them in a buttered baking-tin, pour in about half a pound of scalded fresh butter, and bake it on both sides a nice brown
4. When done, pour some hot syrup all over, and let it remain for ten minutes or so
5. Dish up, and serve hot or cold

title: Dana-Eti Pulakissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- neck or loin of veal
- salt
- water
- parsley
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of a neck or loin of veal in pieces the size of kidneys
2. Wash and place them in a stewpan, with a little salt, and cover them with water
3. Set it on the fire
4. When boiling, skim and simmer until the meat is nearly cooked
5. Add a handful or two of picked parsley, and let it simmer for a few minutes longer
6. Two minutes before taking it off the fire, add the juice of one lemon
7. Dish it up, and serve

title: Ddmatizli Kizartma Ydhni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- fresh butter
- onions
- green tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- ripe tomatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Cut five or six pounds of the best part of mutton into pieces about the size of kidneys, wash and dry them in a cloth
2. Fry them a nice colour in fresh butter
3. Place them in a stewpan with three or four sliced onions fried in fresh butter, eight or ten green tomatoes each cut in four, and sufficient salt and pepper
4. Squeeze two or three pounds of ripe tomatoes in a basin, pass the juice through a cloth or sieve, and then pour it in the stewpan with sufficient water to cover the meat
5. Set it on a charcoal fire with the cover over, and let it simmer until the liquor is reduced to a nice thick gravy
6. Dish it up tastefully, and serve

title: Ddmdtiz-Dolmdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two dozen ripe or unripe tomatos
- stuffing
- broth

## Directions

1. Procure one or two dozen of either ripe or unripe tomatos
2. Cut off the tops about the size of a shilling but without separating them so as to form lids
3. Take out the seeds and part of the inside
4. Prepare some stuffing as above and stuff them
5. Close the lids
6. Place them carefully in a stewpan and nearly cover them with broth
7. Put the pan on the fire and let it simmer till a sufficient gravy is left
8. Dish up tastefully and serve

title: Digher Vishna Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- about three pounds of crushed sugar
- two pints of cold water
- white of an egg
- a pound of morella cherries

## Directions

1. Put about three pounds of crushed sugar in a stewpan, with two pints of cold water, and the white of an egg, well beaten up
2. Stir it well, and put it on the fire
3. When boiling, skim it well, and let it simmer until a little thickish
4. Add a pound of morella cherries with the stalks taken off, and well washed
5. Let it boil gently for a few minutes longer, then take it off
6. When nearly cold, take the cherries out with a strainer, and pass the syrup through a clean cloth
7. Then bottle and cork it up

title: Digher Vishna Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of crushed sugar
- two pints of cold water
- white of an egg
- one pound of morella cherries

## Directions

1. Put about three pounds of crushed sugar in a stewpan, with two pints of cold water, and the white of an egg, well beaten up
2. Stir it well, and put it on the fire
3. When boiling, skim it well, and let it simmer until a little thickish
4. Add a pound of morella cherries with the stalks taken off, and well washed
5. Let it boil gently for a few minutes longer, then take it off
6. When nearly cold, take the cherries out with a strainer, and pass the syrup through a clean cloth
7. Bottle and cork it up

title: Domatiz-Dolmessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two dozen of either ripe or unripe tomatoes
- stuffing

## Directions

1. Procure one or two dozen of either ripe or unripe tomatoes
2. Cut off the tops about the size of a shilling, but without separating them, so as to form lids
3. Take out the seeds and part of the inside
4. Prepare some stuffing
5. Stuff them, and close the lids
6. Place them carefully in a stewpan, and nearly cover them with broth
7. Put the pan on the fire, and let it simmer till a sufficient gravy is left
8. Dish up tastefully, and serve

title: Dombtiz Pilbwi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two finely-chopped onions
- fresh butter
- a pint of tomato juice
- a pint of broth
- sufficient salt
- a pound of the best rice, well washed
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Fry a nice brown one or two finely-chopped onions in fresh butter.
2. Put them in a saucepan with a pint of tomato juice which you have previously passed through a cloth or a sieve, the same quantity of broth, and sufficient salt, and put the pan on the fire.
3. When boiling, add a pound of the best rice, well washed, and let it boil gently till the liquor is absorbed.
4. Put on the fire a saucepan with a quarter of a pound of fresh butter.
5. When very hot, pour it over the rice, stir it once round with a fork.
6. Put the cover over the pan, and let it remain on the hob for fifteen or twenty minutes.
7. Dish it up, sprinkle a pinch of pepper over, and serve.

title: Dombuiz Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two finely-chopped onions
- fresh butter
- a pint of tomato juice
- a pint of broth
- sufficient salt
- a pound of the best rice, well washed
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- a pinch of pepper

## Directions

1. Fry a nice brown one or two finely-chopped onions in fresh butter.
2. Put them in a saucepan with a pint of tomato juice, which you have previously passed through a cloth or a sieve, the same quantity of broth, and sufficient salt, and put the pan on the fire.
3. When boiling, add a pound of the best rice, well washed, and let it boil gently till the liquor is absorbed.
4. Put on the fire a saucepan with a quarter of a pound of fresh butter.
5. When very hot, pour it over the rice, stir it once round with a fork, put the cover over the pan, and let it remain on the hob for fifteen or twenty minutes.
6. Dish it up, sprinkle a pinch of pepper over, and serve.

title: Ehmek Khdayifi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- white sugar
- water
- muffins
- pistachios or almonds
- clotted cream
- syrup

## Directions

1. Put a pound of white sugar in a clean saucepan, with two pints of water
2. Set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
3. Cut open four or five muffins
4. Put them in the syrup for two or three minutes
5. Take them out carefully with a slice
6. Lay half of them in a baking-tin
7. Sprinkle some pounded pistachios or almonds over
8. Lay a layer of clotted cream over, a quarter of an inch thick
9. Sprinkle again pistachios or almonds
10. Cover them with the other half of the muffins
11. Pour three-quarters of a pint of the syrup over
12. Put it in the oven, or on a moderate charcoal fire, until the syrup is nearly absorbed
13. Dish up and serve

title: Ekshili-Badinjan
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five egg-plants
- salt
- olive oil or fresh butter
- four or five onions
- half a teacup of wine vinegar
- one teacup of water
- two or three teaspoonfuls of honey or sugar

## Directions

1. Cut four or five egg-plants in rather thin slices lengthways
2. Sprinkle them with salt and let them remain until the bitter juice is drawn from them
3. Fry them a nice colour in olive oil or fresh butter and lay them in a pan
4. Fry a nice brown four or five sliced onions in fresh butter or olive oil with a little salt and lay them over the egg-plants
5. Add half a teacup of wine vinegar, one of water, and two or three teaspoonfuls of honey or sugar
6. Set the pan on a moderate fire until the liquor is nearly absorbed
7. Dish them up carefully and serve

title: Ekshili-Chorba
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- nice mutton
- sufficient salt
- water
- sufficient flour
- yolks of a few eggs
- sufficient wine vinegar or lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Cut some nice mutton into pieces the size of small walnuts
2. Wash and put them in a stewpan with sufficient salt and as much water as the quantity of soup required
3. Put the pan on the fire
4. When boiling, skim it well
5. Let it simmer until the pieces of meat are tender and skim off the fat
6. Put in a basin sufficient flour, which mix with water to form a smooth batter, but not too thick
7. Add it to the broth by degrees, stirring at the same time, so as to prevent it from getting lumpy
8. Let it simmer till the smell of flour is dispersed
9. Take it off the fire
10. Beat up the yolks of a few eggs in a small saucepan with sufficient wine vinegar or lemon-juice
11. Set it on a slow charcoal

title: Emrud Tatlusi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- stewing pears
- syrup
- cloves
- prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Peel and take off the stalks of as many stewing pears as are wanted
2. If too large, cut them in two and remove the cores
3. Place them in a stewpan with some syrup, a few cloves, and a few drops of prepared cochineal
4. Let it simmer gently on a slow fire until the pears are tender
5. Take it off and serve cold

title: Emrud Tdtlussi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- Stewing pears
- Syrup
- Cloves
- Prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Peel and take off the stalks of as many stewing pears as are wanted
2. If too large, cut them in two and remove the cores
3. Place them in a stewpan with some syrup, a few cloves, and a few drops of prepared cochineal
4. Let it simmer gently on a slow fire until the pears are tender
5. Take it off and serve cold

title: Erkshili-Chorba
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- nice mutton
- sufficient salt
- water
- flour
- yolks of a few eggs
- sufficient wine vinegar or lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton into pieces the size of small walnuts
2. Wash and put them in a stewpan with sufficient salt and as much water as the quantity of soup required
3. Put the pan on the fire
4. When boiling, skim it well
5. Let it simmer until the pieces of meat are tender, and skim off the fat
6. Put sufficient flour in a basin and mix with water to form a smooth batter, but not too thick
7. Add it to the broth by degrees, stirring at the same time, so as to prevent it from getting lumpy
8. Let it simmer till the smell of flour is dispersed
9. Take it off the fire
10. Beat up the yolks of a few eggs in a small saucepan with sufficient wine vinegar or lemon-juice
11. Set it on a slow charcoal

title: Et-Suyu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six pounds of shin of beef or knuckle of veal
- sufficient salt
- three or four onions
- a small stick of celery
- three or four quarts of water

## Directions

1. Wash the meat well
2. Put it in a stewpan with sufficient salt, three or four onions cut in four cross-wise, a small stick of celery, and three or four quarts of water
3. Set the pan on the fire
4. When boiling, skim it well
5. Let it simmer until the meat separates from the bones
6. Pass the broth through a sieve

title: Et-Telvasssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- two or three onions
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of a leg of mutton into pieces the size of large eggs
2. split each piece in two or three places, and beat them out with a rolling-pin
3. sprinkle them with sufficient salt and pepper, cinnamon, and two or three finely-chopped onions, and let them remain for half an hour or so
4. put some nice butter in the fryingpan, place it on the fire
5. When quite hot, lay the pieces of mutton in it, and when nicely browned on one side turn them over, and do the other side the same
6. take them out, drain the fat, and serve

title: Et-Tetivassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- two or three finely-chopped onions
- nice butter

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of a leg of mutton into pieces the size of large eggs
2. Split each piece in two or three places, and beat them out with a rolling-pin
3. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt and pepper, cinnamon, and two or three finely-chopped onions
4. Let them remain for half an hour or so
5. Put some nice butter in the fryingpan, place it on the fire
6. When quite hot, lay the pieces of mutton in it
7. When nicely browned on one side turn them over, and do the other side the same
8. Take them out, drain the fat, and serve

title: Fassulya Mujmeri
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- French beans
- salt
- flour
- four or five eggs
- four or five tablespoonfuls of fried minced meat
- pepper
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Scald sufficient quantity of French beans with a little salt
2. when partly tender, put them on a board, and chop them up
3. place them in a basin, sprinkle some flour over, and rub them smooth with your hands
4. add four or five eggs, four or five tablespoonfuls of fried minced meat, a little salt and pepper, and beat them up well with a fork
5. put some fresh butter into the fryingpan, set it on the fire until hot
6. fry the mixture a tablespoonful at a time
7. When all are fried a nice colour, dish them up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Fddla Khdayiji
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four French rolls
- eggs
- scalded fresh butter
- white sugar
- water
- rose-water

## Directions

1. Cut the French rolls in slices lengthways, about a quarter of an inch thick
2. Beat up a few eggs in a basin
3. Dip the slices of bread in the beaten eggs
4. Fry them a nice delicate colour in scalded fresh butter on both sides
5. Take them out with a slice and let them drain
6. Make a syrup of white sugar and water as in No. 159
7. Put the fried bread in hot syrup and let them soak for ten or fifteen minutes
8. Take them out and place them tastefully on a dish
9. Sprinkle a little rose-water over and serve
10. If rolls are not handy, slices of bread with the crusts taken off can be used instead

title: Fedla Khdayiji
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four French rolls
- eggs
- scalded fresh butter
- white sugar
- water
- rose-water

## Directions

1. Cut the rolls in slices lengthways, about a quarter of an inch thick
2. Beat up a few eggs in a basin
3. Dip the slices of bread in the beaten eggs
4. Fry them a nice delicate colour in scalded fresh butter on both sides
5. Take them out with a slice and let them drain
6. Put them in hot syrup which you have previously made of white sugar and water
7. Let them soak for ten or fifteen minutes
8. Take them out and place them tastefully on a dish
9. Sprinkle a little rose-water over and serve
10. If rolls are not handy, slices of bread with the crusts taken off can be used instead

title: Finjan-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- ingredients from preceding pastry
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Place on a paste-board three or four layers of paste (No. 86), one over the other
2. Put over in small lots any of the ingredients used in the preceding pastry, about an inch and a half distance from each other
3. Wet slightly round them with a paste-brush, and cover them with the same number of pastes as before, but a little larger
4. Press over each lot with a Turkish coffee-cup, or a mould the size of an egg-cup, and cut them out, which will then be in the shape of the above cup
5. When all done, either fry them in hot butter, or arrange them in a baking-tin, previously buttered
6. Sprinkle a little butter over them with a paste-brush, and bake them a nice colour in a moderate oven
7. When done, dish them up on a white napkin, and serve hot

title: Finjan-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- any of the ingredients used in preceding pastry
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Place on a paste-board three or four layers of paste, one over the other
2. Put over in small lots any of the ingredients used in the preceding pastry, about an inch and a half distance from each other
3. Wet slightly round them with a paste-brush, and cover them with the same number of pastes as before, but a little larger
4. Press over each lot with a Turkish coffee-cup, or a mould the size of an egg-cup, and cut them out, which will then be in the shape of the above cup
5. When all done, either fry them in hot butter, or arrange them in a baking-tin, previously buttered
6. Sprinkle a little butter over them with a paste-brush, and bake them a nice colour in a moderate oven
7. When done, dish them up on a white napkin, and serve hot

title: Fisstik Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half or a pound of pistachios
- a teacup or two of rose-water
- powdered sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Procure half or a pound of pistachios and skin them
2. Pound the broken and part of the small ones very fine in a stone mortar
3. Add a teacup or two of rose-water and stir it well
4. Pass it through a clean cloth into a glass bowl
5. Mix it with sufficient powdered sugar and water to make a nice sherbet
6. Add the remaining pistachios and serve

title: Fisstik Khoshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half or a pound of pistachios
- a teacup or two of rose-water
- powdered sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Procure half or a pound of pistachios, and skin them
2. Pound the broken and part of the small ones very fine in a stone mortar
