3. Add a teacup or two of rose-water, stir it well, and pass it through a clean cloth into a glass bowl
4. Mix it with sufficient powdered sugar and water to make a nice sherbet
5. Add the remaining pistachios, and serve

title: Ghdziler Helwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of fresh butter
- a pound and a half of flour
- two pints of boiling syrup
- honey or loaf sugar
- water
- a pint of boiling milk
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Put a pound of fresh butter in a very clean stewpan
2. Put it on a charcoal fire and scald it
3. Take it off and when it gets a little cold, add by degrees a pound and a half of the best flour
4. Set it again on the fire and keep stirring until the flour becomes a gold colour
5. Take it off and immediately pour two pints of boiling syrup over, which you have previously prepared of honey or loaf sugar and water
6. Pour a pint of boiling milk over
7. Put the cover over and let it remain for five minutes
8. Stir it well with a wooden fork
9. Dish up in tablespoonfuls and let it bear the impression of the spoon
10. Shake some white sugar over and serve

title: Ghilbur
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four onions
- fresh butter
- water
- salt
- tablespoonful of vinegar
- six or seven eggs
- pepper
- powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel and slice fine three or four onions, and fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
2. Lay them on the bottom of a dish, and keep it hot
3. Pour in a pan a pint of water, add a little salt, and a tablespoonful of vinegar, put it on the fire
4. When boiling, break carefully in the pan six or seven eggs, simmer till the white is firm
5. Take them out with a slice, and arrange them over the onions
6. Pour a little hot fresh butter over them, sprinkle a little pepper and powdered cinnamon over, and serve whilst hot

title: Ghilbur
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four onions
- fresh butter
- a pint of water
- a little salt
- a tablespoonful of vinegar
- six or seven eggs
- pepper
- powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel and slice fine three or four onions, and fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
2. Lay them on the bottom of a dish, and keep it hot
3. Pour in a pan a pint of water, add a little salt, and a tablespoonful of vinegar, put it on the fire
4. When boiling, break carefully in the pan six or seven eggs, simmer till the white is firm
5. Take them out with a slice, and arrange them over the onions
6. Pour a little hot fresh butter over them, sprinkle a little pepper and powdered cinnamon over, and serve whilst hot

title: Ghimyonlu Kyiifte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw mutton or beef
- two or three onions peeled
- salt
- pepper
- cummin
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Mince very fine as much as is wanted of either raw mutton or beef, with two or three onions peeled
2. sprinkle sufficient salt, pepper, and cummin
3. knead well and make it up in the shape of small buns
4. fry them in fresh butter, or broil them on a gridiron a nice brown
5. When done, arrange them in a dish, and serve hot, with chopped parsley mixed with thinly sliced onions round the dish

title: Ghimyonlu Kyiifte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw mutton or beef
- two or three onions peeled
- salt
- pepper
- cummin
- fresh butter or for broiling on a gridiron
- chopped parsley
- thinly sliced onions

## Directions

1. Mince very fine as much as is wanted of either raw mutton or beef, with two or three onions peeled
2. Sprinkle sufficient salt, pepper, and cummin
3. Knead well and make it up in the shape of small buns
4. Fry them in fresh butter, or broil them on a gridiron a nice brown
5. When done, arrange them in a dish, and serve hot, with chopped parsley mixed with thinly sliced onions round the dish

title: Ghurabiye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds and a half of the best fresh butter, melted
- two pounds and a half of powdered sugar
- flour

## Directions

1. Put in a basin or pan two pounds and a half of the best fresh butter, melted, with two pounds and a half of powdered sugar
2. Stir it well
3. Add gradually as much flour as it will take
4. Knead it well, and make it into small cakes
5. Place them in a buttered tin, and bake a nice colour in the oven

title: Ghyi Habiye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of loaf sugar
- a pint of water
- half a pound of fresh butter
- a pound of wheat-starch
- a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds
- three or four tablespoonfuls of rose-water
- musk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put a pound of loaf sugar in a stewpan, with a pint of water, and set it on the fire
2. When boiling, add half a pound of fresh butter, a pound of wheat-starch, previously formed into a thin batter with water, a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds, and three or four tablespoonfuls of rose-water, mixed with a little musk
3. Stir it gently until it becomes crummy
4. Then spread it in a rather deep dish, press it all over with the back of the spoon, cut it up in diamonds, and serve with a little sugar sifted over

title: GhyiHabiye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of loaf sugar
- a pint of water
- half a pound of fresh butter
- a pound of wheat-starch
- a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds
- three or four tablespoonfuls of rose-water
- musk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put a pound of loaf sugar in a stewpan, with a pint of water, and set it on the fire
2. When boiling, add half a pound of fresh butter, a pound of wheat-starch previously formed into a thin batter with water, a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds, and three or four tablespoonfuls of rose-water, mixed with a little musk
3. Stir it gently until it becomes crummy
4. Spread it in a rather deep dish, press it all over with the back of the spoon, cut it up in diamonds, and serve with a little sugar sifted over

title: Ghyugliej Balighi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- any fish
- salt
- a teacupful of olive oil
- four or five onions
- a handful or two of chopped parsley
- half a teacupful of wine vinegar
- one teacupful of water
- three or four tomatoes
- preserved tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Get any fish you fancy, scale and clean it well
2. If a large one, cut it in three or four pieces
3. Sprinkle some salt over and let them remain for a little while
4. Put a teacupful of olive oil in a frying-pan with four or five sliced onions and a handful or two of chopped parsley
5. Put it on the fire and stew it rather brown
6. Lay half of it on the bottom of a rather deep baking-dish and place the fish over
7. Put the other half of the onions and parsley on the top with the oil that is left in the frying-pan
8. Add half a teacupful of wine vinegar and one of water, three or four tomatoes cut in four crossways, or one or two tablespoonfuls of preserved tomato sauce if the tomato is not in season
9. Put it in the oven for twenty minutes or so
10. When nicely baked, dish it up and serve

title: Ghyugliej Bassissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pounds of loin of mutton
- one or two egg-plants
- salt
- six ounces of French beans trimmed
- about sixty okras
- one or two vegetable marrows, peeled
- four or five green tomatos
- five or six onions peeled
- sufficient salt and pepper
- juice of one or two lemons
- juice of a pound and a half of tomatos
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, melted (if needed)
- flour paste
- a little cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut three or four pounds of loin of mutton in pieces the size of walnuts
2. Wash and place them in an earthen stewpan
3. Cut one or two egg-plants in four crossways, and cut them in slices an inch thick
4. Sprinkle some salt over, and when the bitter juice is drawn from them, lay them over the meat
5. Add six ounces of French beans trimmed, about sixty okras with the stalks and the ends cut off and stringed, one or two vegetable marrows peeled and cut in slices as the egg-plants, four or five green tomatos cut in four crossways, five or six onions peeled and cut as the tomatos, sufficient salt and pepper, the juice of one or two lemons, and the juice of a pound and a half of tomatos
6. If the meat is not sufficiently fat, add a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, melted
7. Put the lid over the pan, and cement round the edges with flour paste, so as to prevent the steam from escaping
8. Put it in a moderately hot oven for about three hours
9. When done, shake the pan gently, that the gravy may mix with the vegetables, and form a nice, thick sauce, then dish up, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Ghyugliej Bastsissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pounds of loin of mutton
- one or two egg-plants
- salt
- six ounces of French beans trimmed
- about sixty okras
- one or two vegetable marrows, peeled
- four or five green tomatos
- five or six onions
- pepper
- juice of one or two lemons
- juice of a pound and a half of tomatos
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, melted
- flour paste
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut three or four pounds of loin of mutton in pieces the size of walnuts; wash and place them in an earthen stewpan, of which the lid must closely fit
2. Cut one or two egg-plants in four crossways, and cut them in slices an inch thick; sprinkle some salt over, and when the bitter juice is drawn from them, lay them over the meat
3. Add six ounces of French beans trimmed, about sixty okras, the stalks and the ends cut off and stringed, one or two vegetable marrows, peeled and cut in slices as the egg-plants, four or five green tomatos cut in four crossways, five or six onions peeled and cut as the tomatos, sufficient salt and pepper, the juice of one or two lemons, and the juice of a pound and a half of tomatos
4. If the meat is not sufficiently fat, add a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, melted
5. Put the lid over the pan, and cement round the edges with flour paste, so as to prevent the steam from escaping
6. Put it in a moderately hot oven for about three hours
7. When done, shake the pan gently, that the gravy may mix with the vegetables, and form a nice, thick sauce, then dish up, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Ghyul Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound and six ounces of fresh-gathered rose-leaves
- a pint and a half of water
- two pounds and three-quarters of sifted sugar
- white of an egg

## Directions

1. Procure a pound and six ounces of fresh-gathered rose-leaves
2. Pour in a very clean stewpan a pint and a half of water, put it on the fire
3. When boiling, put the rose-leaves in, and as soon as it boils up again take it off
4. Put the cover over, and leave it till cold
5. Pass it through a sieve into the saucepan, and cast the residue away
6. Add two pounds and three-quarters of sifted sugar and the white of an egg well beaten up
7. Put the pan on the fire
8. When it boils up, skim and take it off
9. Then pass it through a clean cloth, bottle, and cork it up till required

title: Ghyul Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound 6 ounces fresh-gathered rose-leaves
- 1 pint and a half of water
- 2 pounds 3 3/4 ounces sifted sugar
- white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Pour a pint and a half of water in a very clean stewpan and put it on the fire
2. When boiling, put the rose-leaves in, and as soon as it boils up again take it off
3. Put the cover over and leave it till cold
4. Pass it through a sieve into the stewpan and cast the residue away
5. Add 2 pounds and 3 3/4 ounces of sifted sugar and the white of an egg well beaten up
6. Put the pan on the fire
7. When it boils up, skim and take it off
8. Pass it through a clean cloth, bottle, and cork it up till required

title: Ghyul Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and six ounces of fresh-gathered rose-leaves
- one pint and a half of water
- two pounds and three-quarters of sifted sugar
- the white of an egg well beaten up

## Directions

1. Pour a pint and a half of water in a very clean stewpan and put it on the fire.
2. When boiling, put the rose-leaves in, and as soon as it boils up again take it off.
3. Put the cover over and leave it till cold.
4. Pass it through a sieve into the stewpan and cast the residue away.
5. Add two pounds and three-quarters of sifted sugar and the white of an egg well beaten up.
6. Put the pan on the fire.
7. When it boils up, skim and take it off.
8. Pass it through a clean cloth, bottle, and cork it up till required.

title: Ghyulbe Sheheri Shemsiyye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fresh rose-leaves
- 2 pounds and a half white sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Procure a pound of fresh rose-leaves and cut the white ends off with a pair of scissors
2. Throw the white ends away
3. Put the other parts in a basin with 2 pounds and a half of white sifted sugar
4. Work it together with the hands until the rose-leaves are reduced to a pulp or a paste
5. Fill the preserve jars or glass bowls with cover
6. Arrange them on a tray and put it where the sun is powerful
7. When near sun-set, put the cover over and take them in
8. The next day uncover the jars and expose the preserve to the sun as before
9. Continue the same for a month or until the top of the preserve is nicely crystallized

title: Ghyuldj Baladivessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- the whites of twenty eggs
- about a pound of wheat starch
- water
- rose-water
- milk
- clotted cream
- powdered sugar
- a pound of loaf sugar
- two pints of water
- pounded almonds or pistachios

## Directions

1. Put in a basin the whites of twenty eggs, and whip them up well
2. Add about a pound of wheat starch by degrees, stir it well, and mix it well with water, to form a very thin batter
3. Put over a charcoal fire a circular and slightly-domed sheet-iron, the under side in the centre of which you have previously cemented with ashes half an inch thick, to prevent it from getting fiercely hot
4. When moderately hot, pour a portion of the batter over the centre, with a convenient spoon, sufficient to form a round cake, the size of a large dinner-plate, or larger, and which will be done in less than a minute; it ought to be very white, and as thin as tissue-paper
5. Prepare fifty or sixty pieces in this manner
6. Lay half of them in a suitable baking-tin, one over the other, sprinkling between each piece some rose-water and milk as you lay them
7. Lay some clotted cream over, a quarter of an inch thick, and shake some powdered sugar over
8. Cover it with the other half of the cakes, sprinkling rose-water and milk between each one as before
9. Put in a stewpan a pound of loaf sugar, with two pints of water, and set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
10. Take it off and pour it over the cakes, till covered, and let it remain covered until cold
11. Cut it up in diamonds, dish up and serve. Pounded almonds or pistachios may be used, if clotted cream is not handy

title: Ghyuldj Balddivassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- the whites of twenty eggs
- about a pound of wheat starch
- water
- rose-water
- milk
- clotted cream
- powdered sugar
- a pound of loaf sugar
- two pints of water

## Directions

1. Put in a basin the whites of twenty eggs, and whip them up well
2. Add about a pound of wheat starch by degrees, stir it well, and mix it well with water, to form a very thin batter
3. Put over a charcoal fire a circular and slightly-domed sheet-iron, the under side in the centre of which you have previously cemented with ashes half an inch thick, to prevent it from getting fiercely hot
4. When moderately hot, pour a portion of the batter over the centre, with a convenient spoon, sufficient to form a round cake, the size of a large dinner-plate, or larger, and which will be done in less than a minute
5. Prepare fifty or sixty pieces in this manner
6. Lay half of them in a suitable baking-tin, one over the other, sprinkling between each piece some rose-water and milk
7. Lay some clotted cream over, a quarter of an inch thick, and shake some powdered sugar over
8. Cover it with the other half of the cakes, sprinkling rose-water and milk between each one
9. Put in a stewpan a pound of loaf sugar, with two pints of water, and set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
10. Take it off and pour it over the cakes, till covered, and let it remain covered until cold
11. Cut it up in diamonds, dish up and serve

title: Ghyuldj Kizartmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty or thirty cakes
- rose-water
- pounded almonds or pistachios
- egg
- hot fresh butter
- boiling syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare twenty or thirty cakes as No. 186, and moisten them by sprinkling rose-water over
2. Fold them in two separately
3. Put on each one some pounded almonds or pistachios
4. Fold them in square or triangular shape
5. Dip them in egg, and fry them a delicate colour on both sides in hot fresh butter
6. Drain them, and then throw them in boiling syrup
7. When sufficiently soaked, take them out

title: Ghyuldj Kizdrtmdissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty or thirty cakes prepared as No. 186
- rose-water
- pounded almonds or pistachios
- egg
- hot fresh butter
- boiling syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare twenty or thirty cakes as No. 186, and moisten them by sprinkling rose-water over
2. Fold them in two separately, put on each one some pounded almonds or pistachios, then fold them in square or triangular shape
3. Dip them in egg, and fry them a delicate colour on both sides in hot fresh butter
4. Drain them, and then throw them in boiling syrup, prepared as above
5. When sufficiently soaked, take them out

title: Ghyuldj Kizdrtmdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty or thirty cakes (No. 186)
- rose-water
- pounded almonds or pistachios
- egg
- fresh butter
- boiling syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare twenty or thirty cakes as No. 186
2. Moisten them by sprinkling rose-water over
3. Fold them in two separately
4. Put on each one some pounded almonds or pistachios
5. Fold them in square or triangular shape
6. Dip them in egg
7. Fry them a delicate colour on both sides in hot fresh butter
8. Drain them
9. Throw them in boiling syrup, prepared as above
10. When sufficiently soaked, take them out with a spoon
11. Arrange them tastefully on a dish
12. Serve hot or cold

title: Ghyuldj Pdliidessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of loaf sugar
- two pints of water
- cake (No. 186)
- rose-water
- skinned almonds or pistachios

## Directions

1. Put a pound of loaf sugar in a stewpan with two pints of water
2. Set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
3. Take it off and pour it into a rather deep baking-tin
4. Put it on a moderate charcoal fire
5. When boiling, put in a cake (No. 186)
6. Bruise it with the back of a wooden spoon
7. Continue in this manner as many cakes as it will take
8. When thick, take it off
9. Sprinkle some rose-water over
10. Let it remain until cold
11. Dish it up
12. Ornament the top with some skinned almonds or pistachios
13. Serve

title: Ghyuldj Pdliidessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of loaf sugar
- two pints of water
- cakes (No. 186)
- rose-water
- skinned almonds or pistachios

## Directions

1. Put a pound of loaf sugar in a stewpan, with two pints of water
2. Set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
3. Take it off and pour it into a rather deep baking-tin
4. Put it on a moderate charcoal fire
5. When boiling, put in a cake and bruise it with the back of a wooden spoon
6. Continue in this manner as many cakes as it will take
7. When thick, take it off and sprinkle some rose-water over
8. Let it remain until cold
9. Dish it up and ornament the top with some skinned almonds or pistachios
10. Serve

title: Ghyulhe-Sheker
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound rose-leaves
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- 3 pounds and a half white sifted sugar

## Directions

1. When the roses are in full bloom, gather about a pound of the leaves
2. Pick out the large ones and cut the white ends off with a pair of scissors
3. Put the small ones and the white ends in a very clean stewpan with three-quarters of a pint of water and scald them
4. Pass the liquor through a piece of muslin or a sieve and squeeze well the leaves
5. Throw the leaves away
6. Put the liquor in the pan with 3 pounds and a half of white sifted sugar
7. Stir with a wooden spoon till the sugar is dissolved
8. Add the large leaves that you have picked out
9. Stir and boil till rather thick
10. Try a little on a plate to see if it sets
11. When cold, fill the preserve jars and cover them air-tight
12. Keep them in a rather cool place

title: Ghyulhe-Sheker
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- about one pound of rose leaves
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- three pounds and a half of white sifted sugar

## Directions

1. When the roses are in full bloom, gather about a pound of the leaves.
2. Pick out the large ones and cut the white ends off with a pair of scissors.
3. Put the small ones and the white ends in a very clean stewpan with three-quarters of a pint of water and scald them.
4. Pass the liquor through a piece of muslin or a sieve and squeeze well the leaves and throw them away.
5. Put the liquor in the pan with three pounds and a half of white sifted sugar.
6. Stir them with a wooden spoon till the sugar is dissolved.
7. Add the large leaves that you have picked out.
8. Stir and boil till rather thick.
9. Try a little on a plate.
10. If it sets, remove it from the fire.
11. When cold, fill the preserve jars and cover them air-tight.
12. Keep them in a rather cool place.

title: Haceb-Dolmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen egg-plants
- raw mutton
- one or two onions
- mint
- saffron
- two or three tablespoonfuls of the inside of egg-plants
- a handful of well-washed rice
- salt
- pepper
- rib-bones of mutton
- broth
- juice of two or three lemons

## Directions

1. Cut off the tops and take out the insides of half a dozen egg-plants
2. Put them in salt water for ten minutes or so
3. Mince fine a sufficient quantity of raw mutton, add one or two onions, a little mint, a small quantity of saffron, two or three tablespoonfuls of the inside of egg-plants, all chopped fine, a handful of well-washed rice, a little salt and pepper, mix them well all together with your hands
4. Stuff the egg-plants with it, and cover them with the tops
5. Lay some rib-bones of mutton on the bottom of a stewpan, and place the egg-plants over carefully
6. Cover them with broth, add the juice of two or three lemons
7. Set the pan on a charcoal fire with the cover over, and let it simmer until the egg-plants are soft, and a nice gravy is formed at the bottom
8. Dish them up carefully, and serve

title: Hadji Ossman Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- loin of mutton or lamb
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut sufficient quantity of a loin of mutton or lamb in pieces the size of walnuts, and rinse them with water
2. Sprinkle one or two chopped onions over, with sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon
3. Mix well together, put them in a stewpan without any water, and cover it air tight
4. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire for nearly four hours, and stir it now and then
5. Be careful, for if the fire is too slow the meat will not brown nicely
6. When the pieces of meat are a nice colour and tender, dish them up, and serve hot

title: Haji Ossman Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- loin of mutton or lamb
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut sufficient quantity of a loin of mutton or lamb in pieces the size of walnuts, and rinse them with water
2. Sprinkle one or two chopped onions over, with sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon
3. Mix well together
4. Put them in a stewpan without any water, and cover it air tight
5. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire for nearly four hours, and stir it now and then
6. When the pieces of meat are a nice colour and tender, dish them up, and serve hot

title: Hashmerim
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of very fresh cream-cheese
- ounce of flour
- sifted sugar or honey

## Directions

1. Get two pounds of very fresh cream-cheese, break it in small pieces into a stewpan, put it on the fire, and stir until it is melted
2. Add an ounce of flour, and keep stirring until the butter begins to run from the cheese
3. Take it off, dish up, and serve with some sifted sugar or honey over

title: Hctjeb-Ddlmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen egg-plants
- raw mutton
- one or two onions
- mint
- small quantity of saffron
- two or three tablespoonfuls of the inside of egg-plants
- handful of well-washed rice
- salt
- pepper
- rib-bones of mutton
- broth
- juice of two or three lemons

## Directions

1. Cut off the tops and take out the insides of half a dozen egg-plants, leaving only sufficient to bear the stuffing
2. Put them in salt water for ten minutes or so
3. Mince fine a sufficient quantity of raw mutton
4. Add one or two onions, a little mint, a small quantity of saffron, two or three tablespoonfuls of the inside of egg-plants, all chopped fine
5. Add a handful of well-washed rice, a little salt and pepper
6. Mix them well all together with your hands
7. Stuff the egg-plants with it, and cover them with the tops
8. Lay some rib-bones of mutton on the bottom of a stewpan, and place the egg-plants over carefully
9. Cover them with broth, add the juice of two or three lemons
10. Set the pan on a charcoal fire with the cover over, and let it simmer until the egg-plants are soft, and a nice gravy is formed at the bottom
11. Dish them up carefully, and serve

title: Helwdi Khdkdni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of the best flour
- half a pound of wheat-starch
- half a pound of rice-flour
- half a pound of the best fresh butter
- three pints of boiling syrup
- two pints of boiling milk

## Directions

1. Have ready half a pound of the best flour, ditto of wheat-starch, and the same of rice-flour
2. Put half a pound of the best fresh butter in a stewpan, set it on a charcoal fire
3. When melted, add by degrees the above flours, and keep stirring until it becomes a gold colour
4. Pour all at once three pints of boiling syrup over, which you have previously prepared of loaf sugar and water, as in No. 159, and two pints of boiling milk
5. Instantly put the cover on the pan, and let it remain for fifteen minutes
6. Bruise it with the back of a spoon, dish up a tablespoonful at a time, letting it bear the impression of the spoon, and serve

title: Hindiba Salatassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three heads of endive
- small teaspoonful of salt
- one or two pinches of pepper
- juice of a small lemon
- three tablespoonfuls of olive oil
- tablespoonful of chopped chervil
- one tablespoonful of chives

## Directions

1. Remove the dark green leaves from endive
2. Pick off the others one by one
3. Cut the large ones in two
4. Wash and drain them in a cloth, by shaking it to and fro violently, and extract all the water
5. Put in the salad-bowl a small teaspoonful of salt, one or two pinches of pepper, the juice of a small lemon, three tablespoonfuls of olive oil
6. Beat up until it becomes like cream
7. Put in the salad
8. Turn it with spoon and fork till well mixed
9. Serve

title: Hindiba Salatassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three heads of endive
- salt
- pepper
- juice of a small lemon
- three tablespoonsful of olive oil

## Directions

1. Remove the dark green leaves from the endive heads
2. Pick off the other leaves one by one
3. Cut the large ones in two
4. Wash and drain them in a cloth by shaking it to and fro violently
5. Extract all the water
6. Put in the salad-bowl a small teaspoonful of salt, one or two pinches of pepper, the juice of a small lemon, three tablespoonsful of olive oil
7. Beat up until it becomes like cream
8. Put in the salad and turn it with spoon and fork till well mixed
9. Serve

title: Hldshlama Balih Kyulbasstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- grey mullet or other fish
- olive-oil
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- wine vinegar
- broth

## Directions

1. Get some grey mullet, or any other fish you fancy, clean it well, then scald it
2. Rub a gridiron with a little olive-oil, and place the fish over
3. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire, and turn it now and then until both sides are nearly browned
4. Take it off; then lay one or two handfuls of chopped parsley on the bottom of a baking dish or tin, and lay the fish over with a little salt and pepper
5. Add half a teacup of wine vinegar, and two of broth
6. Put it on a charcoal fire until the broth is absorbed, then dish it up, and serve hot

title: Ichi Dblmush Kiizu Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- small lamb
- liver
- heart
- fresh butter
- onions
- skinned pistachios
- currants
- rice
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- onion-juice

## Directions

1. Get the whole of a small lamb with the liver and the heart
2. Place it in a large saucepan or iron pot without the liver and the heart
3. Cover it with water, set it on the fire, and let it boil for ten or fifteen minutes
4. Chop up the liver very fine together with the heart
5. Put three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter in a stewpan with three or four chopped onions
6. Put it on the fire, and let them stew till nearly brown
7. Add the liver and the heart, with a handful of skinned pistachios, and the same of currants well cleaned
8. Stir till the liver begins to cook, then add two or three pounds of the best rice, well washed, sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon
9. Keep stirring, and add now and then a little of the liquor in which the lamb is boiled, until the rice is rather soft
10. Stuff the lamb with this preparation, and sew it up
11. Rub the lamb all over with some onion-juice
12. Place it in a baking tin, and bake it a nice brown on both sides in a very hot oven
13. When done, dish up, and serve

title: Ichi Dblmush Kuzu Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a whole small lamb
- the liver and the heart
- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- three or four chopped onions
- a handful of skinned pistachios
- a handful of currants
- two or three pounds of the best rice
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- onion-juice

## Directions

1. Get the whole of a small lamb with the liver and the heart, place it in a large saucepan or iron pot without the liver and the heart
2. Cover it with water, set it on the fire, and let it boil for ten or fifteen minutes
3. Chop up the liver very fine together with the heart
4. Put three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter in a stewpan with three or four chopped onions, put it on the fire, and let them stew till nearly brown
5. Add the liver and the heart, with a handful of skinned pistachios, and the same of currants well cleaned
6. Stir till the liver begins to cook, then add two or three pounds of the best rice, well washed, sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon
7. Keep stirring, and add now and then a little of the liquor in which the lamb is boiled, until the rice is rather soft
8. Stuff the lamb with this preparation, and sew it up
9. Rub the lamb all over with some onion-juice, place it in a baking tin, and bake it a nice brown on both sides in a very hot oven
10. When done, dish up, and serve

title: Ildshlama Balih Kyulbasstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- grey mullet or other fish
- olive-oil
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- wine vinegar
- broth

## Directions

1. Get some grey mullet or any other fish you fancy
2. Clean it well, then scald it
3. Rub a gridiron with a little olive-oil and place the fish over
4. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire and turn it now and then until both sides are nearly browned
5. Take it off
6. Lay one or two handfuls of chopped parsley on the bottom of a baking dish or tin
7. Lay the fish over with a little salt and pepper
8. Add half a teacup of wine vinegar and two of broth
9. Put it on a charcoal fire until the broth is absorbed
10. Dish it up and serve hot

title: Imam-Bayildi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen egg-plants
- salt
- five or six onions
- olive oil or fresh butter
- half a dozen peeled cloves of garlic
- a pint of water

## Directions

1. Procure half a dozen egg-plants and cut each one in four lengthways without separating from the stalk
2. Sprinkle them with salt and let them remain for five or ten minutes
3. Peel and cut five or six onions in rather thick slices
4. Fry them partly brown in olive oil or fresh butter
5. Lay half of them on the bottom of a stewpan, over which place the egg-plants and lay the other half of the onions over them
6. Add half a dozen peeled cloves of garlic and a pint of water or little more
7. Put the pan on the fire and let it simmer till the egg-plants are soft
8. Dish them up carefully and serve hot or cold

title: Inja-Burek
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound and a half of flour
- a teaspoonful of salt
- four eggs
- sufficient water
- fresh butter
- wheat-starch
- minced mutton
- mushrooms
- onions

## Directions

1. Put a pound and a half of flour on a slab, make a hole in the centre, in which put a teaspoonful of salt and four eggs
2. Mix all together, then add sufficient water, and form it a softish flexible paste
3. Dip your hands into melted fresh butter, and work it for a few minutes longer
4. Divide it into thirty pieces the size of walnuts, sprinkle a little wheat-starch both under and over them, and roll them out one by one with a rolling-pin as thin as a wafer, or thinner than that if you can, and the size of the baking-tin you intend to lay them in
5. Butter the baking-tin, in which lay five of the pieces of paste one over the other, sprinkle a little fresh butter all over, and lay five more pieces over, butter again, and over which lay five more pieces, butter again
6. Lay all over either minced mutton, mixed with mushrooms and onions, both chopped very fine, and previously stewed a nice brown in fresh butter, together with the meat, or cheese, or any other ingredients you may fancy
7. Cover it with the remaining pieces of paste in the same way as before
8. Cut it in four crossways, pour a little hot fresh butter over, and bake it a nice brown in a moderate oven
9. When done, dish up and serve

title: Inja-Burek
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound and a half of flour
- a teaspoonful of salt
- four eggs
- sufficient water
- melted fresh butter
- wheat-starch
- fresh butter
- minced mutton or cheese or mushrooms
- onions

## Directions

1. Put a pound and a half of flour on a slab, make a hole in the centre, in which put a teaspoonful of salt and four eggs
2. Mix all together, then add sufficient water, and form it a softish flexible paste
3. Dip your hands into melted fresh butter, and work it for a few minutes longer
4. Divide it into thirty pieces the size of walnuts, sprinkle a little wheat-starch both under and over them, and roll them out one by one with a rolling-pin as thin as a wafer, or thinner than that if you can, and the size of the baking-tin you intend to lay them in
5. Butter the baking-tin, in which lay five of the pieces of paste one over the other, sprinkle a little fresh butter all over, and lay five more pieces over, butter again, and over which lay five more pieces, butter again
6. Lay all over either minced mutton, mixed with mushrooms and onions, both chopped very fine, and previously stewed a nice brown in fresh butter, together with the meat, or cheese, or any other ingredients you may fancy
7. Cover it with the remaining pieces of paste in the same way as before
8. Cut it in four crossways, pour a little hot fresh butter over, and bake it a nice brown in a moderate oven
9. When done, dish up and serve

title: Injik Elmassiyessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four calf's feet
- six or seven pints of water
- a pound and a quarter of powdered sugar
- a pint and three-quarters of water
- the juice of three or four lemons
- the rind of one lemon, with the pith taken off
- the whites and shells of five or six eggs
- a few drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower
- a few white and black grapes
- strawberries and cherries

## Directions

1. Procure three or four calf's feet, wash and place them in a stew-pan, with six or seven pints of water, and put it on the fire
2. When boiling, skim and simmer until the flesh comes off the bones; then pass it through a clean cloth or a sieve into a basin, and let it remain until quite hard
3. Remove all the fat, &c., from the top
4. Put in a saucepan a pound and a quarter of powdered sugar, a pint and three-quarters of water, the juice of three or four lemons, the rind of one, with the pith taken off, the whites and shells of five or six eggs, and stir with a wooden spoon until the sugar is dissolved
5. Add the jelly, set it on the fire, and stir well until it boils; then pass it through a jelly-bag till quite clear
6. Add a few drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower, and then put it in a mould, with a few white and black grapes, the same of strawberries and cherries, &c.
7. Place it on ice till quite firm, turn it out, and serve

title: Injir Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two pounds of figs
- two or three pints of hot or cold water
- a few drops of orange-flower
- a few pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Cut off the stalks of one or two pounds of figs
2. Prick each one here and there with a wooden needle
3. Place them in a basin with two or three pints of hot or cold water
4. Let them remain for ten or twelve hours
5. Pass the liquid through a sieve into a glass bowl
6. Add the figs with a few drops of orange-flower and a few pieces of ice
7. Serve

title: Irimik Helwassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- a pound and six ounces of sago
- four ounces of skinned almonds
- a pint and a half of boiling milk
- a pound and a half of loaf sugar
- a pint and a half of water
- rose-water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Set on the fire a stewpan, with three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
2. When hot, add gradually a pound and six ounces of sago, with four ounces of skinned almonds, and keep stirring it until the almonds are a nice brown
3. Then pour in all at once a pint and a half of boiling milk, with boiling syrup, which you have previously made of a pound and a half of loaf sugar and a pint and a half of water
4. Stir it well, put the cover over, and let it remain for fifteen minutes
5. Then sprinkle a little rose-water over, dish up, shake some sugar over, and serve

title: Irimik Helwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- a pound and six ounces of sago
- four ounces of skinned almonds
- a pint and a half of boiling milk
- a pound and a half of loaf sugar
- a pint and a half of water
- rose-water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Set on the fire a stewpan, with three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
2. When hot, add gradually a pound and six ounces of sago, with four ounces of skinned almonds, and keep stirring it until the almonds are a nice brown
3. Pour in all at once a pint and a half of boiling milk, with boiling syrup, which you have previously made of a pound and a half of loaf sugar and a pint and a half of water
4. Stir it well, put the cover over, and let it remain for fifteen minutes
5. Sprinkle a little rose-water over, dish up, shake some sugar over, and serve

title: Ishhenbe Chbrbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of well-cleaned and uncooked bullock's tripe
- two pints of water
- salt
- broth
- yolks of two or three eggs

## Directions

1. Get three pounds of well-cleaned and uncooked bullock's tripe
2. Put it into a stewpan with two pints of water, sufficient salt
3. Put it on the fire
4. When boiling, skim it well, and let it simmer until tender
5. Take it off and skim off the fat
6. Take out the tripe, and chop it up very fine
7. Put it again in the pan with what liquor is left, which you have previously passed through a sieve
8. Add one or two pints of broth, set it again on the fire, and let it simmer for ten minutes or so
9. Add the yolks of two or three eggs prepared as in the Terbiyeli Jigher Chbrbassi recipe, and serve

title: Ishhenbe Chorbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of well-cleaned and uncooked bullock's tripe
- two pints of water
- sufficient salt
- broth
- yolks of two or three eggs

## Directions

1. Get three pounds of well-cleaned and uncooked bullock's tripe
2. Put it into a stewpan with two pints of water, sufficient salt
3. Put it on the fire
4. When boiling, skim it well, and let it simmer until tender
5. Take it off and skim off the fat
6. Take out the tripe, and chop it up very fine
7. Put it again in the pan with what liquor is left, which you have previously passed through a sieve
8. Add one or two pints of broth, set it again on the fire, and let it simmer for ten minutes or so
9. Add the yolks of two or three eggs prepared as in Terbiyeli Jigher Chorbassi, and serve

title: Isshayriyye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of fresh butter
- a pound and a half of rice-flour
- three-quarters of a pound of almonds, skinned and well pounded
- a pint of boiling milk
- two pints of boiling syrup
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Put on the fire a stewpan with a pound of fresh butter
2. When melted, add gradually a pound and a half of rice-flour, with three-quarters of a pound of almonds, skinned and well pounded, and keep stirring until it becomes a gold colour
3. Then immediately add a pint of boiling milk, with two pints of boiling syrup, which you have previously prepared of loaf sugar and water, as in No. 159
4. Instantly put the cover over, and let it remain for five minutes
5. Then stir it well with a fork, dish it up in moulds the size of a small teacup, letting it bear the impression of the mould, and serve with some white sugar sifted over

title: Isshaìriyye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of fresh butter
- a pound and a half of rice-flour
- three-quarters of a pound of almonds, skinned and well pounded
- a pint of boiling milk
- two pints of boiling syrup
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Put on the fire a stewpan with a pound of fresh butter
2. When melted, add gradually a pound and a half of rice-flour, with three-quarters of a pound of almonds, skinned and well pounded, and keep stirring until it becomes a gold colour
3. Immediately add a pint of boiling milk, with two pints of boiling syrup, which you have previously prepared of loaf sugar and water, as in No. 159
4. Instantly put the cover over, and let it remain for five minutes
5. Stir it well with a fork, dish it up in moulds the size of a small teacup, letting it bear the impression of the mould, and serve with some white sugar sifted over

title: Istridiye Kyulbasstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- salt
- pepper
- olive oil
- lemon

## Directions

1. Open as many oysters as are wanted, lay them on a gridiron
2. Sprinkle with your fingers a little salt and pepper over each one, with a few drops of olive oil
3. Set the gridiron on a charcoal fire
4. When the oysters are nicely broiled and begin to stick to the shells, dish them up in their shells
5. Serve with lemon

title: Istridiye Pulalcissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- three or four table spoonfuls of olive oil
- three or four spoonfuls of chopped parsley
- one or two cups of water
- yolks of two or three eggs
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Open as many oysters as are wanted, take them out of the shells, and put them in a basin
2. Put three or four table spoonfuls of olive oil in a saucepan, set it on the fire
3. When hot, place three or four spoonfuls of chopped parsley, and the oysters over
4. Add one or two cups of water, and let it boil for ten minutes or so
5. Beat up the yolks of two or three eggs in a saucepan, with three or four tablespoonfuls of the liquor of the oysters and the juice of a lemon
6. Put it on a slow fire, and keep stirring with a wooden spoon until the egg begins to thicken
7. Dish the oysters up, and pour this sauce over, and serve

title: Istridiye Pulalcissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- three or four table spoonfuls of olive oil
- three or four spoonfuls of chopped parsley
- one or two cups of water
- yolks of two or three eggs
- three or four tablespoonfuls of oyster liquor
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Open as many oysters as are wanted, take them out of the shells, and put them in a basin
2. Put three or four table spoonfuls of olive oil in a saucepan, set it on the fire
3. When hot, place three or four spoonfuls of chopped parsley, and the oysters over
4. Add one or two cups of water, and let it boil for ten minutes or so
5. Beat up the yolks of two or three eggs in a saucepan, with three or four tablespoonfuls of the liquor of the oysters and the juice of a lemon
6. Put it on a slow fire, and keep stirring with a wooden spoon until the egg begins to thicken
7. Dish the oysters up, and pour this sauce over, and serve

title: Izmariâ Tâivâssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- smelts
- salt
- flour
- egg
- olive oil
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take out the insides of two or three dozens of smelts, wash and sprinkle them with a little salt
2. Dip them in flour, and then in egg
3. Fry them a nice colour in hot olive oil
4. When done, dish up tastefully, squeeze a lemon over, and serve hot

title: Izmārid Tāivāssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three dozens of smelts
- salt
- flour
- egg
- hot olive oil
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take out the insides of two or three dozens of smelts, wash and sprinkle them with a little salt.
2. Dip them in flour, and then in egg.
3. Fry them a nice colour in hot olive oil.
4. When done, dish up tastefully, squeeze a lemon over, and serve hot.

title: Jajik
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers, small
- salt
- garlic
- curd
- water
- ice

## Directions

1. Peel and cut in thin small dice one or two small cucumbers
2. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt, and toss them up
3. Pound two or three cloves of garlic, and put them in a bowl
4. Add one or two pints of the best curd
5. Beat it up with water until you bring it to the thickness of thin cream
6. Put the cucumbers in, with a few pieces of ice
7. Stir it, and serve

title: Jajik
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- garlic
- curd
- ice water

## Directions

1. Peel and cut in thin small dice one or two small cucumbers
2. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt and toss them up
3. Pound two or three cloves of garlic and put them in a bowl
4. Add one or two pints of the best curd and beat it up with water until you bring it to the thickness of thin cream
5. Put the cucumbers in with a few pieces of ice
6. Stir it and serve

title: Jigher Kebdbi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's or lamb's liver
- caul-fat
- garlic
- wine vinegar
- broth
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted, sheep's or lamb's liver in dice, an inch and a half square
2. Pass them on skewers, and wrap each one with a thin piece of caul-fat
3. Place them before a moderate fire, and turn them slowly until the liver is nicely roasted
4. When done, dish it up
5. Well pound two or three skinned cloves of garlic in a mortar
6. Add half a teacup of wine vinegar, two of broth, and a little salt and pepper
7. Stir it well with a wooden spoon, and pour it over the liver
8. Put the cover on the dish, and place it in the oven, or on a charcoal fire covered with ash for five minutes or so, and then serve

title: Jigher Kidbabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's or lamb's liver
- thin caul-fat
- two or three skinned cloves of garlic
- half a teacup of wine vinegar
- two teacups of broth
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted, sheep's or lamb's liver in dice, an inch and a half square
2. Pass them on skewers, and wrap each one with a thin piece of caul-fat
3. Place them before a moderate fire, and turn them slowly until the liver is nicely roasted
4. When done, dish it up
5. Well pound two or three skinned cloves of garlic in a mortar, add half a teacup of wine vinegar, two of broth, and a little salt and pepper
6. Stir it well with a wooden spoon, and pour it over the liver
7. Put the cover on the dish, and place it in the oven, or on a charcoal fire covered with ash for five minutes or so, and then serve

title: Jigher-Tâvâssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's or lamb's liver
- salt
- flour
- hot butter
- parsley
- onions

## Directions

1. Get a sheep's or lamb's liver, wash, and cut it into large dice
2. Sprinkle a little salt over
3. Dip them in flour, and fry them a nice brown in hot butter
4. When done, take them out and let them drain
5. Dish up, sprinkle a handful or two of chopped parsley mixed with finely-sliced onions over, and serve very hot

title: Jigher-Tāwassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's or lamb's liver
- salt
- flour
- hot butter
- handful or two of chopped parsley
- finely-sliced onions

## Directions

1. Get a sheep's or lamb's liver, wash, and cut it into large dice, sprinkle a little salt over.
2. Dip them in flour, and fry them a nice brown in hot butter.
3. When done, take them out and let them drain.
4. Dish up, sprinkle a handful or two of chopped parsley mixed with finely-sliced onions over, and serve very hot.

title: Jizleme
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of the best flour
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- a very small piece of German yeast
- water
- clotted cream
- powdered sugar
- three-quarters of a pound of sugar
- one pint of water
- three ounces of fresh butter
- rose water

## Directions

1. Put in a basin or pan a pound of the best flour, half a teaspoonful of salt, and a very small piece of German yeast, which mix with water to form a nice crumpet dough.
2. Keep it covered in a warm place until it has nicely risen.
3. Pour a portion of it on a stove sufficient to bake a crumpet the size of a dinner plate, and as thick as a crown-piece.
4. When you have baked five or six, lay them in a rather deep and circular baking-tin, one over the other, and between each one a thin layer of clotted cream and powdered sugar over.
5. Dissolve in a saucepan three-quarters of a pound of sugar with a pint of water, add three ounces of fresh butter, set it on the fire, and commence stirring until the butter is melted.
6. Pour it over the crumpets, and put the baking-tin on a very slow charcoal fire, or in the oven, for ten or fifteen minutes.
7. Dish it up carefully, sprinkle a little rose water over, and serve.

title: Kabak-Basstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of mutton or lamb
- salt
- water
- three or four sliced onions
- fresh butter
- four or five vegetable marrows
- juice of one or two lemons
- chopped mint
- pepper or cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of mutton or lamb in pieces about the size of walnuts
2. Put them in a stewpan with a little salt, and cover them with water
3. Set it on a charcoal fire
4. When beginning to scum, skim it well, and let it simmer till the meat is nearly tender
5. Put three or four sliced onions in a fryingpan with sufficient fresh butter, and fry them partly brown
6. Peel four or five vegetable marrows, cut each one in four crossways, take out the insides, if seeded, and slice them three-quarters of an inch thick
7. Lay some of the pieces of meat on the bottom of the stewpan; then add some slices of vegetable marrow, with part of the fried onions, then meat, then pieces of vegetable marrow and onions, then meat, &c., until the whole of them are laid
8. Add the liquor in which the pieces of mutton were boiled, and sufficient salt; if the liquor is not enough, add some of the broth
9. Set the stewpan on the fire, and let it simmer until the vegetable marrow is tender
10. Add the juice of one or two lemons, with a little chopped mint
11. Let it simmer for two minutes longer, and then take it off
12. Dish it up, sprinkle a little pepper or cinnamon over, and serve hot

title: Kadin-Budu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- two or three chopped onions
- handful or two of rice
- salt
- pepper
- four or five eggs
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Mince very fine as much as is required of a leg of mutton, add two or three chopped onions, with a handful or two of rice, well washed, sufficient salt and pepper
2. knead well, and make it into shapes, as the fleshy part of a leg of capon
3. throw them in boiling water
4. When nearly cooked, take them out, and let them remain till cold
5. beat up four or five eggs, dip the pieces of meat in it, and fry them in hot fresh butter till a nice colour
6. When done, take them out, and let them drain
7. Dish up tastefully and serve hot

title: Kaiyssi Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of apricots
- half a pint of water
- a pound and a quarter of powdered sugar
- two or three pints of water
- a few drops of the essence of almonds or other essence
- a few pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Pick out the large apricots and put the small ones in a saucepan, with half a pint of water or a little more
2. Set it to boil until the stones come out
3. Pass them through a cloth into a basin, and well squeeze the juice from them
4. Put the juice in the pan with a pound and a quarter of powdered sugar
5. Set it to boil for a minute, and pass it again through the cloth into the basin
6. Put the large ones in a saucepan, with two or three pints of water, and boil them until they begin to crack
7. Take it off and let it cool
8. Pass the liquor through a sieve into a glass bowl
9. According to taste, sweeten it with the syrup which you have prepared
10. Add the apricots, with a few drops of the essence of almonds or any other essence you fancy, and a few pieces of ice, and serve

title: Kalkān-Bālighi Tāwāssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- salt
- five or six lemons
- three-quarters of a pint of salt water
- half an ounce of saffron
- oil or fresh butter

## Directions

1. Get a turbot as large as is required, cut the fins off, wash and sprinkle it with sufficient salt.
2. Cut in slices five or six lemons, lay half of them on the bottom of a convenient dish, place the fish over, and cover it with the other half of the lemons.
3. Pour on it three-quarters of a pint of salt water coloured with half an ounce of saffron.
4. Place a plate or dish over, so as to press the fish slightly, and let it remain for one night.
5. The next day fry it a nice brown either in oil or fresh butter, and serve.

title: Kapali Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- fresh butter
- eggs
- minced raw lamb or mutton
- onions
- salt
- almonds
- pistachios
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Roll out as thin as a wafer twenty pieces of paste prepared with fresh butter and eggs
2. Well butter the inside of a shallow and dome-like mould
3. Lay carefully ten of the pastes one over the other, which must reach up the brim of the mould
4. Place in a rather deep fryingpan some finely-minced raw lamb or mutton, two or three onions chopped fine, a little salt and a little fresh butter
5. Set it on the fire, and stew them partly brown
6. Add one or two ounces of almonds skinned, the same of pistachios, a little pepper and cinnamon
7. Mix them well together with a wooden spoon
8. Fill up the mould with this mixture
9. Cover the top with the remaining ten pieces of paste
10. Butter the bottom of a baking-tin, in which place the mould upside down
11. Put it in a hot oven, and bake it a nice brown
12. When done, turn it out of the mould on to a dish, and serve

title: Kapali Bureglii
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- paste prepared with fresh butter and eggs
- fresh butter
- finely-minced raw lamb or mutton
- onions
- salt
- almonds
- pistachios
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Roll out as thin as a wafer twenty pieces of paste prepared with fresh butter and eggs
2. Well butter the inside of a shallow and dome-like mould
3. Lay carefully ten of the pastes one over the other, which must reach up the brim of the mould
4. Place in a rather deep fryingpan some finely-minced raw lamb or mutton, two or three onions chopped fine, a little salt and a little fresh butter
5. Set it on the fire, and stew them partly brown
6. Add one or two ounces of almonds skinned, the same of pistachios, a little pepper and cinnamon
7. Mix them well together with a wooden spoon
8. Fill up the mould with this mixture, cover the top with the remaining ten pieces of paste
9. Butter the bottom of a baking-tin, in which place the mould upside down
10. Put it in a hot oven, and bake it a nice brown
11. When done, turn it out of the mould on to a dish, and serve

title: Kawun Bahlawassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- fifteen or twenty pieces of paste (No. 135)
- scalded fresh butter
- small musk-melon or any other sweet melon
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Butter a baking-tin, in which lay fifteen or twenty pieces of paste (No. 135), sprinkling scalded fresh butter between each one
2. Peel a small musk-melon, or any other sweet melon, cut it in two, take out the seeds, mash one half or the whole of the melon with a wooden spoon
3. Lay it all over the paste half an inch thick
4. Cover it with fifteen or twenty pieces of paste as before
5. Bake it a nice colour
6. When done, and while hot, shake some powdered sugar over, and serve

title: Kawun Bahlawdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- fifteen or twenty pieces of paste (No. 135)
- scalded fresh butter
- small musk-melon or sweet melon
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Butter a baking-tin, in which lay fifteen or twenty pieces of paste (No. 135), sprinkling scalded fresh butter between each one
2. Peel a small musk-melon or any other sweet melon, cut it in two, take out the seeds
3. Mash one half or the whole of the melon with a wooden spoon
4. Lay it all over the paste half an inch thick
5. Cover it with fifteen or twenty pieces of paste as before
6. Bake it a nice colour
7. When done, and while hot, shake some powdered sugar over, and serve

title: Kawurmali-Badinjan
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- egg-plants
- salt
- pepper
- half a pint of broth
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut mutton in pieces and fry them
2. Bake three or four good-sized egg-plants in an oven
3. When soft, peel them
4. Lay them in a pan, over which place the pieces of meat with the fat that is left from it in the stewpan
5. Add half a pint of broth
6. Put the pan on a slow charcoal fire with the cover over and let it simmer for twenty minutes
7. Dish up carefully, sprinkle a little cinnamon and pepper over, and serve while hot

title: Kaymak Baklawassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two pounds of clotted cream
- forty or fifty pieces of paste as No. 135
- scalded fresh butter
- white of four or five eggs

## Directions

1. Procure one or two pounds of clotted cream
2. Prepare forty or fifty pieces of paste as No. 135, and lay half of them in a baking-tin, sprinkling a little scalded fresh butter between each piece
3. Put in a basin the white of four or five eggs
4. Divide the cream in pieces, rub them with the white of eggs both under and over with a quill or a paste-brush, and lay them all over the paste
5. Cover them with the remaining pieces of paste, sprinkling butter between each as before
6. Cut it in diamonds, or any other shape you fancy
7. Bake it a delicate colour, then pour some syrup over and serve

title: Kcidin Ghyubeghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- about two pounds and three-quarters of the best flour
- one teaspoonful of salt
- water
- one teaspoonful of carbonate of soda
- syrup (No. 135)

## Directions

1. Put in a stewpan three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter, and set it on the fire
2. When melted, mix in by degrees about two pounds and three-quarters of the best flour, and keep stirring until the flour is partly browned
3. Take it out, and place it on a pastry slab, make a hole in the centre, in which put a teaspoonful of salt
4. Mix it with water (in which a teaspoonful of carbonate of soda is dissolved previously) into a softish paste with your hands
5. Form it into small cakes, and make a dent in the centre of each one with the point of your finger
6. Place them in a buttered tin, and bake them a nice colour
7. Have ready some syrup as in No. 135
8. When the cakes are nicely done, pour it over
9. Put it again in the oven, or on a slow charcoal fire until the syrup is absorbed
10. Dish up tastefully, and serve

title: Kdiyssi Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of apricots
- half a pint of water
- a pound and a quarter of powdered sugar
- two or three pints of water
- a few drops of the essence of almonds or other essence
- a few pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Pick out the large apricots and put the small ones in a saucepan with half a pint of water or a little more
2. Set it to boil until the stones come out
3. Pass them through a cloth into a basin and well squeeze the juice from them
4. Put the juice in the pan with a pound and a quarter of powdered sugar
5. Set it to boil for a minute and pass it again through the cloth into the basin
6. Put the large apricots in a saucepan with two or three pints of water and boil them until they begin to crack
7. Take it off and let it cool
8. Pass the liquor through a sieve into a glass bowl
9. Sweeten it with the syrup which you have prepared, according to taste
10. Add the apricots, with a few drops of the essence of almonds or any other essence you fancy, and a few pieces of ice
11. Serve

title: Kdymctkli Kaddyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- butter
- clotted cream
- syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare four pounds of vermicelli as No. 149
2. Lay half of it in a buttered baking-tin, and the other half in another buttered tin
3. Pour in each about half a pound of scalded fresh butter
4. Bake them a delicate colour on both sides
5. When cooked, strain and wash out all the butter from both
6. Lay some clotted cream over one, half an inch thick
7. Cover it with the other one
8. Pour hot syrup all over as No. 149
9. Put it again in the oven until the syrup is nearly absorbed
10. Cut it in any shape you fancy, or turn it on to a dish as it is, and serve

title: Kebab Kyufte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three pounds of raw leg of mutton, beef, or veal
- half a handful of parsley
- two or three onions
- crumb of a French penny roll
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- fresh butter
- two or three teacups of brown gravy
- two or three teaspoonfuls of flour
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Put on a chopping board two or three pounds of either a raw leg of mutton, or beef, or veal, half a handful of parsley cleaned, two or three onions peeled, and the crumb of a French penny roll
2. Sprinkle a little salt, pepper, and cinnamon over, and mince them very fine together until like a paste
3. Make it up in shapes of small buns, or any shape you may fancy, and fry them a nice colour in fresh butter
4. Put two or three teacups of brown gravy in a saucepan, with two or three teaspoonfuls of flour, the juice of a lemon, and a little salt
5. Set it on a slow fire, and keep stirring until it is a nice consistency
6. Dish the meat up tastefully, and pour the gravy over, and serve hot, or serve it with finely sliced onions and chopped parsley over, instead of gravy, if approved of

title: Kebab Kyufte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three pounds of raw leg of mutton, or beef, or veal
- half a handful of parsley cleaned
- two or three onions peeled
- crumb of a French penny roll
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- fresh butter
- two or three teacups of brown gravy
- two or three teaspoonfuls of flour
- juice of a lemon

## Directions

1. Put on a chopping board two or three pounds of either a raw leg of mutton, or beef, or veal, half a handful of parsley cleaned, two or three onions peeled, and the crumb of a French penny roll
2. Sprinkle a little salt, pepper, and cinnamon over, and mince them very fine together until like a paste
3. Make it up in shapes of small buns, or any shape you may fancy, and fry them a nice colour in fresh butter
4. Put two or three teacups of brown gravy in a saucepan, with two or three teaspoonfuls of flour, the juice of a lemon, and a little salt
5. Set it on a slow fire, and keep stirring until it is a nice consistency
6. Dish the meat up tastefully, and pour the gravy over, and serve hot, or serve it with finely sliced onions and chopped parsley over, instead of gravy, if approved of

title: Kechelli Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- jam
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Proceed as No. 194
2. When the omelette is nearly cooked put in the centre some jam of any sort
3. Turn the edges over, let it set a moment, then turn it over on to a dish
4. Shake some white sugar over, and serve

title: Kechelli Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five eggs
- salt
- pepper
- two ounces of fresh butter
- jam of any sort
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Proceed as No. 194
2. When the omelette is nearly cooked put in the centre some jam of any sort
3. Turn the edges over, let it set a moment, then turn it over onto a dish
4. Shake some white sugar over, and serve

title: Kefal-Balighi Pulakissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen very fresh grey mullets
- salt
- an ounce of fresh butter
- half a teacup of olive oil
- three-quarters of a pound of sliced onions
- water
- a handful of chopped parsley
- cayenne pepper
- the juice of one or two lemons

## Directions

1. Take out the gills and the insides of half a dozen very fresh grey mullets
2. Cut them in two crossways
3. Wash and sprinkle them with a little salt and let them remain for an hour
4. Put in a stewpan an ounce of fresh butter and half a teacup of olive oil
5. Add a little salt and three-quarters of a pound of sliced onions
6. Put it on the fire and let the onions stew but not brown
7. Lay the fish over and cover it with water
8. Let simmer for twenty or thirty minutes
9. When nearly done, add a handful of chopped parsley and a pinch or two of cayenne pepper
10. Add the juice of one or two lemons and let it simmer for four or five minutes longer
11. Dish it up and serve hot

title: Kefal-Balighi Pulalcissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen very fresh grey mullets
- salt
- an ounce of fresh butter
- half a teacup of olive oil
- three-quarters of a pound of sliced onions
- water
- a handful of chopped parsley
- cayenne pepper
- the juice of one or two lemons

## Directions

1. Take out the gills and the insides of half a dozen very fresh grey mullets and cut them in two crossways
2. Wash and sprinkle them with a little salt and let them remain for an hour
3. Put in a stewpan an ounce of fresh butter, half a teacup of olive oil, a little salt, and three-quarters of a pound of sliced onions
4. Put it on the fire and let the onions stew but not brown
5. Lay the fish over, cover it with water, and let simmer for twenty or thirty minutes
6. When nearly done, add a handful of chopped parsley, a pinch or two of cayenne pepper, and the juice of one or two lemons
7. Let it simmer for four or five minutes longer
8. Dish it up and serve hot

title: Khiyar Tereturu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- pound of nuts or almonds
- salt
- three or four cloves of garlic
- crumb of half of a French penny roll
- water
- half a teacup of the best wine vinegar or juice of two or three lemons
- one or two cucumbers
- white pepper
- one or two tablespoonfuls of the best olive oil

## Directions

1. Crack a pound of nuts or almonds, scald and skin them, then pound them in a stone mortar
2. Add half a teaspoonful of salt, three or four cloves of garlic peeled, and pound them well together with the nuts
3. Add the crumb of half of a French penny roll, previously soaked in water, and pound again all together till it forms a smooth substance like paste
4. Moisten it with half a teacup of the best wine vinegar, or the juice of two or three lemons, and bring it to the consistency of cream with some water, stirring it all the time with a wooden spoon
5. Peel and cut in thin slices one or two cucumbers in a dish, season with a little salt and white pepper
6. Pour the sauce over, then pour one or two tablespoonfuls of the best olive oil over by degrees, and serve

title: Khiyar Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two dozen of small cucumbers
- fennel
- salt water

## Directions

1. Get one or two dozen of small cucumbers
2. Put some fennel on the bottom of a stone jar
3. Place the cucumbers over
4. Lay some fennel on the top
5. Cover them with salt water
6. Place a piece of board over them
7. Put an iron weight or a heavy stone on the top to press it
8. Put the jar for a few days in a place where the sun is powerful, or in a warm place
9. Remove it to a cool place
10. Let it remain eight or ten days before using

title: Khunkyar Pilaví
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of the best rice
- two pounds and a half of the choice part of mutton
- four pints of water
- salt
- half a pound of fresh butter
- three pints of the liquor
- three or four tablespoonfuls of pistachios
- minced raw mutton
- one or two tablespoonfuls of currants
- half a teaspoonful of mixed spice

## Directions

1. Wash two pounds of the best rice, put it in a clean cloth, tie it, and hang it up
2. Cut two pounds and a half of the choice part of mutton in pieces about the size of walnuts, wash and place them in a stewpan with four pints of water, and put it on a charcoal fire
3. When boiling, skim it well, add sufficient salt, and let it simmer for twenty or thirty minutes
4. Drain the liquor into a basin, put half a pound of fresh butter over the pieces of meat, and fry them a nice brown
5. Add three pints of the liquor, with a little salt, three or four tablespoonfuls of pistachios, the same of minced raw mutton, previously fried a nice colour in fresh butter, one or two of currants, and half a teaspoonful of mixed spice
6. As soon as it boils, add the rice, and boil it for two or three minutes longer
7. Reduce the heat of the fire by removing the burning coals, and leaving only the ashes and the sparks, put on the pan with the cover over for about an hour
8. Dish up tastefully, and serve

title: Khunkyar Pilayi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of the best rice
- two pounds and a half of choice mutton
- four pints of water
- salt
- half a pound of fresh butter
- three pints of liquor
- three or four tablespoonfuls of pistachios
- one or two tablespoonfuls of minced raw mutton
- one or two tablespoonfuls of currants
- half a teaspoonful of mixed spice

## Directions

1. Wash two pounds of the best rice, put it in a clean cloth, tie it, and hang it up
2. Cut two pounds and a half of the choice part of mutton in pieces about the size of walnuts
3. Wash and place them in a stewpan with four pints of water, and put it on a charcoal fire
4. When boiling, skim it well, add sufficient salt, and let it simmer for twenty or thirty minutes
5. Drain the liquor into a basin, put half a pound of fresh butter over the pieces of meat, and fry them a nice brown
6. Add three pints of the liquor, with a little salt, three or four tablespoonfuls of pistachios, the same of minced raw mutton previously fried a nice colour in fresh butter, one or two of currants, and half a teaspoonful of mixed spice
7. As soon as it boils, add the rice, and boil it for two or three minutes longer
8. Reduce the heat of the fire by removing the burning coals, and leaving only the ashes and the sparks, put on the pan with the cover over for about an hour
9. Dish up tastefully, and serve

title: Khurma
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound and a quarter of the best flour
- a teaspoonful of salt
- four or five ounces of fresh butter, melted
- half a pint of water
- carbonate of soda
- fresh butter for frying
- honey or loaf sugar and water

## Directions

1. Put a pound and a quarter of the best flour on the pastry-slab, make a hole in the centre, in which put a teaspoonful of salt, four or five ounces of fresh butter, melted
2. Mix all together, then add half a pint of water, or a little more, mixed with a little carbonate of soda, and form it a softish flexible paste
3. Then roll it with hands as long as it will extend, a little thicker than your thumb, and cut it up in pieces the size of a date
4. Then roll them one by one upon a basket-lid, or a table-mat made of fine twigs, and fry them a delicate colour in boiling fresh butter
5. When done, let them drain, then throw them in boiling syrup, which you have previously prepared of honey, or loaf sugar and water, and let them remain for a few minutes
6. Then dish them up tastefully, and serve

title: Khurmd
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound and a quarter of the best flour
- a teaspoonful of salt
- four or five ounces of fresh butter, melted
- half a pint of water
- carbonate of soda
- boiling fresh butter
- syrup prepared of honey, or loaf sugar and water

## Directions

1. Put a pound and a quarter of the best flour on the pastry-slab, make a hole in the centre
2. In the hole put a teaspoonful of salt, four or five ounces of fresh butter, melted
3. Mix all together, then add half a pint of water, or a little more, mixed with a little carbonate of soda, and form it a softish flexible paste
4. Roll it with hands as long as it will extend, a little thicker than your thumb, and cut it up in pieces the size of a date
5. Roll them one by one upon a basket-lid, or a table-mat made of fine twigs
6. Fry them a delicate colour in boiling fresh butter
7. When done, let them drain, then throw them in boiling syrup, which you have previously prepared of honey, or loaf sugar and water
8. Let them remain for a few minutes, then dish them up tastefully, and serve

title: Kiltj Balighti Yahnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five pounds of swordfish
- salt
- flour
- egg
- olive oil
- one or two handfuls of picked parsley
- five or six cloves of garlic
- Cayenne pepper
- the juice of two or three lemons
- two teacups of water

## Directions

1. Procure four or five pounds of swordfish in one piece
2. Cut it in slices, wash them, and sprinkle a little salt over
3. Dip them in flour and then in egg
4. Partly fry them in olive oil
5. Spread one or two handfuls of picked parsley on the bottom of a stewpan with five or six cloves of garlic
6. Lay the slices of fish over
7. Spread the same quantity of parsley and garlic on the top
8. Add sufficient salt and two or three pinches of Cayenne pepper
9. Add the juice of two or three lemons and two teacups of water
10. Put the pan over a charcoal fire with the lid on and let it simmer gently for twenty minutes or until the liquor is nearly absorbed
11. Dish it up and serve

title: Kiltj Bhlighi Yahnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five pounds of swordfish
- salt
- flour
- egg
- olive oil
- one or two handfuls of picked parsley
- five or six cloves of garlic
- Cayenne pepper
- the juice of two or three lemons
- two teacups of water

## Directions

1. Procure four or five pounds of swordfish in one piece
2. Cut it in slices, wash them, and sprinkle a little salt over
3. Dip them in flour and then in egg
4. Partly fry them in olive oil
5. Spread one or two handfuls of picked parsley on the bottom of a stewpan with five or six cloves of garlic
6. Lay the slices of fish over and spread the same quantity of parsley and garlic on the top
7. Add sufficient salt, two or three pinches of Cayenne pepper, the juice of two or three lemons, and two teacups of water
8. Put the pan over a charcoal fire with the lid on and let it simmer gently for twenty minutes or until the liquor is nearly absorbed
9. Dish it up and serve

title: Kirma Tawuk Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three chickens
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- raw onion-juice
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Clean two or three chickens
2. Split each one with a sharp knife, and remove the whole of the bones
3. Cut the flesh in small pieces, but without separating one piece from another
4. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon
5. Rub them in with half a teacup of raw onion-juice, and let them remain for an hour
6. Pass them on skewers, and place them before a moderate fire
7. Turn them continually, and when the chickens begin to brown, baste them with fresh butter with a quill, until they are nicely cooked
8. Dish up, and serve hot

title: Kirmizi Lhaana Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three red cabbages
- salt
- peppercorns
- bunch of parsley
- mint
- thyme
- strong wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut two or three red cabbages into thin slices
2. Remove the hard stalks
3. Place them on a dish, or board
4. Cover them with salt for ten or twelve hours
5. Turn them now and then
6. Clean off all the salt
7. Lay them in a stone jar with some peppercorns, and a bunch of parsley mixed with some mint and thyme
8. Cover them with strong wine vinegar
9. Put the lid on the jar
10. Let it remain in a place for three or four weeks before using

title: Kirmizi Lhbana Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three red cabbages
- salt
- peppercorns
- parsley
- mint
- thyme
- strong wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut two or three red cabbages into thin slices
2. Remove the hard stalks
3. Place them on a dish or board
4. Cover them with salt for ten or twelve hours
5. Turn them now and then
6. Clean off all the salt
7. Lay them in a stone jar with some peppercorns and a bunch of parsley mixed with some mint and thyme
8. Cover them with strong wine vinegar
9. Put the lid on the jar
10. Let it remain in a place for three or four weeks before using

title: Kirmizi Yahni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb or mutton
- fresh butter
- three or four onions
- half or three-quarters of a pint of chick-peas

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is required of a leg of lamb or mutton in pieces, and boil as above
2. Add three or four finely-sliced onions, fried in fresh butter
3. Add half or three-quarters of a pint of chick-peas previously scalded in water
4. Cook and serve as No. 36

title: Kirmizi Yahni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb or mutton
- fresh butter
- onions
- chick-peas

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is required of a leg of lamb or mutton in pieces
2. Boil as in Beyaz Ydhmi
3. Add three or four finely-sliced onions, fried in fresh butter
4. Add half or three-quarters of a pint of chick-peas previously scalded in water
5. Cook and serve as Beyaz Ydhmi

title: Kiyma Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- about a pound of nice mutton
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- a pound of the best rice, well washed
- two pints of hot water
- salt
- cinnamon

## Directions
