1. Chop up about a pound of nice mutton
2. Put it in a saucepan with a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
3. Set it on the fire, and let the meat stew a nice brown
4. Add a pound of the best rice, well washed, two pints of hot water, sufficient salt, and a pinch or two of cinnamon
5. Let it boil slowly till there is no liquid remaining
6. Take it off and place it on the hob with the cover over for twenty or thirty minutes
7. Dish it up in a dome shape, and serve hot

title: Kiymali Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four ounces of raw mutton
- small onion
- parsley
- fresh butter
- five or six eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mince three or four ounces of raw mutton, with a small onion and a little parsley, and partly fry them in a little fresh butter
2. Break into a basin five or six eggs, and beat them up well with a fork
3. Add the minced meat with half a teaspoonful of salt and a pinch or two of pepper, and beat them up well
4. Put three or four ounces of fresh butter into the frying-pan, and put it on the fire
5. When hot, pour in the mixture, and fry a delicate colour on both sides
6. When done, turn it on to a dish carefully, and serve

title: Kiymali Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four ounces of raw mutton
- a small onion
- a little parsley
- a little fresh butter
- five or six eggs
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- a pinch or two of pepper
- three or four ounces of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Mince three or four ounces of raw mutton, with a small onion and a little parsley, and partly fry them in a little fresh butter
2. Break into a basin five or six eggs, and beat them up well with a fork
3. Add the minced meat with half a teaspoonful of salt and a pinch or two of pepper, and beat them up well
4. Put three or four ounces of fresh butter into the frying-pan, and put it on the fire
5. When hot, pour in the mixture, and fry a delicate colour on both sides
6. When done, turn it onto a dish carefully, and serve

title: Kiymbtddn Firm Kebdbi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- onions
- fresh butter
- skinned pistachios
- currants
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- caul-fat

## Directions

1. Mince three or four pounds of a leg of mutton, or any other part of raw mutton
2. Put three chopped onions in a stewpan with some fresh butter, and partly fry them
3. Add the minced mutton with half a handful of skinned pistachios, the same of currants, a little salt, pepper, and cinnamon
4. Stir round with a spoon until the minced meat is also partly fried
5. Cut a caul-fat in pieces about the size of vine leaves
6. Divide the minced meat in each piece, and wrap it up to the shape of a small cup, or any shape you may fancy
7. Lay them on a baking tin
8. Place it in a hot oven until nicely browned
9. Dish up tastefully, and serve

title: Kiymbtddn Firm Kebdbi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pounds of a leg of mutton
- three chopped onions
- fresh butter
- half a handful of skinned pistachios
- a handful of currants
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- caul-fat

## Directions

1. Mince three or four pounds of a leg of mutton, or any other part of raw mutton
2. Put three chopped onions in a stewpan with some fresh butter, and partly fry them
3. Add the minced mutton with half a handful of skinned pistachios, the same of currants, a little salt, pepper, and cinnamon
4. Stir round with a spoon until the minced meat is also partly fried
5. Cut a caul-fat in pieces about the size of vine leaves, divide the minced meat in each piece, and wrap it up to the shape of a small cup, or any shape you may fancy
6. Lay them on a baking tin, place it in a hot oven until nicely browned
7. Dish up tastefully, and serve

title: Kizartma
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg or sirloin of mutton
- salt
- water
- curd
- fresh butter
- onions

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is required of a leg or sirloin of mutton in pieces the size of large eggs
2. Place them in a saucepan with sufficient salt, and cover them with water
3. Set it on the fire, and let it boil for twenty or thirty minutes
4. Skim it well
5. Take out the pieces of meat, and rub them all over with curd
6. Fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
7. Place them in a saucepan with sufficient onions, sliced thin and fried a nice brown in fresh butter
8. Add the liquor in which the pieces of mutton were boiled
9. Put the cover over the pan, set it on a moderate charcoal fire until it forms a nice thick gravy at the bottom, and the meat is tender
10. Dish it up, and serve

title: Kol-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- eggs
- broth
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Lay on the paste-board separately as many pieces of paste as are required
2. Beat up two or three eggs in a basin, with three or four tablespoonfuls of broth
3. Dip a paste brush in it, rub it all over each piece of the paste, and fold them separately in two
4. Lay on each one lengthways any preparation you fancy, fold the two side edges over, and then the ends, thus making it an oblong shape
5. Make the others in the same shape, and fry them a nice brown on both sides in hot fresh butter
6. When all done, let them drain, then dish up on a white napkin, and serve hot

title: Kom Un-Dolmessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a musk-melon or any other melon
- raw minced mutton
- fresh butter
- two onions
- half a teacup of rice
- salt
- pepper
- a tablespoonful of currants
- two or three tablespoonfuls of almonds, skinned
- two or three tablespoonfuls of pistachios

## Directions

1. Procure a musk-melon, or any other one
2. Cut out the part where the stalk is, twice the size of a crown piece, so as to form a lid
3. Take out the seeds, and part of the inside
4. Fry a nice brown a sufficient quantity of raw minced mutton in fresh butter, with two onions chopped fine
5. Add half a teacup of rice well cleaned, sufficient salt and pepper, a tablespoonful of currants, two or three tablespoonfuls of almonds, skinned, and the same of pistachios
6. Stir three or four minutes longer, and take it off
7. Stuff the melon with this mixture
8. Put the piece over that you have previously cut out for a lid, and pin it round with a few wooden pins
9. Put it in a baking-dish, and bake it nicely in the oven
10. Dish it up, and serve

title: Kom un-Dolmetssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a musk-melon or any other melon
- raw minced mutton
- fresh butter
- two onions
- half a teacup of rice
- salt
- pepper
- a tablespoonful of currants
- two or three tablespoonfuls of almonds, skinned
- two or three tablespoonfuls of pistachios
- wooden pins

## Directions

1. Cut out the part where the stalk is, twice the size of a crown piece, so as to form a lid
2. Take out the seeds and part of the inside
3. Fry a nice brown a sufficient quantity of raw minced mutton in fresh butter with two onions chopped fine
4. Add half a teacup of rice well cleaned, sufficient salt and pepper, a tablespoonful of currants, two or three tablespoonfuls of almonds skinned, and the same of pistachios
5. Stir three or four minutes longer and take it off
6. Stuff the melon with this mixture
7. Put the piece over that you have previously cut out for a lid and pin it round with a few wooden pins
8. Put it in a baking-dish and bake it nicely in the oven
9. Dish it up and serve

title: Koyun ve Kiizu Kyul-Basstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton or lamb neck
- salt
- pepper
- sweet marjoram
- parsley
- onion

## Directions

1. Cut from the neck of mutton or lamb as many cutlets as are required
2. Remove the bone at the thick end and beat them out with a rolling pin or a small chopper
3. Sprinkle with your fingers sufficient salt and pepper, and a small quantity of sweet marjoram
4. Rub them well in the chops
5. Place them on a gridiron, set it on a moderate charcoal fire, turn them frequently with a pair of tongs
6. When nicely cooked, serve whilst hot
7. If desired, place a handful or two of parsley together with an onion, chopped fine, over the cutlets

title: Koyun ve Kuzü Kyul-Basstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton or lamb
- salt
- pepper
- sweet marjoram
- parsley
- onion

## Directions

1. Cut from the neck of mutton or lamb as many cutlets as are required, remove the bone at the thick end, and beat them out with a rolling pin or a small chopper
2. Sprinkle with your fingers sufficient salt and pepper, and a small quantity of sweet marjoram; rub them well in the chops
3. Place them on a gridiron, set it on a moderate charcoal fire, turn them frequently with a pair of tongs
4. When nicely cooked, serve whilst hot
5. If desired, place a handful or two of parsley together with an onion, chopped fine, over the cutlets

title: Kuru Armud Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two pounds of dried pears
- two or three pints of boiling water
- powdered sugar
- a few cloves
- a few small pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Wash one or two pounds of dried pears
2. Cut each one in four crossways and remove the cores
3. Put them in an earthen pan and pour two or three pints of boiling water over
4. Cover the pan and leave it for one night
5. Place them in a stewpan with the liquid
6. Add some powdered sugar with a few cloves according to palate
7. Set it to boil for a few minutes and take it off
8. Let it cool and pass the liquid into a glass bowl through a sieve
9. Put in the pears with a few small pieces of ice
10. Serve

title: Kuru Silkme
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pounds of mutton
- salt
- pepper
- three or four ounces of fresh butter
- three or four egg-plants

## Directions

1. Cut three or four pounds of mutton into small pieces, wash and dry them in a cloth, then place them in a stewpan with sufficient salt and pepper
2. Set it on the fire, and stir with a wooden spoon until it begins to hiss, which indicates that all the moisture is dispersed
3. Add three or four ounces of fresh butter, and keep stirring till the meat is nicely browned
4. When done, take them out with a hand-strainer, and place them in a dish; keep it hot
5. Cut three or four egg-plants in thin slices, sprinkle them with salt, and let them remain for a few minutes
6. When the bitter juice is drawn from them, fry them a nice colour on both sides in the remaining fat from the meat, then arrange them tastefully over the meat, or round the dish, sprinkle a little pepper over, and serve hot

title: Kuru Silkme
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pounds of mutton
- sufficient salt and pepper
- three or four ounces of fresh butter
- three or four egg-plants
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut three or four pounds of mutton into small pieces
2. Wash and dry them in a cloth, then place them in a stewpan with sufficient salt and pepper
3. Set it on the fire, and stir with a wooden spoon until it begins to hiss, which indicates that all the moisture is dispersed
4. Add three or four ounces of fresh butter, and keep stirring till the meat is nicely browned
5. When done, take them out with a hand-strainer, and place them in a dish
6. Keep it hot
7. Cut three or four egg-plants in thin slices, sprinkle them with salt, and let them remain for a few minutes
8. When the bitter juice is drawn from them, fry them a nice colour on both sides in the remaining fat from the meat
9. Arrange them tastefully over the meat, or round the dish, sprinkle a little pepper over, and serve hot

title: Kush Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton or lamb
- raw onion-juice
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- cinnamon
- cummin

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of mutton or lamb in pieces the size of walnuts
2. Pour a small teacup of raw onion-juice over, mixed with sufficient salt and pepper
3. Rub it well in, and let them remain for about two hours
4. Pass them on the skewers one by one, place them in front of a moderate fire, and turn them continually
5. As soon as the meat begins to get brown, and the gravy drops, sprinkle a little flour over
6. When the meat is thoroughly done, sprinkle a little cinnamon and cummin over, and serve hot

title: Kuzu Kizdrtmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg or any other part of lamb
- curd
- fresh butter
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- water
- lamb liver
- lamb heart
- pistachios
- currants
- mixed spice
- broth
- rice

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of a leg or any other part of lamb in pieces the size of kidneys
2. Dip each piece in curd, and fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
3. Place them in a stewpan with two or three sliced onions fried in butter, sufficient salt and pepper
4. Cover them with hot or cold water, set it on a charcoal fire, and let it simmer gently until it forms a nice thick gravy at the bottom of the stewpan
5. While the meat is cooking, chop up the liver and the heart of a lamb together, then fry it in fresh butter with a little salt
6. When nicely done, add a handful of pistachios skinned, a tablespoonful of currants, and a little mixed spice
7. Stir it with a wooden spoon for five minutes or so
8. Place it in a saucepan, with some of the gravy of the lamb, and two pints of broth
9. Set it on a charcoal fire with the cover over
10. When boiling, add a pound of rice well washed, stir it, put the cover over, and let it boil until each grain is rather soft, but separate
11. Take it off, wrap round the lid a wet cloth, to prevent the steam from escaping, and put it by the side of the fire for twenty minutes or so
12. Dish it up in the form of a dome, arrange the pieces of lamb over tastefully, and serve hot

title: Káymák Báklawássi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two pounds of clotted cream
- paste as No. 135
- scalded fresh butter
- white of four or five eggs

## Directions

1. Procure one or two pounds of clotted cream
2. Prepare forty or fifty pieces of paste as No. 135, and lay half of them in a baking-tin, sprinkling a little scalded fresh butter between each piece
3. Put in a basin the white of four or five eggs, divide the cream in pieces, rub them with the white of eggs both under and over with a quill or a paste-brush, and lay them all over the paste
4. Cover them with the remaining pieces of paste, sprinkling butter between each as before
5. Cut it in diamonds, or any other shape you fancy
6. Bake it a delicate colour, then pour some syrup over as above, and serve

title: Kâlkân-Bâlighi Tâvâssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- salt
- lemons
- salt water
- saffron
- oil or fresh butter

## Directions

1. Get a turbot as large as is required, cut the fins off, wash and sprinkle it with sufficient salt
2. Cut in slices five or six lemons, lay half of them on the bottom of a convenient dish
3. Place the fish over, and cover it with the other half of the lemons
4. Pour on it three-quarters of a pint of salt water coloured with half an ounce of saffron
5. Place a plate or dish over, so as to press the fish slightly, and let it remain for one night
6. The next day fry it a nice brown either in oil or fresh butter, and serve

title: Kéymekli Kaddyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- butter
- clotted cream
- syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare four pounds of vermicelli as No. 149
2. Lay half of it in a buttered baking-tin, and the other half in another buttered tin
3. Pour in each about half a pound of scalded fresh butter
4. Bake them a delicate colour on both sides
5. When cooked, strain and wash out all the butter from both
6. Lay some clotted cream over one, half an inch thick
7. Cover it with the other one
8. Pour hot syrup all over, as No. 149
9. Put it again in the oven until the syrup is nearly absorbed
10. Cut it in any shape you fancy, or turn it on to a dish as it is, and serve

title: Kıymalı Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of nice mutton
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- a pound of the best rice, well washed
- two pints of hot water
- sufficient salt
- a pinch or two of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Chop up about a pound of nice mutton, put it in a saucepan with a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, set it on the fire, and let the meat stew a nice brown
2. Add a pound of the best rice, well washed, two pints of hot water, sufficient salt, and a pinch or two of cinnamon, and let it boil slowly till there is no liquid remaining
3. Take it off and place it on the hob with the cover over for twenty or thirty minutes
4. Dish it up in a dome shape, and serve hot

title: Lacum
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- one pound and six ounces of the best flour
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- about ten eggs
- jam or any other jam, clotted cream, or minced meat

## Directions

1. Put three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter in a saucepan, and set it on the fire
2. When hot, add one pound and six ounces of the best flour, and keep stirring till it becomes a light brown
3. Pour three-quarters of a pint of water over, and continue stirring until it becomes like a paste
4. Take it off and let it remain till cold
5. Add about ten eggs, and work it with your hands to form a softish paste
6. Divide it in round pieces the size of small peaches, hollow the centre of each with the point of your finger
7. Lay them in a buttered baking-tin, and bake them a nice delicate colour
8. When done, fill them with the jam, or any other jam, clotted cream, or minced meat, previously stewed brown in fresh butter
9. Dish them up tastefully on a white napkin, and serve

title: Latin Chicheghi Salatassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- nasturtium flowers
- chervil, chopped
- salt
- olive oil
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put a plate of flowers of the nasturtium in a salad-bowl, with a tablespoonful of chopped chervil
2. Sprinkle over with your fingers half a teaspoonful of salt, two or three tablespoonfuls of olive oil, and the juice of a lemon
3. Turn the salad in the bowl with a spoon and fork till well mixed, and serve

title: Latin Chicheghi Salatası
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- nasturtium flowers
- chervil
- salt
- olive oil
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put a plate of flowers of the nasturtium in a salad-bowl with a tablespoonful of chopped chervil
2. Sprinkle over with your fingers half a teaspoonful of salt and two or three tablespoonfuls of olive oil and the juice of a lemon
3. Turn the salad in the bowl with a spoon and fork till well mixed and serve

title: Ldmunya Kdymaghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of strawberries or raspberries
- three or four ounces of powdered sugar
- two or three tablespoonfuls of water
- an ounce and a half of melted isinglass
- half a pint of the fruit

## Directions

1. Take off the stalks of a pound of strawberries or raspberries, wash and put them in a stewpan.
2. Add three or four ounces of powdered sugar, and two or three tablespoonfuls of water.
3. Put the pan on a moderate charcoal fire, and boil till rather thick, stirring it at the same time with a wooden spoon.
4. When cold, pass it through a sieve into a basin.
5. Add an ounce and a half of melted isinglass to half a pint of the fruit, and stir it well.

title: Lemonyd Kdymaghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of strawberries or raspberries
- three or four ounces of powdered sugar
- two or three tablespoonfuls of water
- an ounce and a half of melted isinglass
- half a pint of the fruit
- a pint of whipped cream
- a few drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower

## Directions

1. Take off the stalks of the strawberries or raspberries, wash and put them in a stewpan
2. Put the pan on a moderate charcoal fire, and boil till rather thick, stirring it at the same time with a wooden spoon
3. When cold, pass it through a sieve into a basin
4. Add an ounce and a half of melted isinglass to half a pint of the fruit, and stir it well
5. Add a pint of whipped cream, with a few drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower
6. Mix well all together, then pour it into a mould, and place it on ice, or in a cool place, until firm
7. When ready, turn it out and serve

title: Lemunyd Kdymaghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of strawberries or raspberries
- three or four ounces of powdered sugar
- two or three tablespoonfuls of water
- an ounce and a half of melted isinglass
- half a pint of the fruit
- a pint of whipped cream
- a few drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower

## Directions

1. Take off the stalks of a pound of strawberries or raspberries, wash and put them in a stewpan, with three or four ounces of powdered sugar, and two or three tablespoonfuls of water
2. Put the pan on a moderate charcoal fire, and boil till rather thick, stirring it at the same time with a wooden spoon
3. When cold, pass it through a sieve into a basin, add an ounce and a half of melted isinglass to half a pint of the fruit, and stir it well
4. Add a pint of whipped cream, with a few drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower; mix well all together
5. Pour it into a mould, and place it on ice, or in a cool place, until firm
6. When ready, turn it out and serve

title: Lharana Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four nice white-heart cabbages
- yeast
- half a dozen of ripe capsicums
- brine

## Directions

1. Cut the stalks off
2. Remove the outer leaves of three or four nice white-heart cabbages
3. Cut them in four crosswise
4. Put a little yeast on the bottom of a convenient stone jar
5. Place the cabbages over
6. Add half a dozen of ripe capsicums, chopped fine
7. Cover them with brine
8. Put the lid over the jar
9. Place it in the pantry
10. Pour the brine in the jar to and fro every five days
11. When the cabbage is turned acid, it is then fit for use

title: Lhirina Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four nice white-heart cabbages
- yeast
- half a dozen ripe capsicums
- brine

## Directions

1. Cut the stalks off the cabbages
2. Remove the outer leaves
3. Cut them in four crosswise
4. Put a little yeast on the bottom of a convenient stone jar
5. Place the cabbages over
6. Add half a dozen ripe capsicums, chopped fine
7. Cover them with brine
8. Put the lid over the jar
9. Place it in the pantry
10. Pour the brine in the jar to and fro every five days
11. When the cabbage is turned acid, it is then fit for use

title: Limdn Elmetsiyyessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- about an ounce of isinglass
- a small tumbler of water
- five lemons
- half a pound of loaf sugar
- one pint of water
- three wine-glasses of water
- two tablespoonfuls of rose-water

## Directions

1. Dissolve about an ounce of isinglass in a small tumbler of water.
2. Boil and reduce it to half, then pass it through a cloth into a basin.
3. Cut in two five lemons, take off the rind of one of them and remove the pith, place them in a basin, and squeeze the juice of the lemons into it.
4. Put half a pound of loaf sugar in a stewpan with a pint of water, set it to boil until it becomes a syrup.
5. Take it off and add the juice and the rind of the lemons.
6. Put the stewpan again on the fire with the cover over.
7. As soon as it begins to boil, skim it well, and add three wine-glasses of water by degrees.
8. Let it boil one or two minutes longer, then add the isinglass.
9. Pass it through a jelly-bag, if fancied, add two tablespoonfuls of rose-water.
10. Pour it into a mould, and place it on ice.
11. When ready, dip the mould in warm water and turn it out.
12. If summer, add a little more isinglass.

title: Limdn Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of powdered sugar
- white of an egg
- two pints of water
- three-quarters of a pint of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Put three pounds of powdered sugar in a stewpan, with the white of an egg well beaten up, and two pints of water
2. Stir well and set it on the fire
3. When boiling, skim it well
4. As soon as it begins to thicken add three-quarters of a pint of lemon-juice, and let it boil for half a minute longer, then take it off
5. When nearly cold, pass it through a clean cloth, bottle, and cork it up till wanted

title: Limun Elmetsiyyessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- about an ounce of isinglass
- water
- five lemons
- half a pound of loaf sugar
- a pint of water
- three wine-glasses of water
- two tablespoonfuls of rose-water

## Directions

1. Dissolve about an ounce of isinglass in a small tumbler of water, boil and reduce it to half, then pass it through a cloth into a basin
2. Cut in two five lemons, take off the rind of one of them and remove the pith, place them in a basin, and squeeze the juice of the lemons into it
3. Put half a pound of loaf sugar in a stewpan with a pint of water, set it to boil until it becomes a syrup, then take it off and add the juice and the rind of the lemons
4. Put the stewpan again on the fire with the cover over
5. As soon as it begins to boil, skim it well, and add three wine-glasses of water by degrees, which will assist its clarification
6. Let it boil one or two minutes longer, then add the isinglass, pass it through a jelly-bag if fancied, add two tablespoonfuls of rose-water, and then pour it into a mould, and place it on ice
7. When ready, dip the mould in warm water and turn it out

title: Limón Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of powdered sugar
- white of an egg
- two pints of water
- three-quarters of a pint of lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Put three pounds of powdered sugar in a stewpan, with the white of an egg well beaten up, and two pints of water
2. Stir well and set it on the fire
3. When boiling, skim it well, and as soon as it begins to thicken add three-quarters of a pint of lemon-juice, and let it boil for half a minute longer
4. Take it off
5. When nearly cold, pass it through a clean cloth, bottle, and cork it up till wanted

title: Lâlcum
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- one pound and six ounces of the best flour
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- about ten eggs
- jam or clotted cream or minced meat

## Directions

1. Put three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter in a saucepan, and set it on the fire
2. When hot, add one pound and six ounces of the best flour, and keep stirring till it becomes a light brown
3. Pour three-quarters of a pint of water over, and continue stirring until it becomes like a paste
4. Take it off and let it remain till cold
5. Add about ten eggs, and work it with your hands to form a softish paste
6. Divide it in round pieces the size of small peaches, hollow the centre of each with the point of your finger
7. Lay them in a buttered baking-tin, and bake them a nice delicate colour
8. When done, fill them with jam or any other jam, clotted cream, or minced meat, previously stewed brown in fresh butter
9. Dish them up tastefully on a white napkin, and serve

title: Makarna-Chbrbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- broth
- macaroni
- white pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make exactly as Shariys Chbrbassi, only it takes two or three times longer than the vermicelli

title: Makarna-Chorbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- broth
- macaroni
- white pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make macaroni soup exactly as Shariys Chorbassi
2. It takes two or three times longer than the vermicelli

title: Makhlut Turshu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen gherkins
- a dozen vegetable marrows the size of your finger
- a dozen melons the size of walnuts
- a dozen water-melons
- a dozen pumpkins or gourds
- a dozen and a half button onions
- best wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Procure a dozen gherkins, the same of vegetable marrows the size of your finger, ditto of melons the size of walnuts, ditto of water-melons, ditto of pumpkins or gourds, and a dozen and a half of button onions
2. Scald and drain them
3. Prick each one here and there with a wooden needle
4. Arrange them in a stone jar
5. Cover them with the best wine vinegar
6. Let it remain covered for ten or fifteen days before using
7. If it is to be kept for any length of time, change the vinegar every five days

title: Marmarina
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a sufficient quantity of spinach
- fresh butter
- four or five very thin round pieces of puff-paste
- three or four eggs
- half a pound of nice cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Well clean a sufficient quantity of spinach, pick off the stalks, boil it for one or two minutes, let it drain, and chop it up very fine
2. Well butter a rather deep and circular baking-tin, in which lay four or five very thin round pieces of puff-paste, one over the other
3. Sprinkle a little fresh butter over with a paste-brush, and lay the spinach all over
4. Break into a basin three or four eggs, to which add half a pound of nice cheese, grated; beat up well together, and lay it over the spinach
5. Put it in a rather hot oven, and bake it a nice colour
6. When done, cut it in four crossways, and serve hot

title: Marul Salatassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuces
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks, hard boiled
- wine vinegar or lemon-juice
- olive oil
- chervil, chopped
- tarragon
- young onions
- beet-root

## Directions

1. Get two or three lettuces
2. Remove the faded and all the coarse green leaves, and cut the green top off
3. Pull the leaves off one by one, and cut them in three or four pieces, according to the size of each leaf
4. Well wash and drain them in a cloth
5. Put in a salad-bowl half a teaspoonful of salt, one or two pinches of pepper
6. Add the yolk of an egg or two, hard boiled, then bruise them to a pulp with the back of a wooden spoon
7. Add half a tablespoonful of the best wine vinegar or lemon-juice and stir it round
8. Pour a tablespoonful of olive oil over by degrees, and keep stirring
9. Add a spoonful more of vinegar, and two of oil, and beat them well up
10. When as smooth as cream, add the lettuce, with a tablespoonful of chopped chervil, and a teaspoonful of tarragon, one of young onions, and half-a-dozen slices or so of beet-root
11. Turn the salad gently till well mixed, and serve

title: Marul Salatası
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuces
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- wine vinegar
- lemon juice
- olive oil
- chervil
- tarragon
- young onions
- beet-root

## Directions

1. Get two or three lettuces and remove the faded and all the coarse green leaves
2. Cut the green top off
3. Pull the leaves off one by one and cut them in three or four pieces according to the size of each leaf
4. Well wash and drain them in a cloth
5. Put in a salad-bowl half a teaspoonful of salt and one or two pinches of pepper
6. Add the yolk of an egg or two, hard boiled, then bruise them to a pulp with the back of a wooden spoon
7. Add half a tablespoonful of the best wine vinegar or lemon-juice and stir it round
8. Pour a tablespoonful of olive oil over by degrees and keep stirring
9. Add a spoonful more of vinegar and two of oil and beat them well up
10. When as smooth as cream add the lettuce with a tablespoonful of chopped chervil and a teaspoonful of tarragon, one of young onions, and half-a-dozen slices or so of beet-root
11. Turn the salad gently till well mixed and serve

title: Maydanaslu Sikma-Kyiifte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw leg of mutton
- three or four onions
- rice flour
- salt
- pepper
- parsley chopped very fine
- juice of one or two lemons
- teacup of broth

## Directions

1. Mince very fine as much as is wanted of a raw leg of mutton
2. Pound three or four onions in a mortar, and squeeze the juice over the meat
3. Knead it well with a handful of rice flour, sufficient salt and pepper
4. Make it into balls the size of small peaches
5. Roll them in parsley chopped very fine, till they are well covered
6. Arrange them in a saucepan, add the juice of one or two lemons, with a teacup of broth
7. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire, with the cover over
8. When the liquor is nearly absorbed, add some more broth, and let it simmer till nicely cooked
9. Dish it up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Maydanosslu Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- teaspoonful of finely chopped onions or spring onions
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Proceed exactly as No. 194
2. Add a tablespoonful of chopped parsley, a teaspoonful of finely chopped onions, or spring onions, sufficient salt and pepper
3. Fry as above, and serve

title: Maydanosslu Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five eggs
- a tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- a teaspoonful of finely chopped onions or spring onions
- salt
- pepper
- two ounces of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Proceed as No. 194
2. Add a tablespoonful of chopped parsley, a teaspoonful of finely chopped onions, or spring onions, sufficient salt and pepper
3. Fry as above, and serve

title: Maydanosslu Yalhni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- onions
- fresh butter
- parsley
- three or four eggs
- one or two lemons

## Directions

1. Cut sufficient mutton in pieces the size of small kidneys
2. Boil and skim it as above
3. Add a few onions sliced thin and partly fried in fresh butter
4. Let it simmer until the meat is nearly cooked
5. Add two or three handfuls of picked parsley, and let it simmer for ten or fifteen minutes longer
6. Beat up the yolks of three or four eggs in a basin with the juice of one or two lemons
7. Pour it into a small saucepan, set it on a slow fire, and stir it with a wooden spoon till it begins to thicken, without permitting it to boil
8. Dish the stew up tastefully, pour this sauce over, and serve

title: Maydanosslu Ydhni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- onions
- fresh butter
- parsley
- egg yolks
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut sufficient mutton in pieces the size of small kidneys
2. Boil and skim it
3. Add a few onions sliced thin and partly fried in fresh butter
4. Let it simmer until the meat is nearly cooked
5. Add two or three handfuls of picked parsley
6. Let it simmer for ten or fifteen minutes longer
7. Beat up the yolks of three or four eggs in a basin with the juice of one or two lemons
8. Pour it into a small saucepan, set it on a slow fire, and stir it with a wooden spoon till it begins to thicken, without permitting it to boil
9. Dish the stew up tastefully, pour this sauce over, and serve

title: Maydansslu Sikma-Kyiifte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw leg of mutton
- three or four onions
- rice flour
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- juice of one or two lemons
- teacup of broth

## Directions

1. Mince very fine as much as is wanted of a raw leg of mutton
2. pound three or four onions in a mortar, and squeeze the juice over the meat
3. knead it well with a handful of rice flour, sufficient salt and pepper, and make it into balls the size of small peaches
4. roll them in parsley chopped very fine, till they are well covered
5. arrange them in a saucepan, add the juice of one or two lemons, with a teacup of broth
6. set it on a moderate charcoal fire, with the cover over
7. When the liquor is nearly absorbed, add some more broth, and let it simmer till nicely cooked
8. Dish it up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Mdydanbsslu Kyufte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw mutton
- salt
- pepper
- fresh butter
- two or three onions
- chopped parsley
- two or three teacups of broth
- three or four tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Mince very fine a sufficient quantity of raw mutton with a little salt and pepper
2. Make it into balls the size of nectarines, and fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
3. Partly fry in the same butter two or three onions, sliced thin, to which add one or two handfuls of chopped parsley, and keep stirring for two or three minutes
4. Lay them on the bottom of a saucepan, and place the balls of meat over
5. Add two or three teacups of broth and three or four tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar
6. Set it on a slow charcoal fire, and let it simmer for twenty minutes or so, and serve

title: Mdydanbsslu Kyufte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw mutton
- salt
- pepper
- fresh butter
- two or three onions sliced thin
- one or two handfuls of chopped parsley
- two or three teacups of broth
- three or four tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Mince very fine a sufficient quantity of raw mutton with a little salt and pepper
2. Make it into balls the size of nectarines, and fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
3. Partly fry in the same butter two or three onions, sliced thin
4. Add one or two handfuls of chopped parsley, and keep stirring for two or three minutes
5. Lay them on the bottom of a saucepan, and place the balls of meat over
6. Add two or three teacups of broth and three or four tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar
7. Set it on a slow charcoal fire, and let it simmer for twenty minutes or so, and serve

title: Medkune
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five egg-plants
- two or three pounds of mutton
- one or two onions, chopped
- salt
- mutton or beef broth
- juice of two or three small lemons
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel four or five egg-plants, cut them in slices about three-quarters of an inch thick, and partly scald them in salt water
2. Cut two or three pounds of mutton in pieces the size of walnuts, sprinkle one or two chopped onions over with a little salt, and rub them well together with your hands
3. Place into a stewpan the pieces of meat and the egg-plants, in layers, cover them with mutton or beef broth, add the juice of two or three small lemons
4. Put the pan on the fire with the lid on, and let it simmer until it forms a nice gravy at the bottom
5. Dish it up, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Medkune
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five egg-plants
- two or three pounds of mutton
- one or two chopped onions
- a little salt
- mutton or beef broth
- juice of two or three small lemons
- a little cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel four or five egg-plants, cut them in slices about three-quarters of an inch thick, and partly scald them in salt water
2. Cut two or three pounds of mutton in pieces the size of walnuts, sprinkle one or two chopped onions over with a little salt, and rub them well together with your hands
3. Place into a stewpan the pieces of meat and the egg-plants, in layers, cover them with mutton or beef broth, add the juice of two or three small lemons
4. Put the pan on the fire with the lid on, and let it simmer until it forms a nice gravy at the bottom
5. Dish it up, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Menekshe Sherbely
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound very fresh violet-leaves
- 5 pounds white sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Procure a pound of very fresh violet-leaves
2. Put them on a convenient dish or a clean board with 5 pounds of white sifted sugar
3. Rub well together until the sugar becomes the same colour as the violet leaves
4. Fill your preserve jars and cover them air tight
5. Put them by
6. When wanted, dissolve 2 or 3 teaspoonfuls in a glass of cold water and take

title: Menekshe Sherbely
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of very fresh violet-leaves
- five pounds of white sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Procure a pound of very fresh violet-leaves.
2. Put them on a convenient dish or a clean board with five pounds of white sifted sugar.
3. Rub well together until the sugar becomes the same colour as the violet leaves.
4. Fill your preserve jars and cover them air tight.
5. Put them by until wanted.

title: Menekshe Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of fresh gathered violets
- two pints and a gill of boiling water
- about three pounds of crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Procure a pound of fresh gathered violets, cut the stalks off, and put the leaves in a basin
2. Pour two pints and a gill of boiling water over, immediately cover it, and let it remain for twelve or fifteen hours
3. Pass the liquor through a clean cloth into a stewpan, add about three pounds of crushed sugar
4. Stir with a wooden spoon till the sugar is dissolved, and set it on a charcoal fire
5. Just as it commences to boil up, instantly remove it, and let it remain covered till nearly cold
6. Then pass it again through a cloth, bottle and cork it up till wanted

title: Menekshe Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fresh gathered violets
- two pints and a gill of boiling water
- three pounds of crushed sugar

## Directions

1. Procure a pound of fresh gathered violets, cut the stalks off, and put the leaves in a basin
2. Pour two pints and a gill of boiling water over, immediately cover it, and let it remain for twelve or fifteen hours
3. Pass the liquor through a clean cloth into a stewpan
4. Add about three pounds of crushed sugar
5. Stir with a wooden spoon till the sugar is dissolved, and set it on a charcoal fire
6. Just as it commences to boil up, instantly remove it, and let it remain covered till nearly cold
7. Pass it again through a cloth, bottle and cork it up till wanted

title: Mevlevi Pilawi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of water
- sufficient salt
- a pound of rice, well washed
- half a pound of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put two quarts of water in a saucepan with sufficient salt, put it on the fire.
2. When boiling, throw into it a pound of rice, well washed, and boil until each grain is soft and separate.
3. Drain it through a clean cloth or in a colander, put it back in the pan, and set it on a slow fire without the cover until the moisture is dispersed.
4. Put a saucepan on the fire with half a pound of fresh butter.
5. When it boils, pour it over the rice and stir it once round with a fork, put the pan on the hob with the cover over, and let it remain for five or ten minutes.
6. Dish up, and serve hot.

title: Mevlevi Pilawi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of water
- sufficient salt
- a pound of rice, well washed

## Directions

1. Put two quarts of water in a saucepan with sufficient salt, put it on the fire
2. When boiling, throw into it a pound of rice, well washed, and boil until each grain is soft and separate
3. Drain it through a clean cloth or in a colander, put it back in the pan, and set it on a slow fire without the cover

title: Mevlevi Pilawi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of water
- salt
- a pound of rice, well washed

## Directions

1. Put two quarts of water in a saucepan with sufficient salt
2. Put it on the fire
3. When boiling, throw into it a pound of rice, well washed
4. Boil until each grain is soft and separate
5. Drain it through a clean cloth or in a colander
6. Put it back in the pan, and set it on a slow fire without the cover

title: Midiy's Pilayi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of the best olive-oil
- two or three chopped onions
- two or three tomatos
- one or two tablespoonfuls of tomato sauce
- three or four dozen mussels with their shells open
- two pints and a half of water
- sufficient salt
- a pound of the best rice, well washed

## Directions

1. Put half a pint of the best olive-oil in a saucepan with two or three chopped onions, put it on the fire until the onions are nicely browned, stirring them now and then
2. Add two or three tomatos, cut in four crossways, or if not in season, one or two tablespoonfuls of tomato sauce
3. Add three or four dozen mussels with their shells open but not separated one from another, and well scraped and washed
4. Add two pints and a half of water, and sufficient salt
5. When boiling, add a pound of the best rice, well washed
6. Put the cover over the pan, and let it simmer till the liquor is absorbed
7. Take it off, and place it on the hob for twenty minutes or so
8. Dish up, and serve hot or cold

title: Midiye Tuvâssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- flour
- eggs
- olive oil or fresh butter
- salt
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Open as many mussels as are required, and remove them from the shells
2. Dip them in flour, and then in eggs
3. Fry them a nice colour in hot olive oil or fresh butter
4. Dish up, sprinkle a little salt and pepper over, and serve hot with lemon

title: Midiye Tuwāssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- flour
- eggs
- hot olive oil or fresh butter
- salt
- pepper
- lemon

## Directions

1. Open as many mussels as are required, and remove them from the shells.
2. Dip them in flour, and then in eggs.
3. Fry them a nice colour in hot olive oil or fresh butter.
4. Dish up, sprinkle a little salt and pepper over, and serve hot with lemon.

title: Midiye-Dolmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four dozen of mussels
- half a pint of olive oil
- two or three onions, chopped fine
- a pound of well-washed rice
- two or three ounces of pistachios, skinned
- an ounce of currants
- salt
- pepper
- mixed spice
- a teacup of water

## Directions

1. Procure three or four dozen of mussels
2. Well scrape the shells in water
3. Wash them several times
4. Open them with a knife without separating one from the other
5. Put half a pint of olive oil in a saucepan, with two or three onions chopped fine, and set it on the fire
6. When hot, begin to stir with a wooden spoon until the onions are nicely browned
7. Add a pound of well-washed rice, two or three ounces of pistachios, skinned, and an ounce of currants, a little salt and pepper, and mixed spice
8. Stir till the rice begins to brown
9. Add a teacup of water, and keep stirring till the moisture is dispersed
10. Take it off
11. When nearly cold, stuff the mussels, and close the shells
12. Arrange them in a stewpan, nearly cover them with water
13. Place a plate over, inside downwards
14. Set the pan on the fire with the cover over, and let it boil until the whole of the liquid is absorbed
15. Dish up carefully, and serve hot or cold

title: Midiye-Dolması
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four dozen of mussels
- half a pint of olive oil
- two or three onions chopped fine
- a pound of well-washed rice
- two or three ounces of pistachios, skinned
- an ounce of currants
- a little salt and pepper
- mixed spice
- a teacup of water

## Directions

1. Procure three or four dozen of mussels, well scrape the shells in water, wash them several times, and open them with a knife without separating one from the other
2. Put half a pint of olive oil in a saucepan, with two or three onions chopped fine, and set it on the fire
3. When hot, begin to stir with a wooden spoon until the onions are nicely browned
4. Add a pound of well-washed rice, two or three ounces of pistachios, skinned, and an ounce of currants, a little salt and pepper, and mixed spice, and stir till the rice begins to brown
5. Add a teacup of water, and keep stirring till the moisture is dispersed
6. Take it off and let it nearly cool
7. Stuff the mussels, and close the shells
8. Arrange them in a stewpan, nearly cover them with water, place a plate over, inside downwards, set the pan on the fire with the cover over, and let it boil until the whole of the liquid is absorbed
9. Dish up carefully, and serve hot or cold

title: Morella Cherries with Fried Bread
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of water
- two pounds of morella cherries
- two French penny rolls
- yolks of two eggs
- eight tablespoonfuls of milk
- hot fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put a pint of water in a pan and put it on the fire.
2. When boiling, skim it, then add two pounds of morella cherries with the stalks taken off and the stones taken out.
3. Stir with a wooden spoon, and boil them until getting tender.
4. Cut two French penny rolls lengthwise in eight slices.
5. Put the yolks of two eggs in a basin, with eight tablespoonfuls of milk, and beat it up well.
6. Dip quickly each slice in it, and fry it a nice colour on both sides in hot fresh butter.
7. Lay them in a convenient pie-dish, then put the cherries over with the syrup.
8. If you find the syrup too thick, add a little water.
9. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire, and let it simmer until the syrup is absorbed, then serve.

title: Muhallebi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- about three pints of nice milk
- six or seven ounces of rice-flour
- milk or water
- three or four ounces of powdered sugar
- a few drops of the essence of orange-flower
- almond oil
- pieces of clotted cream

## Directions

1. Pour into a stewpan about three pints of nice milk, set it on the fire
2. As soon as it commences to boil, put six or seven ounces of rice-flour in a basin, which mix with milk or water, to form a smooth batter
3. Add it gradually to the milk, with three or four ounces of powdered sugar, and a few drops of the essence of orange-flower, and keep stirring it until it forms a smooth substance, but not too thick
4. Pour it into a mould previously oiled with almond oil; put it on ice, or in cold water
5. When firm, dip the mould in warm water, turn it out on to a dish, ornament the top with pieces of clotted cream, the size of nuts, and serve

title: Muhzir Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- caul-fat

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is required of the best part of mutton in pieces the size of an egg
2. Sprinkle one or two chopped onions over, with sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon
3. Mix well together, and let them remain for one or two hours
4. Pass the pieces of meat on the skewers, and wrap them up with some thin caul-fat
5. Place them before the fire, and turn them gently
6. As soon as the meat begins to brown, baste it with the gravy that drops from it
7. When done, dish it up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Muhzir Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- best part of mutton
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- thin caul-fat

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is required of the best part of mutton in pieces the size of an egg
2. Sprinkle one or two chopped onions over, with sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon
3. Mix well together, and let them remain for one or two hours
4. Pass the pieces of meat on the skewers, and wrap them up with some thin caul-fat
5. Place them before the fire, and turn them gently
6. As soon as the meat begins to brown, baste it with the gravy that drops from it
7. When done, dish it up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Mujmer
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three pounds of raw mutton or veal
- one or two ounces of fresh butter
- half a teacup of water
- salt
- two or three onions
- one or two handfuls of picked parsley
- crumb of a French roll
- three or four eggs
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mince two or three pounds of either raw mutton or veal, place it in a stewpan with one or two ounces of fresh butter, half a teacup of water, and a little salt
2. set it on the fire
3. When boiling, stir round with a wooden spoon until nicely browned and the moisture is dispersed
4. take it off, and put the meat in a basin, add two or three onions chopped very fine, one or two handfuls of picked parsley, the crumb of a French roll previously soaked in water, three or four eggs, and a little salt and pepper, and mix them well together
5. put a sufficient quantity of fresh butter in a convenient fryingpan, place it on a charcoal fire
6. When boiling, pour in the preparation
7. When set, and one side is nicely browned, turn it on the side, when nicely browned, dish up, and serve quite hot

title: Mujmer
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three pounds of raw mutton or veal
- one or two ounces of fresh butter
- half a teacup of water
- salt
- two or three onions chopped very fine
- one or two handfuls of picked parsley
- crumb of a French roll previously soaked in water
- three or four eggs
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place minced meat in a stewpan with one or two ounces of fresh butter, half a teacup of water, and a little salt
2. Set it on the fire
3. When boiling, stir round with a wooden spoon until nicely browned and the moisture is dispersed
4. Take it off, and put the meat in a basin
5. Add two or three onions chopped very fine, one or two handfuls of picked parsley, the crumb of a French roll previously soaked in water, three or four eggs, and a little salt and pepper, and mix them well together
6. Put a sufficient quantity of fresh butter in a convenient fryingpan, place it on a charcoal fire
7. When boiling, pour in the preparation
8. When set, and one side is nicely browned, turn it on the side
9. When nicely browned, dish up, and serve quite hot

title: Mumbar ve Shirdan
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- ventricle or intestine of sheep
- raw mutton
- one or two teacupfuls of rice, well washed
- sufficient salt and pepper
- a little cinnamon
- a little water
- sufficient salt
- fresh butter
- egg

## Directions

1. Get some ventricle or intestine of sheep well cleaned and ready for use
2. Mince a sufficient quantity of raw mutton, add one or two teacupfuls of rice, well washed, sufficient salt and pepper, a little cinnamon, and mix them well together with a little water, to form it like a paste
3. Tie one end of the ventricle and stuff it with the minced meat through a funnel
4. Tie the other end and prick it all over with a needle
5. Nearly fill a saucepan with water and sufficient salt, and place it on the fire
6. When boiling, throw in the stuffed ventricle
7. When partly cooked, take it out and place it on a trencher, then twist it round and round to form it like a twisted bun
8. When cold, fry it a nice colour in hot fresh butter, and serve
9. If approved of, cut it in thin slices, dip them in egg, and then fry them in butter, and serve

title: Mumbar Ve Shirdan
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- ventricle or intestine of sheep
- raw mutton
- one or two teacupfuls of rice
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- water

## Directions

1. Get some ventricle or intestine of sheep well cleaned and ready for use
2. Mince a sufficient quantity of raw mutton
3. Add one or two teacupfuls of rice, well washed, sufficient salt and pepper, a little cinnamon, and mix them well together with a little water, to form it like a paste
4. Tie one end of the ventricle and stuff it with the minced meat through a funnel
5. Tie the other end and prick it all over with a needle

title: Mumbar ve Shirdan
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- ventricle or intestine of sheep
- raw mutton
- one or two teacupfuls of rice, well washed
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- water
- fresh butter
- egg

## Directions

1. Get some ventricle or intestine of sheep well cleaned and ready for use
2. Mince a sufficient quantity of raw mutton
3. Add one or two teacupfuls of rice, well washed, sufficient salt and pepper, a little cinnamon, and mix them well together with a little water, to form it like a paste
4. Tie one end of the ventricle and stuff it with the minced meat through a funnel
5. Tie the other end and prick it all over with a needle
6. Nearly fill a saucepan with water and sufficient salt, and place it on the fire
7. When boiling, throw in the stuffed ventricle
8. When partly cooked, take it out and place it on a trencher
9. Twist it round and round to form it like a twisted bun
10. When cold, fry it a nice colour in hot fresh butter, and serve
11. If approved of, cut it in thin slices, dip them in egg, and then fry them in butter, and serve

title: Murina-Balighi Kyulbasstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- small cod-fish slices
- salt
- flour
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Cut from a small cod-fish as many slices as are required
2. Rub them with a little salt, and dip them in flour
3. Rub a gridiron with olive oil, lay the fish over
4. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire, and turn the fish often
5. When nicely browned on both sides, take it off
6. Cover the bottom of a dish with some finely-chopped parsley and onions
7. Place the fish over, with one or two lemons cut in quarters round the dish
8. Serve hot

title: Musannd, Khymak Baklawassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of paste (No. 135)
- ground rice
- milk
- white of one or two eggs
- sugar
- cream cheese (optional)
- powdered sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare some pieces of paste as No. 135, and lay half of them in a baking-tin
2. Boil a sufficient quantity of ground rice with milk till reduced to a thick pulp
3. When cold, put it in a basin with the white of one or two eggs, and work it with your hands for five or ten minutes, or until you bring it to the thickness of clotted cream
4. Lay it all over the paste, and cover it with the remaining pieces of paste
5. Cut it up in diamonds, and bake it as above
6. Dish up tastefully, sprinkle sugar over, and serve
7. Cream cheese worked with a little powdered sugar may be used instead of rice

title: Musannd, Khymak Baláctuwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of paste (No. 135)
- ground rice
- milk
- white of one or two eggs
- cream cheese or powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare some pieces of paste as No. 135, and lay half of them in a baking-tin
2. Boil a sufficient quantity of ground rice with milk till reduced to a thick pulp
3. When cold, put it in a basin with the white of one or two eggs, and work it with your hands for five or ten minutes, or until you bring it to the thickness of clotted cream
4. Lay it all over the paste, and cover it with the remaining pieces of paste
5. Cut it up in diamonds, and bake it as above
6. Dish up tastefully, sprinkle sugar over, and serve

title: Muzakka
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two pounds of raw mutton or lamb
- two or three onions
- two or three ounces of fresh butter
- salt
- two or three tablespoonfuls of chopped parsley
- three or four egg-plants
- salt water
- a pint of broth

## Directions

1. Mince very fine one or two pounds of raw mutton or lamb
2. Put it in a frying-pan with two or three finely chopped onions, two or three ounces of fresh butter, and a little salt
3. Set it on the fire and let the meat and the onions stew a nice brown, stirring it now and then
4. Two minutes before taking it off, add two or three tablespoonfuls of chopped parsley and stir it well
5. Cut three or four egg-plants in slices lengthways and place them in salt water for ten or fifteen minutes
6. Place the minced meat and the egg-plants in layers into a pan
7. Add a pint of broth or a little more
8. Put the pan on a moderate charcoal fire with the cover over and let it simmer until the liquor is nearly absorbed and the egg-plants are nicely cooked
9. Turn it on to a dish without spoiling the shape and serve

title: Mídíyí's Pilaví
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pint of the best olive-oil
- two or three chopped onions
- two or three tomatos
- one or two tablespoonfuls of tomato sauce
- three or four dozen mussels with their shells open
- two pints and a half of water
- salt
- a pound of the best rice, well washed

## Directions

1. Put half a pint of the best olive-oil in a saucepan with two or three chopped onions, put it on the fire until the onions are nicely browned, stirring them now and then
2. Add two or three tomatos, cut in four crossways; if not in season, one or two tablespoonfuls of tomato sauce will answer
3. Add three or four dozen mussels with their shells open, but not separated one from another, and well scraped and washed, two pints and a half of water, and sufficient salt
4. When boiling, add a pound of the best rice, well washed
5. Put the cover over the pan, and let it simmer till the liquor is absorbed
6. Take it off, and place it on the hob for twenty minutes or so
7. Dish up, and serve hot or cold

title: Nar Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pomegranates
- four ounces of loaf sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Cut open three or four pomegranates, take the grains out
2. Pick up the large ones, put the small ones in a cloth, and squeeze all the juice from them into a saucepan
3. Add four ounces of loaf sugar, with the same quantity of water as the juice, and stir on a slow fire until it becomes thickish
4. Remove it and let it cool
5. Proceed as with the orange sherbet

title: Ndr Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pomegranates
- four ounces of loaf sugar
- water equal to the quantity of juice

## Directions

1. Cut open three or four pomegranates and take the grains out
2. Pick up the large ones and put the small ones in a cloth
3. Squeeze all the juice from them into a saucepan
4. Add four ounces of loaf sugar with the same quantity of water as the juice
5. Stir on a slow fire until it becomes thickish
6. Remove it and let it cool
7. Proceed as above by pouring into a glass bowl and serving with ice and essence

title: Ndzli-Ash
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of rice
- three pints of water
- one wine-glass of rose-water
- powdered sugar
- powdered almonds or walnuts

## Directions

1. Pound, rather coarse, half a pound of rice in a mortar
2. Put three pints of water in a stewpan with a wine-glass of rose-water
3. Set it on the fire
4. When boiling, add the rice
5. Let it simmer till nearly thick, stirring it now and then
6. Sweeten it with powdered sugar according to taste
7. Let it simmer for a few minutes longer
8. Pour it in a dish
9. When cold, sprinkle some powdered almonds or walnuts over
10. Serve

title: Ndztli-Ash
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of rice
- three pints of water
- a wine-glass of rose-water
- powdered sugar
- powdered almonds or walnuts

## Directions

1. Pound, rather coarse, half a pound of rice in a mortar
2. Put three pints of water in a stewpan, with a wine-glass of rose-water
3. Set it on the fire
4. When boiling, add the rice
5. Let it simmer till nearly thick, stirring it now and then
6. Sweeten it with powdered sugar according to taste
7. Let it simmer for a few minutes longer
8. Pour it in a dish
9. When cold, sprinkle some powdered almonds or walnuts over
10. Serve

title: Nevi Archer
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound or so of flour
- half or three-quarters of a pound of scalded fresh butter
- two or three eggs
- three or four tablespoonfuls of cream
- a little salt
- two or three tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Work lightly in a basin a pound or so of flour, with half or three-quarters of a pound of scalded fresh butter
2. Add two or three eggs, three or four tablespoonfuls of cream, a little salt, two or three tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
3. Work all together well to form a nice stiff paste
4. Make it into cakes, or in any shape you please
5. Arrange them in a buttered baking-tin
6. Egg over, and bake a nice brown in the oven
7. Sprinkle some powdered sugar over, and serve

title: Nevi Atçher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound or so of flour
- half or three-quarters of a pound of scalded fresh butter
- two or three eggs
- three or four tablespoonfuls of cream
- a little salt
- two or three tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Work lightly in a basin a pound or so of flour, with half or three-quarters of a pound of scalded fresh butter
2. Add two or three eggs, three or four tablespoonfuls of cream, a little salt, two or three tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
3. Work all together well to form a nice stiff paste
4. Make it into cakes or in any shape you please
5. Arrange them in a buttered baking-tin, egg over, and bake a nice brown in the oven
6. Sprinkle some powdered sugar over, and serve

title: Nevi Diger
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw beef or mutton
- two or three onions
- equal quantity of Pilaw
- three or four eggs
- salt
- pepper
- parsley chopped fine
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Mince a sufficient quantity of raw beef or mutton together with two or three onions
2. Pound it well in a mortar with an equal quantity of Pilaw
3. Break over it three or four eggs
4. Sprinkle sufficient salt and pepper, with a handful of parsley chopped fine
5. Knead well all together, and let it remain covered for half an hour
6. Divide it in pieces the size of an egg
7. Fry them in boiling fresh butter
8. When all are nicely browned, dish up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- any fish
- three or four onions
- a plate of picked parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one or two tablespoonfuls of tomato sauce
- half a teacup of wine vinegar
- half a teacup of olive oil
- one teacup of water

## Directions

1. Get any fish you fancy and clean it well
2. Cut three or four onions in thin slices and mix them with a plate of picked parsley
3. Lay half on the bottom of a stewpan
4. Place the fish over and the other half of the onions and parsley on the top
5. Add sufficient salt and pepper, one or two tablespoonfuls of tomato sauce, half a teacup of wine vinegar, the same quantity of olive oil, and one of water
6. Set the pan on a charcoal fire and let it simmer gently until the fish is cooked
7. Dish it up and serve

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- fresh butter
- water
- fried minced meat
- onions
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Put sufficient flour upon a pastry slab, make a hole in the centre, in which put a little salt, two or three ounces of fresh butter melted
2. Mix all together, then add sufficient water, and make it a softish paste
3. While working it, dip your hands in butter now and then, and knead it till a nice paste is formed
4. Throw some flour lightly over and under, roll it out with a rolling-pin to the thickness of half-a-crown
5. Put a tablespoonful of fried minced meat on it, mixed with finely-chopped onions stewed brown in fresh butter, or grated cheese, about an inch and a half distance from each other
6. Wet lightly round them with a paste-brush, and place a similar piece of paste over all
7. Press round the part where the minced meat or cheese is, to make the paste stick
8. Cut them out like half an egg, and fry them in hot butter
9. When nicely browned, take them out, and let them drain, dish up

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- clotted cream
- ground rice
- butter
- syrup

## Directions

1. Make three or four pounds of vermicelli as No. 149
2. Lay half of it in a buttered baking-tin
3. Put in a basin three-quarters of a pound of clotted cream, and work it with six or seven ounces of ground rice
4. Lay it all over the vermicelli
5. Cover it with the other half of vermicelli
6. Pour in a little scalded butter
7. Bake it a nice brown on both sides
8. Pour some syrup over as above and serve

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound and six ounces of fresh butter
- two pounds of the best flour
- sifted sugar
- a finely-grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan a pound and six ounces of fresh butter, and set it on the fire
2. When melted, mix in gradually two pounds of the best flour, and stir until the flour is done
3. Take it off, and while it is getting cold, beat it up till it becomes a smooth substance
4. Add by degrees as much sifted sugar as it will take, and a finely-grated nutmeg
5. Work it well with your hands, and make it into small cakes, any fanciful shape you please
6. Place them in a buttered tin, and bake a delicate colour in the oven

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- garlic
- wine vinegar
- water
- olive oil
- parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Peel and cut in four lengthwise one or two cucumbers
2. Cut them in thin slices and sprinkle a little salt over
3. Shake them up
4. Pound two or three cloves of garlic in a stone mortar
5. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of the best wine vinegar, and one of water
6. Add two or three of the best olive oil and stir it well
7. Arrange the slices of cucumber in a plate with a handful of chopped parsley
8. Pour the vinegar and oil over, and serve

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- loin of mutton
- onions
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- caul-fat
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut a hare into pieces
2. Mix with as many pieces of loin of mutton as there are of hare, all of equal size
3. Sprinkle three chopped onions over, with a handful of parsley chopped fine, sufficient salt and pepper
4. Rub the hare and mutton with these, and let them remain covered with a cloth for one or two hours
5. Pass them on skewers, first mutton, then hare, and so on until the whole of them are on skewers
6. If handy, wrap them up with some thin caul-fat
7. Put them before the fire, and turn them gently
8. As soon as the gravy begins to drop, sprinkle a little flour over
9. When the meat is nicely cooked, dish it up, pour the gravy over, and serve

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- garlic
- wine vinegar
- water
- olive oil
- parsley

## Directions

1. Peel and cut in four lengthwise one or two cucumbers
2. Cut them in thin slices and sprinkle a little salt over
3. Shake them up
4. Pound two or three cloves of garlic in a stone mortar
5. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of the best wine vinegar and one of water and two or three of the best olive oil
6. Stir it well
7. Arrange the slices of cucumber in a plate with a handful of chopped parsley and pour the vinegar and oil over
8. Serve

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a hare
- loin of mutton
- three chopped onions
- a handful of parsley chopped fine
- salt
- pepper
- thin caul-fat

## Directions

1. Cut a hare into pieces
2. Mix with as many pieces of loin of mutton as there are of hare, all of equal size
3. Sprinkle three chopped onions over, with a handful of parsley chopped fine, sufficient salt and pepper
4. Rub the hare and mutton with these, and let them remain covered with a cloth for one or two hours
5. Pass them on skewers, first mutton, then hare, and so on until the whole of them are on skewers
6. If handy, wrap them up with some thin caul-fat
7. Put them before the fire, and turn them gently
8. As soon as the gravy begins to drop, sprinkle a little flour over
9. When the meat is nicely cooked, dish it up, pour the gravy over, and serve

title: Nevi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- two or three ounces of fresh butter
- water
- tablespoonful of fried minced meat
- finely-chopped onions
- hot butter

## Directions

1. Put sufficient flour upon a pastry slab, make a hole in the centre, in which put a little salt, two or three ounces of fresh butter melted, mix all together, then add sufficient water, and make it a softish paste.
2. While working it, dip your hands in butter now and then, and knead it till a nice paste is formed.
3. Throw some flour lightly over and under, roll it out with a rolling-pin to the thickness of half-a-crown.
4. Put a tablespoonful of fried minced meat on it, mixed with finely-chopped onions stewed brown in fresh butter, or grated cheese, about an inch and a half distance from each other.
5. Wet lightly round them with a paste-brush, and place a similar piece of paste over all.
6. Press round the part where the minced meat or cheese is, to make the paste stick.
7. Cut them out like half an egg, and fry them in hot butter.
8. When nicely browned, take them out, and let them drain, dish up.

title: Nevi Digher (Crumpets)
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- crumpets
- butter
- powdered sugar
- pounded pistachios or almonds
- rose-water

## Directions

1. Get as many crumpets as are wanted
2. Fry them a nice colour in scalded fresh butter
3. Whilst hot, arrange them in a dish
4. Sprinkle between them powdered sugar, with pounded pistachios or almonds, and rose-water
5. Let them remain covered for a few minutes and serve

title: Nevi Digher (Crumpets)
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- crumpets
- butter
- powdered sugar
- pistachios or almonds
- rose-water

## Directions

1. Get as many crumpets as are wanted
2. Fry them a nice colour in scalded fresh butter
3. Whilst hot, arrange them in a dish
4. Sprinkle between them powdered sugar, with pounded pistachios or almonds, and rose-water
5. Let them remain covered for a few minutes
6. Serve

title: Nevi Digher (Fried)
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- three or four ounces of fresh butter
- two or three eggs
- a little milk
- two or three pinches of salt
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Work lightly in a basin a sufficient quantity of flour with three or four ounces of fresh butter
2. Add two or three eggs, a little milk, and two or three pinches of salt
3. Work all well to form a nice paste
4. Make it into small diamond-shaped cakes
5. Fry them a delicate colour in hot fresh butter
6. Take them out and place them in a strainer
7. When free from grease, dish them up in crown or pyramid
8. Shake some powdered sugar over, and serve

title: Nevi Dighter
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds quinces
- 4 quarts of water
- 3 pounds powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the quinces as above
2. Put them in a stewpan with 4 quarts of water
3. Set the pan on the fire and let it boil until the fruit becomes like a jelly
4. Take it off and pass it through a cloth or a sieve into another pan
5. Add 3 pounds of powdered sugar
6. Set it to boil again till it becomes a thick syrup
7. Take it off and strain it again
8. Try a little on a plate before potting to see if it sets
9. When nearly cold, fill your preserve jars and cover them when cold

title: Nevi Dighter
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of quinces
- four quarts of water
- three pounds of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and cut three pounds of quinces.
2. Put them in a stewpan with four quarts of water.
3. Set the pan on the fire and let it boil until the fruit becomes like a jelly.
4. Take it off and pass it through a cloth or a sieve into another pan.
5. Add three pounds of powdered sugar.
6. Set it to boil again till it becomes a thick syrup.
7. Take it off and strain it again.
8. When nearly cold, fill your preserve jars and cover them when cold.
9. Try a little on a plate before potting to be certain.
10. If it sets, it is done.

title: Nevi Digler
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg or rump of beef
- vinegar
- pepper-corns
- orange-peel
- garlic
- fresh butter
- tomatoes
- water
- salt
- macaroni
- broth
- cheese

## Directions

1. Get in one piece five or six pounds of the leg or rump of beef
2. Cover it with vinegar, and let it remain four and twenty hours
3. Wash it, and make deep holes all over
4. Fill the holes with pepper-corns, orange-peel, and cloves of garlic peeled
5. Place it in a stewpan with three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
6. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire for three or four hours, turning it now and then
7. When nicely browned, add the juice of two or three pounds of tomatoes with two or three pints of water, and sufficient salt and pepper
8. Let it simmer till the meat is nicely cooked
9. Take it out, and keep it hot
10. Put in the stewpan about two pounds and a half of the best macaroni
11. If the liquor is not enough, add some of the broth, and boil till the macaroni is tender
12. Let it be rather firm to the touch
13. Dish it up, sprinkle some finely-grated cheese over, then place the beef in the centre of the macaroni, and serve

title: Nevi Diglier
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five pounds of loin of mutton
- six or seven onions
- salt
- pepper
- four or five vegetable marrows
- water
- juice of two or three lemons
- three or four teaspoonfuls of powdered sugar
- four or five cloves of garlic
- two or three tablespoonfuls of chopped mint

## Directions

1. Get four or five pounds of loin of mutton, remove the bones, and cut the meat in pieces the size of walnuts
2. Add six or seven onions sliced fine
3. Sprinkle sufficient salt and pepper over, mix them all together, and rub them well with your hands
4. Peel four or five vegetable marrows, and cut them in slices; take out the insides, if seeded
5. Lay the bones of the mutton on the bottom of a convenient stewpan; then some slices of vegetable marrow over, then some pieces of mutton, then vegetable marrow, and so forth, till you have laid the whole
6. Cover them with water, add the juice of two or three lemons and a little salt
7. Set it on the fire, and let it simmer gently until the meat and the vegetable marrows are tender
8. Add three or four teaspoonfuls of powdered sugar, four or five cloves of garlic, skinned and well pounded, and two or three tablespoonfuls of chopped mint
9. Let it simmer for five or six minutes longer, then dish it up, and serve

title: Nevi Diglier
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg or rump of beef
- vinegar
- pepper-corns
- orange-peel
- garlic
- fresh butter
- tomatoes
- water
- salt
- pepper
- macaroni
- broth
- cheese

## Directions

1. Get in one piece five or six pounds of the leg or rump of beef, cover it with vinegar, and let it remain four and twenty hours
2. Wash it, and make deep holes all over
3. Fill the holes with pepper-corns, orange-peel, and cloves of garlic peeled
4. Place it in a stewpan with three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
5. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire for three or four hours, turning it now and then
6. When nicely browned, add the juice of two or three pounds of tomatoes with two or three pints of water, and sufficient salt and pepper, and let it simmer till the meat is nicely cooked
7. Take it out, and keep it hot
8. Put in the stewpan about two pounds and a half of the best macaroni
9. If the liquor is not enough, add some of the broth, and boil till the macaroni is tender
10. Let it be rather firm to the touch
11. Dish it up, sprinkle some finely-grated cheese over, then place the beef in the centre of the macaroni, and serve

title: Nevi Diglier
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw beef or mutton
- two or three onions
- Pilaw
- three or four eggs
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Mince a sufficient quantity of raw beef or mutton together with two or three onions
2. pound it well in a mortar with an equal quantity of Pilaw
3. break over it three or four eggs, sprinkle sufficient salt and pepper, with a handful of parsley chopped fine
4. knead well all together, and let it remain covered for half an hour
5. divide it in pieces the size of an egg, and fry them in boiling fresh butter
6. When all are nicely browned, dish up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Nevi Diglier
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- nine ounces of wheat-starch
- water
- two pounds and three quarters of loaf sugar
- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds

## Directions

1. Put nine ounces of wheat-starch in a basin with about six pints of water
2. Stir it until smooth and pass it through a fine sieve
3. Put in a stewpan two pounds and three quarters of loaf sugar with half a pint of water and stir until the sugar is dissolved
4. Add by degrees the starch and water
5. Set it on the fire and stir it gently until it gets very thick
6. Add one tablespoonful at a time three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter previously melted
