7. Add a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds and keep stirring
8. Taste it now and then
9. When it does not stick to the teeth pour it into a convenient dish
10. When cold, cut it up in diamonds
11. Dish up and serve

title: Nevi Diglier
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound and six ounces of the best fresh butter
- a quarter of a pound of flour
- a quarter of a pound of rice-flour
- a quarter of a pound of wheat-starch
- six ounces of skinned almonds
- two pints and a half of boiling syrup
- a pint and a half of boiling milk
- rose-water
- powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Set on a charcoal fire a stewpan with a pound and six ounces of the best fresh butter
2. When melted, add gradually a quarter of a pound of flour, ditto of rice-flour, and the same of wheat-starch, and keep stirring until the flour becomes a gold colour
3. Add six ounces of skinned almonds, and stir for a few minutes longer
4. Pour two pints and a half of boiling syrup over, as above, with a pint and a half of boiling milk
5. Instantly put the cover over the pan, and let it remain for fifteen minutes
6. Stir it well, dish up, sprinkle some rose-water over, with a little powdered cinnamon, and serve

title: Nevi Diglier
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- nine ounces of wheat-starch
- six pints of water
- two pounds and three quarters of loaf sugar
- half a pint of water
- three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter
- a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds

## Directions

1. Put nine ounces of wheat-starch in a basin with about six pints of water
2. Stir it until smooth and pass it through a fine sieve
3. Put in a stewpan two pounds and three quarters of loaf sugar with half a pint of water and stir until the sugar is dissolved
4. Add by degrees the starch and water and set it on the fire
5. Stir it gently until it gets very thick
6. Add one tablespoonful at a time, three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter, previously melted, and a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds
7. Keep stirring and taste it now and then
8. When it does not stick to the teeth, pour it into a convenient dish
9. When cold, cut it up in diamonds, dish up and serve

title: Nevi Higher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two pounds of lamb
- fresh butter
- salt
- water
- six or seven dozens of young okras
- three or four finely-sliced onions
- juice of one or two lemons
- three cups of mutton or beef broth
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut one or two pounds of lamb in pieces about the size of walnuts
2. Place them in a stewpan with an ounce of fresh butter, a small teaspoonful of salt, and half a teacup of water
3. Put on the fire
4. When boiling, stir round with a wooden spoon for about twelve minutes, or until it forms a whitish thick gravy at the bottom
5. Lay the pieces of meat on the bottom of a rather deep and circular baking-pan
6. Over which lay regularly six or seven dozens of young okras, together with three or four finely-sliced onions, fried partly brown in fresh butter
7. Add the gravy, with the juice of one or two lemons, three cups of either mutton or beef broth, and a little salt
8. Bake it in a very hot oven until the okras are quite soft and nicely brown
9. Turn it gently on to a dish, without spoiling the shape, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Nevi Uglier
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- morella cherries
- two round penny loaves
- water
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare some morella cherries as above.
2. Cut two round penny loaves in slices, and toast them.
3. Lay them in a pie-dish or saucepan, put the cherries over with the syrup, and nearly cover them with water.
4. Put the pan on the fire, and let it simmer till the slices of toasted bread are soft.
5. Dish up one over the other, laying between them the cherries, and sprinkling at the same time powdered sugar over.
6. Serve hot or cold.

title: Nevi Uiglier
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- morella cherries
- two round penny loaves
- water
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare some morella cherries as above
2. Cut two round penny loaves in slices, and toast them
3. Lay them in a pie-dish or saucepan, put the cherries over with the syrup, and nearly cover them with water
4. Put the pan on the fire, and let it simmer till the slices of toasted bread are soft
5. Dish up one over the other, laying between them the cherries, and sprinkling at the same time powdered sugar over
6. Serve hot or cold

title: Nokhud Chbrbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- chick peas
- broth
- cinnamon
- toasted or fried bread

## Directions

1. Scald a sufficient quantity of chick peas, and remove the pods, or skins
2. Put them again into the same water, and boil them until quite soft
3. Drain and mash them with the back of a wooden spoon, and pass them through a sieve into a stewpan
4. Moisten it with as much broth as the quantity of soup wanted
5. Set it on the fire, and let it simmer until reduced to a nice consistency
6. Pour it in a tureen, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve with toasted or fried bread in small dice

title: Nokhud Chorbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- chick peas
- broth
- cinnamon
- toasted or fried bread

## Directions

1. Scald a sufficient quantity of chick peas, and remove the pods, or skins
2. Put them again into the same water, and boil them until quite soft
3. Drain and mash them with the back of a wooden spoon, and pass them through a sieve into a stewpan
4. Moisten it with as much broth as the quantity of soup wanted
5. Set it on the fire, and let it simmer until reduced to a nice consistency
6. Pour it in a tureen, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve with toasted or fried bread in small dice

title: Nuriye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- eggs
- lemons
- olive oil or butter
- syrup
- loaf sugar
- water
- clotted cream
- milk
- rose-water
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put in a basin or on a slab one pound of flour
2. Make a hole in the centre, in which put a small teaspoonful of salt, the white of five or six eggs, and the juice of two or three lemons strained
3. Mix all together
4. Dip your hands in olive oil or butter
5. Work it until it forms a softish flexible paste
6. Divide it in pieces the size of walnuts
7. Roll them out to the thickness of a sixpence with a rolling-pin
8. Fold each piece even
9. Fry them a nice brown in scalded fresh butter and put them in a colander
10. When free from fat, throw them in boiling syrup, which you have previously made of loaf sugar and water as in No. 149
11. Take them out and lay half of them in a baking-tin, one over the other
12. Lay all over some clotted cream, about a quarter of an inch thick
13. Cover it with the other half of the paste
14. Add half a pint of milk, or little more
15. Put it on a charcoal fire and let it boil until quite soft
16. Sprinkle some rose-water over, with a little powdered cinnamon, and serve

title: Nuriye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- eggs
- lemons
- olive oil or butter
- syrup
- clotted cream
- milk
- rose-water
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put in a basin or on a slab one pound of flour
2. Make a hole in the centre, in which put a small teaspoonful of salt
3. Add the white of five or six eggs, and the juice of two or three lemons strained
4. Mix all together
5. Dip your hands in olive oil or butter
6. Work it until it forms a softish flexible paste
7. Divide it in pieces the size of walnuts
8. Roll them out to the thickness of a sixpence with a rolling-pin
9. Fold each piece even
10. Fry them a nice brown in scalded fresh butter
11. Put them in a colander
12. When free from fat, throw them in boiling syrup, which you have previously made of loaf sugar and water as in No. 149
13. Take them out and lay half of them in a baking-tin, one over the other
14. Lay all over some clotted cream, about a quarter of an inch thick
15. Cover it with the other half of the paste
16. Add half a pint of milk, or little more
17. Put it on a charcoal fire
18. Let it boil until quite soft
19. Sprinkle some rose-water over, with a little powdered cinnamon, and serve

title: Pacha-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a calf's head or four of the feet
- sufficient water
- salt
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Get half a calf's head, or four of the feet, wash, and put them in a stewpan with sufficient water and salt
2. Set it on the fire, and let it boil until tender
3. Remove the meat from the bones, and cut it in small dice
4. Prepare ten or twelve pieces of paste as No. 78, but instead of scalding them in water, scald them in boiling fresh butter
5. When all done, take out and place them in a colander, and when free from fat, lay half of them in a rather deep and circular baking-tin one over the other, sprinkling between each piece some of the liquor in which the calf's head is boiled
6. Lay the pieces of calf's head all over, cover them with the remaining pieces of paste the same way as before, and bake it a nice colour
7. Dish up, and serve

title: Pachia
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- ten or twelve sheep's trotters
- water
- three or four cloves of garlic
- salt
- half a teacup of wine vinegar or the juice of three or four lemons
- thin slices of toast
- yolks of two or three eggs
- juice of a lemon or two
- pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clean the sheep's trotters and break the long bones
2. Put them in a stewpan nearly full of water
3. Place it on the fire or in a very hot oven with the cover over it until the meat comes off the bones
4. Take out the large bones and cut the meat in small pieces
5. Pound three or four cloves of garlic in a mortar with a little salt
6. Add half a teacup of wine vinegar or the juice of three or four lemons and stir it well with a wooden spoon
7. Pour it over the trotters with a little salt and stir it once
8. Set it on the fire and let it boil again for two or three minutes
9. Lay thin slices of toast on the bottom of a dish, over which place the pieces of trotters with some of the liquor
10. Put it on a very slow charcoal fire until the liquor is nearly absorbed
11. Beat up the yolks of two or three eggs with the juice of a lemon or two and a little of the liquor in which the trotters are cooked
12. Place them on a moderate fire and begin stirring with a wooden spoon (taking it from the fire now and then when getting too hot) until it thickens
13. Pour it over the trotters, sprinkle a little pepper and cinnamon over, and serve very hot

title: Panjar Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five beetroots
- water
- one or two cloves of garlic
- salt
- ounce of powdered sugar
- half a pint of nice wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the stalks off and well wash four or five beetroots
2. Boil them till tender
3. Peel and cut them into rather thin slices
4. Arrange them in a jar or glass bowl
5. Add half a pint of the water in which they are boiled
6. Pound one or two cloves of garlic in a stone mortar with a little salt
7. Add an ounce of powdered sugar, and half a pint of nice wine vinegar
8. Stir well
9. Pour it over the beetroots
10. Ready for use

title: Partakal Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six oranges
- four ounces of powdered sugar
- the juice of two oranges
- water
- a few drops of the essence of orange-flower
- a few small pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Peel five or six oranges, and divide them in pieces
2. Cut each clove or piece of orange in two, and remove the pips and the thin membranes from them
3. Put four ounces of powdered sugar in a stewpan, with the juice of two oranges
4. If they are not sufficiently sweet, add more sugar
5. Put it on a slow fire, and stir with a wooden spoon till it begins to boil
6. Take it off and let it cool
7. Pour in a glass bowl, and mix it with water to form a nice sherbet
8. Add the pieces of orange, with a few drops of the essence of orange-flower, and a few small pieces of ice, and serve

title: Pdhati-l-hdlkum
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds and three-quarters of loaf-sugar
- four quarts of water
- eight ounces and a half of fine wheat starch
- musk
- a little less than a quarter of a pint of rose-water
- almond-oil
- sifted sugar
- fine wheat starch

## Directions

1. Put in a very clean stewpan two pounds and three-quarters of loaf-sugar with four quarts of water
2. Stir with a wooden spoon until the sugar is dissolved
3. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire
4. Immediately add eight ounces and a half of fine wheat starch by degrees, stirring it at the same time so as to prevent it from getting lumpy
5. Continue stirring it from the bottom of the pan until it forms a smooth substance
6. Take a little out and let drop a few drops on powdered sugar to test if done
7. Mix a little musk, the size of a small pea, in a little less than a quarter of a pint of rose-water
8. Add this to it
9. Stir it for half a minute or a minute longer
10. Take it off and pour it into a convenient dish or a plated pan about an inch deep, which you have previously oiled with almond-oil
11. When cold, cut it in pieces an inch broad and two long
12. Dip them in sifted sugar mixed with half the quantity of fine wheat starch
13. See that they are well covered, to prevent them from sticking to each other
14. Serve as a dessert

title: Pdhati-l-hdlkum
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds and three-quarters of loaf-sugar
- four quarts of water
- eight ounces and a half of fine wheat starch
- musk
- a little less than a quarter of a pint of rose-water
- almond-oil
- sifted sugar mixed with fine wheat starch

## Directions

1. Put in a very clean stewpan two pounds and three-quarters of loaf-sugar, with four quarts of water
2. Stir with a wooden spoon until the sugar is dissolved
3. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire
4. Immediately add eight ounces and a half of fine wheat starch by degrees, stirring it at the same time so as to prevent it from getting lumpy
5. Continue stirring it from the bottom of the pan until it forms a smooth substance
6. Take a little out and let drop a few drops on powdered sugar
7. If it moistens or absorbs the sugar it is not done, and if it does not it is done
8. Mix a little musk, the size of a small pea, in a little less than a quarter of a pint of rose-water and add this to it
9. Stir it for half a minute or a minute longer
10. Take it off and pour it into a convenient dish or a plated pan about an inch deep, which you have previously oiled with almond-oil
11. When cold, cut it in pieces an inch broad and two long
12. Dip them in sifted sugar mixed with half the quantity of fine wheat starch
13. See that they are well covered, to prevent them from sticking to each other
14. Serve as a dessert

title: Pdlude
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of white sugar
- two quarts of water
- five ounces of wheat starch
- essence of rose or orange-flower
- almond-oil
- skinned pistachios or almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Dissolve two pounds of white sugar in a stewpan with two quarts of water
2. Add about five ounces of wheat starch
3. Stir it well
4. When as smooth as milk, put it on a moderate charcoal fire
5. Continually stir it for about three hours, or until it forms a smooth substance, but not too thick
6. Add a few drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower
7. Stir it for a few minutes longer
8. Pour it into a mould previously oiled with almond-oil
9. Put it on ice, or in a cool place, till firm
10. When ready, dip the mould in warm water and turn it out
11. Serve (You can ornament the top with some skinned pistachios or almonds)

title: Pdlude
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of white sugar
- two quarts of water
- about five ounces of wheat starch
- essence of rose or orange-flower
- almond-oil
- skinned pistachios or almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Dissolve two pounds of white sugar in a stewpan, with two quarts of water
2. Add about five ounces of wheat starch and stir it well
3. When as smooth as milk, put it on a moderate charcoal fire
4. Continually stir it for about three hours, or until it forms a smooth substance, but not too thick
5. Add a few drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower
6. Stir it for a few minutes longer
7. Pour it into a mould previously oiled with almond-oil
8. Put it on ice, or in a cool place, till firm
9. When ready, dip the mould in warm water and turn it out
10. Serve (you can ornament the top with some skinned pistachios or almonds)

title: Pdrtakdl Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six oranges
- four ounces of powdered sugar
- water
- a few drops of the essence of orange-flower
- a few small pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Peel five or six oranges and divide them in pieces
2. Cut each clove or piece of orange in two and remove the pips and the thin membranes from them
3. Put four ounces of powdered sugar in a stewpan with the juice of two oranges
4. Add more sugar if they are not sufficiently sweet
5. Put it on a slow fire and stir with a wooden spoon till it begins to boil
6. Take it off and let it cool
7. Pour in a glass bowl and mix it with water to form a nice sherbet
8. Add the pieces of orange with a few drops of the essence of orange-flower and a few small pieces of ice
9. Serve

title: Penjar Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five beetroots
- garlic
- salt
- powdered sugar
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the stalks off and well wash four or five beetroots
2. Boil them till tender
3. Peel and cut them into rather thin slices
4. Arrange them in a jar or glass bowl with half a pint of the water in which they are boiled
5. Pound one or two cloves of garlic in a stone mortar with a little salt
6. Add an ounce of powdered sugar and half a pint of nice wine vinegar
7. Stir well and pour it over the beetroots
8. Ready for use

title: Pirinj Baklaivdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of the best white honey
- one pound of the best fresh butter
- one pint of hot or cold water
- one pound of ground rice
- one teaspoonful of carbonate of soda dissolved in three tablespoonfuls of water
- powdered sugar
- loaf-sugar

## Directions

1. Put in a clean stewpan a pint of the best white honey, which you have previously boiled and skimmed, a pound of the best fresh butter, previously melted, a pint of hot or cold water, a pound of ground rice, and a teaspoonful of carbonate of soda dissolved in three tablespoonfuls of water
2. Put the pan on a charcoal fire, and stir it until reduced to a thick pulp
3. Pour it in a lightly-buttered baking-tin, spread it all over with a spoon
4. Bake it a nice brown in a hot oven
5. When done, cut it up in diamonds, sprinkle some powdered sugar over, dish up in pyramid, and serve

title: Pirinj Baklawassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of the best white honey, previously boiled and skimmed
- one pound of the best fresh butter, previously melted
- one pint of hot or cold water
- one pound of ground rice
- one teaspoonful of carbonate of soda dissolved in three tablespoonfuls of water
- powdered sugar
- one pound of loaf-sugar (alternative to honey)

## Directions

1. Put in a clean stewpan a pint of the best white honey, which you have previously boiled and skimmed, a pound of the best fresh butter, previously melted, a pint of hot or cold water, a pound of ground rice, and a teaspoonful of carbonate of soda dissolved in three tablespoonfuls of water
2. Put the pan on a charcoal fire, and stir it until reduced to a thick pulp
3. Pour it in a lightly-buttered baking-tin, spread it all over with a spoon, and bake it a nice brown in a hot oven
4. When done, cut it up in diamonds, sprinkle some powdered sugar over, dish up in pyramid, and serve
5. If honey is not fancied, use a pound of loaf-sugar, previously dissolved in water, and boiled to the consistency of a syrup

title: Pirinj Chbrbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four ounces of the best rice
- salt
- broth
- yolk of one or two eggs
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Wash three or four ounces of the best rice
2. Put it into a stewpan, with a little salt, and three or four pints of broth
3. Set the pan on the fire, and let it boil gently till the rice is soft
4. Add the yolk of one or two eggs prepared as above
5. Pour the soup into a tureen, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Pirinj Chorbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four ounces of the best rice
- salt
- broth
- yolk of one or two eggs
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Wash three or four ounces of the best rice
2. Put it into a stewpan, with a little salt, and three or four pints of broth
3. Set the pan on the fire, and let it boil gently till the rice is soft
4. Add the yolk of one or two eggs prepared as in Terbiyeli Jigher Chorbassi
5. Pour the soup into a tureen, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Pirmj-Unu Helivassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of the best fresh butter
- a pound and a half of rice-flour
- a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds
- a pint and a half of boiling syrup
- a pint and a half of boiling milk
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put in a stewpan a pound of the best fresh butter, and scald it
2. Then remove it from the fire: when a little cold, add by degrees a pound and a half of rice-flour
3. Set it again on the fire, and keep stirring for twenty-five minutes, or until the butter appears on the surface of the flour, and the flour swells up like grains of gunpowder
4. Then add a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds, and stir it for a few minutes longer
5. Then immediately add a pint and a half of boiling syrup, previously prepared of white sugar and water, as in No. 159, and the same quantity of boiling milk
6. Instantly put the cover over, and let it remain for about five minutes
7. Then stir it well with a wooden fork, dish it up in spoonfuls, and let it bear the impression of the spoon
8. Sprinkle a little powdered sugar over, and serve

title: Pirmy-Unu Helivassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of the best fresh butter
- a pound and a half of rice-flour
- a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds
- a pint and a half of boiling syrup
- a pint and a half of boiling milk
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put in a stewpan a pound of the best fresh butter, and scald it, then remove it from the fire
2. When a little cold, add by degrees a pound and a half of rice-flour, set it again on the fire, and keep stirring for twenty-five minutes, or until the butter appears on the surface of the flour, and the flour swells up like grains of gunpowder
3. Add a quarter of a pound of skinned almonds, and stir it for a few minutes longer
4. Immediately add a pint and a half of boiling syrup, previously prepared of white sugar and water, as in No. 159, and the same quantity of boiling milk
5. Instantly put the cover over, and let it remain for about five minutes
6. Stir it well with a wooden fork, dish it up in spoonfuls, and let it bear the impression of the spoon
7. Sprinkle a little powdered sugar over, and serve

title: Piyazli-Kdbak
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight small-sized vegetable-marrows
- cheese, grated
- one or two tablespoonfuls of chopped parsley
- half a tablespoonful of mint chopped
- three or four eggs
- hot fresh butter
- broth

## Directions

1. Peel very thin seven or eight small-sized vegetable-marrows and take out the insides
2. Stuff them with some nice cheese, grated and mixed with one or two tablespoonfuls of chopped parsley, and half a tablespoonful of mint chopped
3. Beat up three or four eggs, in which dip the vegetable-marrows, and fry them a nice brown in hot fresh butter
4. Take them out and place them in a stewpan, cover them with broth
5. Set the pan on a charcoal fire, and let it boil gently, until the liquor is nearly absorbed
6. Dish up, and serve

title: Piyazli-Kdhak
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight small-sized vegetable-marrows
- nice cheese, grated
- one or two tablespoonfuls of chopped parsley
- half a tablespoonful of mint chopped
- three or four eggs
- hot fresh butter
- broth

## Directions

1. Peel very thin seven or eight small-sized vegetable-marrows and take out the insides
2. Stuff them with some nice cheese, grated and mixed with one or two tablespoonfuls of chopped parsley, and half a tablespoonful of mint chopped
3. Beat up three or four eggs, in which dip the vegetable-marrows
4. Fry them a nice brown in hot fresh butter
5. Take them out and place them in a stewpan
6. Cover them with broth
7. Set the pan on a charcoal fire, and let it boil gently, until the liquor is nearly absorbed
8. Dish up, and serve

title: Portakal Elmassiyyessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six nice oranges
- five or six ounces of powdered sugar
- half a pint of water
- a small teacup of water
- an ounce of isinglass
- a few drops of prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Get six nice oranges, take off the rind of one of them, and remove the pith, place them in a basin, and squeeze the juice of the oranges in it
2. Put five or six ounces of powdered sugar in a saucepan with half a pint of water, and boil it until it becomes a syrup
3. Take it off, and add the juice and the rind of the oranges; put the saucepan again on the fire with the cover over
4. As soon as it begins to boil, skim it well, and add gradually a small teacup of water; let it boil one or two minutes longer
5. Add an ounce of isinglass, dissolved as directed; stir well, and pass it through a jelly-bag till clear
6. Add a few drops of prepared cochineal, and pour it in a mould; place it on ice until firm
7. Turn it out as before, and serve

title: Portakali Elmassiyyessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six nice oranges
- five or six ounces of powdered sugar
- half a pint of water
- a small teacup of water
- an ounce of isinglass
- a few drops of prepared cochineal

## Directions

1. Get six nice oranges, take off the rind of one of them, and remove the pith, place them in a basin
2. Squeeze the juice of the oranges in it
3. Put five or six ounces of powdered sugar in a saucepan with half a pint of water, and boil it until it becomes a syrup
4. Take it off, and add the juice and the rind of the oranges
5. Put the saucepan again on the fire with the cover over
6. As soon as it begins to boil, skim it well, and add gradually a small teacup of water
7. Let it boil one or two minutes longer, then add an ounce of isinglass, dissolved as directed
8. Stir well, and pass it through a jelly-bag till clear
9. Add a few drops of prepared cochineal, and pour it in a mould
10. Place it on ice until firm, then turn it out and serve

title: Reward
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and a quarter of the best fresh butter
- twelve eggs
- one pound and a quarter of powdered sugar
- two pounds and two ounces of sago
- three quarters of a pound of loaf sugar
- seven pints of water

## Directions

1. Put in a basin a pound and a quarter of the best fresh butter, and work it well with clean hands until it is like a cream
2. Break in twelve eggs, and beat up well together
3. Mix in by degrees one pound and a quarter of powdered sugar
4. While beating up, add gradually two pounds and two ounces of sago, and keep beating it up for a few minutes longer
5. Butter a rather deep baking-tin, in which pour the preparation
6. Bake it a nice colour in a hot oven
7. Dissolve three quarters of a pound of loaf sugar in seven pints of water, and keep it lukewarm
8. When the pudding is nicely browned, pour gradually some of the sherbet or sugar and water all over with a ladle
9. Put it again in the oven until the sherbet is absorbed, and continue in this manner until you have used the whole of the sherbet
10. Cut it up in diamonds, dish up tastefully, shake some powdered sugar over, and serve

title: Rizdki Uzumu Khdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of raisins
- two or three pints of water
- powdered sugar
- a few drops of any essence of flowers
- some pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Take off the stalks of a pound of raisins and wash them
2. Place them in a stewpan with two or three pints of water
3. Set it to boil until they are quite swollen up
4. Add some powdered sugar according to palate and boil them for one or two minutes longer
5. Take it off and let it cool
6. Pour all together into a glass bowl
7. Add a few drops of any essence of flowers with some pieces of ice
8. Serve

title: Sach-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two pounds of flour
- a little salt
- sufficient water
- finely-minced raw beef or mutton
- a small quantity of onions
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put in a basin one or two pounds of flour, and a little salt, mix it with sufficient water into a softish paste with your hands
2. Dry it off a little with flour till you have well cleared the paste from the basin, and let it remain for two or three minutes
3. Cut it into ten or twelve pieces, and roll them round with a rolling-pin the size of a dinner-plate, and as thin as a wafer
4. Lay some finely-minced raw beef or mutton, mixed with a small quantity of onions chopped very fine, a little salt, pepper, and cinnamon over one half of each piece, half a quarter of an inch thick, and double the other half over
5. Press the two edges with your fingers to make the paste stick
6. Put a trevet on a charcoal fire, over which place a slightly-domed sheet-iron, the under side of which is previously cemented with ashes, to prevent it from getting fiercely hot
7. When moderately hot, dip a paste-brush in fresh butter, previously melted, and rub it all over
8. Place the pieces of paste over, and bake them a nice brown on both sides, now and then buttering them on both sides with the paste-brush
9. When done, take them off with a slice, dish them up one over the other, and serve hot

title: Samun Balıglı Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- bay-leaf
- lemons

## Directions

1. Get sufficient quantity of salmon in one piece, remove the bones, and cut it in pieces the size of an egg
2. Sprinkle sufficient salt and pepper over, with a handful or two of parsley chopped fine, and let them remain for about an hour
3. Pass them on skewers with a bay-leaf between each piece
4. Put them before a moderate fire, and turn them until the fish is nicely browned
5. Dish it up tastefully, with some parsley and one or two lemons cut in quarters round it, and serve hot

title: Samun-Ballighi Kyulbasstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- salt
- bay-leaves
- lemon
- nuts or almonds
- garlic
- bread-crumb
- olive oil
- wine vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Cut from the tail end of a small salmon as many thin slices as are wanted, and rub them with a little salt
2. Cover a gridiron with some bay-leaves, lay the slices of fish over, set it on a moderate charcoal fire
3. When nicely browned on both sides, dish it up
4. For sauce: crack four or five dozen of nuts or almonds, skin them, then pound them well in a mortar with one or two cloves of garlic peeled
5. Add a piece of bread-crumb the size of a large egg, previously soaked in water, a little salt, two or three teaspoonfuls of olive oil
6. Pound them altogether till they form a paste, then moisten it with some lemon-juice or wine vinegar, and some water, to form a smooth sauce, but not too thick
7. Pour it over the fish and serve hot

title: Sardela Salatassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines
- vinegar
- fresh vinegar
- parsley, chopped
- onions, chopped
- olives
- salt
- wine vinegar or lemon juice
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Put as many sardines as are wanted in a basin, with some vinegar, and let them remain in it for half an hour
2. Scrape off the scales gently and wash them in fresh vinegar
3. Arrange them round the edge of a dish
4. Put in the centre two or three handfuls of chopped parsley, two or three tablespoonfuls of chopped onions, and one or two dozens of olives, all mixed together
5. Put half a teaspoonful of salt in a basin, with two or three tablespoonfuls of the best wine vinegar, or the juice of two lemons, and about half a teacup of the best olive oil
6. Beat up well with a wooden fork or spoon until it becomes like cream
7. Pour it over the sardines and the parsley, and serve

title: Sardela Salatası
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines
- vinegar
- fresh vinegar
- parsley
- onions
- olives
- salt
- wine vinegar
- lemon juice
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Put as many sardines as are wanted in a basin with some vinegar and let them remain in it for half an hour
2. Scrape off the scales gently and wash them in fresh vinegar
3. Arrange them round the edge of a dish
4. Put in the centre two or three handfuls of chopped parsley, two or three tablespoonfuls of chopped onions, and one or two dozens of olives, all mixed together
5. Put half a teaspoonful of salt in a basin with two or three tablespoonfuls of the best wine vinegar or the juice of two lemons and about half a teacup of the best olive oil
6. Beat up well with a wooden fork or spoon until it becomes like cream
7. Pour it over the sardines and the parsley and serve

title: Sarimsakli Yahni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- cummin
- onions
- fresh butter
- wine vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Cut some mutton into pieces the size of walnuts
2. Peel a sufficient quantity of garlic
3. Mix two teaspoonfuls of salt, one of pepper, and one of cummin
4. Lay seven or eight cloves of garlic on the bottom of a stewpan
5. Sprinkle a pinch of the seasoning
6. Add some pieces of mutton, season again, then garlic, then mutton, and so forth, until the whole is laid
7. Add two or three sliced onions, fried in fresh butter
8. Add a teacup and a half of wine vinegar, and three of water
9. Set it on a charcoal fire, and let it simmer until the meat is nicely browned and tender
10. Dish up, and serve

title: Sarimsákli Ydhni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- cummin
- onions
- fresh butter
- wine vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Cut some mutton into pieces the size of walnuts
2. Peel a sufficient quantity of garlic
3. Mix two teaspoonfuls of salt, one of pepper, and one of cummin
4. Lay seven or eight cloves of garlic on the bottom of a stewpan
5. Sprinkle a pinch of the seasoning, then add some pieces of mutton
6. Season again, then garlic, then mutton, and so forth, until the whole is laid
7. Add two or three sliced onions, fried in fresh butter
8. Add a teacup and a half of wine vinegar, and three of water
9. Set it on a charcoal fire, and let it simmer until the meat is nicely browned and tender
10. Dish up, and serve

title: Sbmun Balighi Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon in one piece
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- bay-leaf
- lemon

## Directions

1. Remove the bones and cut the salmon in pieces the size of an egg
2. Sprinkle sufficient salt and pepper over, with a handful or two of parsley chopped fine
3. Let them remain for about an hour
4. Pass them on skewers with a bay-leaf between each piece
5. Put them before a moderate fire and turn them until the fish is nicely browned
6. Dish it up tastefully, with some parsley and one or two lemons cut in quarters round it
7. Serve hot

title: Sbmun-Bellighi Kyulbasstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- small salmon
- salt
- bay-leaves
- lemon
- nuts or almonds
- garlic
- bread-crumb
- water
- olive oil
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut from the tail end of a small salmon as many thin slices as are wanted
2. Rub them with a little salt
3. Cover a gridiron with some bay-leaves
4. Lay the slices of fish over
5. Set it on a moderate charcoal fire
6. When nicely browned on both sides, dish it up
7. Squeeze a lemon over, or prepare the following sauce: crack four or five dozen of nuts or almonds, skin them, then pound them well in a mortar with one or two cloves of garlic peeled
8. Add a piece of bread-crumb the size of a large egg, previously soaked in water, a little salt, two or three teaspoonfuls of olive oil
9. Pound them altogether till they form a paste
10. Moisten it with some lemon-juice or wine vinegar, and some water, to form a smooth sauce, but not too thick
11. Pour it over the fish instead of lemon and serve hot

title: Sbuniye Helwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of fresh butter
- a pound of loaf sugar
- water
- half a pound of wheat-starch

## Directions

1. Put a pound of fresh butter in a stewpan with a pound of loaf sugar, previously dissolved in a small quantity of water
2. Set it on a charcoal fire
3. When boiling, add by degrees half a pound of wheat-starch, which you have previously made a batter with a pint of water
4. Keep stirring and tasting it now and then
5. When it does not stick to the teeth, it is done
6. Dish up and serve

title: Sdghtin-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six onions
- a little salt
- some water
- fresh butter
- three or four eggs
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan five or six chopped onions, a little salt, and some water, set it on the fire, and boil it for a few minutes
2. Drain the onions, and put them in a fryingpan with some fresh butter
3. Set the pan on the fire, and stew the onions until the moisture is dispersed
4. Beat up three or four eggs, and add it by degrees to the onions
5. Put it on the fire again, and stir it for three minutes or so with a wooden spoon
6. Take it off, and lay it between pieces of paste as No. 86, and bake it a nice colour
7. Dish up and serve

title: Sdghtin-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six chopped onions
- a little salt
- some water
- fresh butter
- three or four eggs
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan five or six chopped onions, a little salt, and some water, set it on the fire, and boil it for a few minutes
2. Drain the onions, and put them in a fryingpan with some fresh butter
3. Set the pan on the fire, and stew the onions until the moisture is dispersed
4. Beat up three or four eggs, and add it by degrees to the onions
5. Put it on the fire again, and stir it for three minutes or so with a wooden spoon
6. Take it off, and lay it between pieces of paste as No. 86, and bake it a nice colour
7. Dish up and serve

title: Serai Lhmmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of plain white biscuit
- whites of twenty eggs
- fresh butter
- a pound and a quarter of loaf sugar
- water
- prepared cochineal
- essence of orange-flower or rose

## Directions

1. Pound in a mortar half a pound of plain white biscuit and sift it through a fine sieve
2. Beat up the whites of twenty eggs in a basin
3. Add by degrees the powdered biscuit and beat it up together until it forms a smooth consistency
4. Take it up a dessertspoonful at a time and pour it into boiling fresh butter one after the other
5. Fry them a nice delicate brown; they should be round and swell up to the size of a peach
6. When done, drain and place them in a stewpan half full of boiling water
7. Let them boil for ten minutes or so
8. Take it off and strain the water from them
9. Dissolve a pound and a quarter of loaf sugar in as much water as will cover them
10. Add a few drops of prepared cochineal with a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or rose and stir it well
11. Pour it over them and set the pan on a charcoal fire with the cover over
12. Let it boil until the syrup is nearly absorbed
13. Dish up and serve hot or cold

title: Seraii Ldhmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of plain white biscuit
- whites of twenty eggs
- fresh butter
- a pound and a quarter of loaf sugar
- water
- prepared cochineal
- essence of orange-flower or rose

## Directions

1. Pound half a pound of plain white biscuit in a mortar and sift it through a fine sieve
2. Beat up the whites of twenty eggs in a basin
3. Add by degrees the powdered biscuit and beat it up together until it forms a smooth consistency
4. Take it up a dessertspoonful at a time and pour it into boiling fresh butter, one after the other
5. Fry them a nice delicate brown until they are round and swell up to the size of a peach
6. When done, drain and place them in a stewpan half full of boiling water
7. Let them boil for ten minutes
8. Take it off and strain the water from them
9. Dissolve a pound and a quarter of loaf sugar in as much water as will cover them
10. Add a few drops of prepared cochineal and a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or rose
11. Stir it well and pour it over them
12. Set the pan on a charcoal fire with the cover over and let it boil until the syrup is nearly absorbed
13. Dish up and serve hot or cold

title: Serciy Ashicrasi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- skinned wheat
- water
- two or three ounces of washed currants
- a quarter or half a pound of loaf sugar
- a little musk
- two or three tablespoonfuls of rose-water
- skinned almonds
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Put in a stewpan as much skinned wheat as is required, with plenty of water, set it on the fire, and boil until the grains begin to crack
2. Take out with a wooden spoon what pulp there is on the top, and put it in a basin, add some more water, and continue so until you have extracted all the goodness from the grains
3. Strain the remaining grains through a sieve, and put all the pulp you have collected into a saucepan
4. Add two or three ounces of washed currants, and a quarter or half a pound of loaf sugar, previously made into syrup
5. Put it on a charcoal fire and begin stirring it
6. When boiling, add a little musk mixed with two or three tablespoonfuls of rose-water, and stir it one or two minutes longer
7. Take it off and pour it into a dish, arrange on the top tastefully some skinned almonds and pistachios, and serve when cold

title: Serciy Ashicressi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- skinned wheat
- water
- two or three ounces of washed currants
- a quarter or half a pound of loaf sugar
- a little musk
- two or three tablespoonfuls of rose-water
- skinned almonds
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Put in a stewpan as much skinned wheat as is required, with plenty of water.
2. Set it on the fire, and boil until the grains begin to crack.
3. Take out with a wooden spoon what pulp there is on the top, and put it in a basin.
4. Add some more water, and continue so until you have extracted all the goodness from the grains.
5. Strain the remaining grains through a sieve, and put all the pulp you have collected into a saucepan.
6. Add two or three ounces of washed currants, and a quarter or half a pound of loaf sugar, previously made into syrup.
7. Put it on a charcoal fire and begin stirring it.
8. When boiling, add a little musk mixed with two or three tablespoonfuls of rose-water, and stir it one or two minutes longer.
9. Take it off and pour it into a dish.
10. Arrange on the top tastefully some skinned almonds and pistachios, and serve when cold.

title: Serdy Tel-Kaddyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- butter
- water
- syrup

## Directions

1. Make three or four pounds of vermicelli as No. 149
2. Lay it in a rather deep baking-tin, about two inches thick
3. Nearly cover it with scalded fresh butter
4. Bake it a nice colour on both sides in the oven
5. When done, strain the butter
6. Pour hot water over to wash out all the fat
7. Pour hot syrup over, as No. 149
8. Put it again in the oven for five minutes or so
9. Cut it in four crossways, or in any fanciful shape you please
10. Dish up and serve hot or cold

title: Serdy Tel-Kaddyifi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- vermicelli
- butter
- water
- syrup

## Directions

1. Make three or four pounds of vermicelli as No. 149
2. Lay it in a rather deep baking-tin, about two inches thick
3. Nearly cover it with scalded fresh butter
4. Bake it a nice colour on both sides in the oven
5. When done, strain the butter
6. Pour hot water over to wash out all the fat
7. Pour hot syrup over as No. 149
8. Put it again in the oven for five minutes or so
9. Cut it in four crossways, or in any fanciful shape you please
10. Dish up and serve hot or cold

title: Set-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pints of milk
- five or six eggs
- six ounces of flour
- salt
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Pour three pints of milk in a basin
2. Add five or six eggs, six ounces of flour, and a small teaspoonful of salt
3. Beat up well together
4. Well butter a convenient baking-tin
5. Pour in the preparation, and bake it a nice brown
6. When done, cut it in diamonds, and serve

title: Shalghdm Dolmessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen middle-sized turnips
- raw mutton
- two onions
- chopped parsley
- a handful of well-cleaned rice
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar
- one tablespoonful of honey or powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Peel half a dozen middle-sized turnips, and hollow them out
2. Mince sufficient raw mutton, add two finely chopped onions, a little chopped parsley, a handful of well-cleaned rice, and a little salt and pepper
3. Mix well all together with the hands
4. Stuff the turnips with it, and place the lids over
5. Place them in a stewpan, and nearly cover them with broth
6. Add a little salt
7. Put the pan on the fire with the cover over, and let it simmer until the turnips are nearly soft
8. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar, and one of honey or powdered sugar
9. When sufficient gravy is left, and the turnips are quite done, dish up tastefully, and serve

title: Shalghdm Dolmessy
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen middle-sized turnips
- raw mutton
- two onions
- chopped parsley
- a handful of well-cleaned rice
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar
- one tablespoonful of honey or powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Peel half a dozen middle-sized turnips and hollow them out exactly as No. 112
2. Mince sufficient raw mutton and add two finely chopped onions, a little chopped parsley, a handful of well-cleaned rice, and a little salt and pepper
3. Mix well all together with the hands
4. Stuff the turnips with it and place the lids over
5. Place them in a stewpan and nearly cover them with broth
6. Add a little salt
7. Put the pan on the fire with the cover over and let it simmer until the turnips are nearly soft
8. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar and one of honey or powdered sugar
9. When sufficient gravy is left and the turnips are quite done, dish up tastefully and serve

title: Shariys Chbrbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pints of broth
- hearts and livers of several chickens, half cooked
- salt
- five or six ounces of mixed vermicelli
- white pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put three or four pints of broth in a stewpan with the hearts and the livers of several chickens, half cooked and cut into small dice, and a little salt
2. Put it on the fire
3. When boiling, add five or six ounces of mixed vermicelli, and let it boil till tender
4. Remove the pan, and pour the contents in a tureen
5. Sprinkle a little white pepper and cinnamon over, and serve

title: Shariys Chorbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four pints of broth
- hearts and livers of several chickens, half cooked
- a little salt
- five or six ounces of mixed vermicelli
- white pepper
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put three or four pints of broth in a stewpan with the hearts and the livers of several chickens, half cooked and cut into small dice, and a little salt
2. Put it on the fire
3. When boiling, add five or six ounces of mixed vermicelli, and let it boil till tender
4. Remove the pan, and pour the contents in a tureen
5. Sprinkle a little white pepper and cinnamon over, and serve

title: Sheftali Tatlusi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight rather unripe peaches
- half a pound of loaf sugar
- a pint of water

## Directions

1. Peel seven or eight rather unripe peaches
2. Divide each one in two and remove the stones and the red parts where the stones have been
3. Arrange them in a stewpan
4. Put in a saucepan half a pound of loaf sugar with a pint of water
5. Set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
6. Pour it over the peaches
7. Put the pan on the fire till they are tender
8. Place them on a dish tastefully, pour the syrup over, and serve

title: Sheftdli Tdtlussi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven or eight rather unripe peaches
- Half a pound of loaf sugar
- A pint of water

## Directions

1. Peel seven or eight rather unripe peaches
2. Divide each one in two and remove the stones and the red parts where the stones have been
3. Arrange them in a stewpan
4. Put in a saucepan half a pound of loaf sugar with a pint of water
5. Set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
6. Pour it over the peaches
7. Put the pan on the fire till they are tender
8. Place them on a dish tastefully
9. Pour the syrup over and serve

title: Sheilch-ul-Mushi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or eight egg-plants, not seeded
- two or three ounces of fresh butter
- one or two pounds of minced raw mutton
- three or four onions, finely chopped
- two or three tablespoonfuls of parsley, chopped fine
- two or three tablespoonfuls of mint
- salt and pepper
- parsley stalks
- peeled parsley roots
- mutton broth or broth (No. 1)
- curd, flavoured with a little garlic

## Directions

1. Cut in four crossways, without separating from the stalk, six or eight egg-plants, not seeded
2. Cut out part of the inside of each one, and throw it away, and put the egg-plants in brine
3. Put in a fryingpan two or three ounces of fresh butter, one or two pounds of minced raw mutton, three or four finely chopped onions, and fry them a nice brown
4. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of parsley, chopped fine, the same of mint, sufficient salt and pepper, and mix them well together
5. Stuff the egg-plants with this, and tie them up separately with the stalks of parsley
6. Cover the bottom of a stewpan with some peeled parsley roots, in which lay the stuffed egg-plants, and cover them with mutton broth, or with the broth (No. 1)
7. Set the pan on the fire with the cover over
8. When boiling, let it simmer until a nice thick gravy is formed at the bottom
9. Dish them up tastefully one over the other, and serve, or dress them with some curd, flavoured with a little garlic, if approved of, then serve

title: Sheilch-ul-Mushi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or eight egg-plants, not seeded
- two or three ounces of fresh butter
- one or two pounds of minced raw mutton
- three or four finely chopped onions
- two or three tablespoonfuls of parsley, chopped fine
- the same of mint
- sufficient salt and pepper
- stalks of parsley
- peeled parsley roots
- mutton broth, or broth (No. 1)
- curd (optional)
- a little garlic (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut in four crossways, without separating from the stalk, six or eight egg-plants, not seeded
2. Cut out part of the inside of each one, and throw it away, and put the egg-plants in brine
3. Put in a frying pan two or three ounces of fresh butter, one or two pounds of minced raw mutton, three or four finely chopped onions, and fry them a nice brown
4. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of parsley, chopped fine, the same of mint, sufficient salt and pepper, and mix them well together
5. Stuff the egg-plants with this, and tie them up separately with the stalks of parsley
6. Cover the bottom of a stewpan with some peeled parsley roots, in which lay the stuffed egg-plants, and cover them with mutton broth, or with the broth (No. 1)
7. Set the pan on the fire with the cover over
8. When boiling, let it simmer until a nice thick gravy is formed at the bottom
9. Dish them up tastefully one over the other, and serve, or dress them with some curd, flavoured with a little garlic if approved of, then serve

title: Shhlghctm Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- powdered mustard-seeds
- sugar
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel and cut turnips in four crosswise
2. Boil them in plenty of water till nearly tender
3. Take out the turnips into a colander
4. Put a moderate quantity of powdered mustard-seeds in the water and let it remain for two or three hours
5. Pass it through a cloth or a sieve into a basin
6. Sweeten it just sufficient to taste the sugar
7. Place the turnips in a stone jar
8. Half cover them with the sweetened water and half with nice wine vinegar
9. Put the cover over the jar and let it remain a few days before using

title: Shish Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five pounds of loin or leg of mutton
- four middle-sized onions
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- half a dozen tomatos or four or five tablespoonfuls of preserved tomato sauce
- water

## Directions

1. Cut five pounds of loin or leg of mutton in pieces the size of an egg
2. Chop four middle-sized onions, and sprinkle them over the meat with sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon
3. Rub them in well with the hands, and let it remain covered for two or three hours
4. If in season, squeeze half a dozen tomatos, and pass the juice through a sieve into a basin; if not, mix four or five tablespoonfuls of preserved tomato sauce in three quarters of a pint of water
5. Pass the pieces of meat one by one on the skewers, place them in front of the fire, and gradually turn them
6. As soon as the meat begins to brown, baste it now and then with the tomato juice
7. When nicely cooked, dish it up, and serve hot

title: Sighir-eti Ydhni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- rump-steak
- string
- garlic
- fresh butter
- potatoes
- ripe tomatoes
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut five or six pounds of rump-steak into rather long slices, half or three-quarters of an inch thick
2. Tie them together one over the other with a piece of string, as if one piece of meat
3. With the point of a knife, make about fifteen holes here and there, and stick in each a clove of garlic peeled
4. Place it in a stewpan with about three-quarters of a pound of fresh butter, set it on a moderate charcoal fire with the cover over for about three hours, turning the meat every half hour
5. Add two or three pounds of sliced potatoes partly fried in butter, and the juice of two or three pounds of ripe tomatoes with a little water, and sufficient salt and pepper
6. Put the cover over the pan and let it simmer until the meat and the potatoes are well done
7. Cut the string off the meat, dish up tastefully, and serve

title: Sikenjebin
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- white of an egg
- three pounds of powdered sugar
- three-quarters of a pint of the best white-wine vinegar
- a small bunch of sweet-marjoram

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan the white of an egg, well beaten up, three pounds of powdered sugar, and pour in all at once three-quarters of a pint of the best white-wine vinegar
2. Stir it once round with a wooden spoon, set it on a charcoal fire, and boil it for half a minute
3. Skim and take it off, and instantly put in a small bunch of sweet-marjoram tied up in a piece of muslin
4. Put the cover over the pan, and leave it until cold
5. Then pass it through a clean cloth, bottle and cork it up until wanted

title: Sikenjebin
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- white of an egg
- three pounds of powdered sugar
- three-quarters of a pint of the best white-wine vinegar
- sweet-marjoram

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan the white of an egg, well beaten up, three pounds of powdered sugar
2. Pour in all at once three-quarters of a pint of the best white-wine vinegar
3. Stir it once round with a wooden spoon
4. Set it on a charcoal fire, and boil it for half a minute, then skim and take it off
5. Instantly put in a small bunch of sweet-marjoram tied up in a piece of muslin
6. Put the cover over the pan, and leave it until cold
7. Pass it through a clean cloth, bottle and cork it up until wanted

title: Silt Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg or loin of mutton or lamb
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut a sufficient quantity of a leg or loin of mutton or lamb in pieces the size of a small egg
2. Sprinkle with your fingers sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon over, and let them remain for an hour or two
3. Place them in a stewpan, and cover them with milk
4. Set it on the fire until the milk begins to boil, then take it off the fire, skim it well, and take the pieces of meat out
5. When cold, pass them on skewers one by one, put them before a moderate fire, and turn them gently
6. Get ready some fresh milk, and keep it hot by the side of the fire
7. As soon as the meat commences to brown, baste it now and then with the hot milk, with a quill
8. When the gravy drops freely, sprinkle a small quantity of flour over the meat
9. Continue the same till it is nicely cooked
10. Dish it up tastefully, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve hot

title: Skt-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pints of milk
- five or six eggs
- six ounces of flour
- salt
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Pour three pints of milk in a basin
2. Add five or six eggs, six ounces of flour, and a small teaspoonful of salt
3. Beat up well together
4. Well butter a convenient baking-tin, pour in the preparation, and bake it a nice brown
5. When done, cut it in diamonds, and serve

title: Su-Bureglii
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- eggs
- fresh butter
- minced meat or cheese

## Directions

1. Put on the dresser, or in a basin, sufficient quantity of flour, with a little salt
2. Mix it up with as many eggs as it will take, and form it a softish flexible paste
3. Cut it in twelve or fourteen pieces, and roll them out the size of the baking-tin intended for laying them in
4. Fill a saucepan or iron pot with water, put in sufficient salt, and set it on the fire
5. When boiling, scald the pieces of paste one by one, except two
6. When done, drain them, then dry them with a piece of clean cloth, and spread out the wrinkled parts carefully
7. Put some fresh butter in a saucepan, and set it on the fire
8. When the butter is at proper heat, take it off, and when rather cold, butter the baking-tin with it
9. In the baking-tin lay one of the two unscalded pieces of paste, sprinkle a tablespoonful of the butter over
10. Lay half of the scalded pieces, sprinkling between each one a spoonful of butter
11. Lay minced meat or cheese all over, half an inch thick
12. Cover it with the remaining pieces of paste that are scalded, sprinkling some butter between each one as before
13. Lay the other unscalded piece over, well butter the top, and place it in a hot oven
14. When nicely browned, cut it in any shape you choose, dish up, and serve hot

title: Sungheriye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- white of forty eggs
- three-quarters of a pound of ground rice
- a few drops of the essence of rose, or any other essence
- five or six pints of milk
- a tablespoonful of the mixture
- sugar and water for syrup

## Directions

1. Put in a basin the white of forty eggs, and beat them up until they are formed into a firm froth
2. Add by degrees three-quarters of a pound of ground rice, with a few drops of the essence of rose, or any other essence you fancy
3. Beat it up again for some time
4. Put into a saucepan five or six pints of milk, and set it on a charcoal fire
5. When boiling, throw in a tablespoonful of the mixture at a time, which will be done in a few minutes
6. Take it out with a hand-strainer, and throw it into boiling syrup, which you have previously prepared, of sugar and water
7. Take it out again with the hand-strainer after a few seconds, and place it in a dish
8. Continue in this manner until the whole is done, and serve

title: Sungheriye
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- white of forty eggs
- three-quarters of a pound of ground rice
- a few drops of the essence of rose or any other essence
- five or six pints of milk
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Put in a basin the white of forty eggs, and beat them up until they are formed into a firm froth
2. Add by degrees three-quarters of a pound of ground rice, with a few drops of the essence of rose, or any other essence you fancy, and beat it up again for some time
3. Put into a saucepan five or six pints of milk, and set it on a charcoal fire
4. When boiling, throw in a tablespoonful of the mixture at a time, which will be done in a few minutes
5. Take it out with a hand-strainer, and throw it into boiling syrup, which you have previously prepared, of sugar and water
6. Take it out again with the hand-strainer after a few seconds, and place it in a dish
7. Continue in this manner until the whole is done, and serve

title: Sussiiz Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of mutton or lamb
- sufficient salt and pepper
- two pounds and a half of rice
- half a pound of hot fresh butter
- half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic
- two or three pieces of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of mutton or lamb into pieces the size of walnuts, wash them, sprinkle them with sufficient salt and pepper, and lay them on the bottom of a stewpan with a tight-fitting cover.
2. Well wash two pounds and a half of rice, and place it over the meat.
3. Pour half a pound of hot fresh butter over, add half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic, two or three pieces of cinnamon, put the cover over the pan contrariwise, wrap a wet cloth round it, and fill the cover with water.
4. Set the pan on a slow charcoal fire, and let it simmer for about four hours.
5. Take off the cover, and turn the contents carefully on to a hot dish, and serve.

title: Sussiiz Pilotw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of mutton or lamb
- sufficient salt and pepper
- two pounds and a half of rice
- half a pound of hot fresh butter
- half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic
- two or three pieces of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut two pounds of mutton or lamb into pieces the size of walnuts, wash them, sprinkle them with sufficient salt and pepper, and lay them on the bottom of a stewpan with a tight-fitting cover.
2. Well wash two pounds and a half of rice, and place it over the meat.
3. Pour half a pound of hot fresh butter over, add half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic, two or three pieces of cinnamon.
4. Put the cover over the pan contrariwise, wrap a wet cloth round it, and fill the cover with water.
5. Set the pan on a slow charcoal fire, and let it simmer for about four hours.
6. Take off the cover, and turn the contents carefully on to a hot dish, and serve.

title: Sussuz Kyufte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- raw mutton
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- onion juice
- a teacup of water
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter

## Directions

1. Mince very fine as much as is required of the best part of raw mutton
2. Sprinkle a little salt, pepper, and cinnamon over
3. Knead well with a little onion juice, and make it into balls the size of walnuts
4. Put in a bowl or basin a teacup of water, and a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
5. Arrange the balls of meat in the bowl, and place it into an iron pot
6. Put the cover over and paste it round, or wrap a wet cloth round it, so as to prevent the steam from escaping
7. Set it on a moderate fire, and let it simmer for about two hours
8. Dish it up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Sutlu-Ash
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces of the best rice
- two pints and three-quarters of nice milk
- powdered sugar
- rose-water (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash and scald six ounces of the best rice
2. Drain it
3. Pour into a stewpan two pints and three-quarters of nice milk
4. Set it on the fire
5. As soon as it commences to boil, add the rice
6. Let it simmer until quite soft
7. Take it off and pour it into a pie-dish
8. Put it on a very slow charcoal fire, covered with ashes, or on the hob
9. Let it remain for about fifteen minutes, during which time the top of the rice will be covered with cream
10. When cold, sprinkle sufficient powdered sugar over
11. Serve (You may sweeten the rice while boiling, and add one or two tablespoonfuls of rose-water)

title: Sâbuniye Helwdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of fresh butter
- a pound of loaf sugar
- water
- half a pound of wheat-starch

## Directions

1. Put a pound of fresh butter in a stewpan with a pound of loaf sugar previously dissolved in a small quantity of water
2. Set it on a charcoal fire
3. When boiling, add by degrees half a pound of wheat-starch which you have previously made a batter with a pint of water
4. Keep stirring and tasting it now and then
5. When it does not stick to the teeth, it is done
6. Dish up and serve

title: Taivuk-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- water
- salt
- onions
- fresh butter
- pepper
- paste

## Directions

1. Put a nice and well-cleaned fowl in a saucepan with some water and a little salt
2. Set it on the fire, and boil it till sufficiently tender
3. Take the fowl out, and when cold, remove the meat from the bones, and chop it up fine
4. Stew one or two finely-chopped onions in fresh butter until quite brown, but not burnt
5. Mix them well with the fowl, add a little salt and pepper
6. Butter a baking-tin, in which place ten very fine layers of paste one over the other, sprinkling a little butter between each one
7. Lay the minced fowl all over, and cover it with the same number of pastes as before
8. Cut it in four crossways, pour a little hot fresh butter over
9. Put it in the oven, bake it a nice brown, and serve hot

title: Tap-Marul Salatası
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage-lettuces
- beet-root
- eggs
- chervil
- tarragon
- chives or young onions
- salt
- pepper
- olive oil
- wine vinegar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Procure two or three cabbage-lettuces and remove the outer leaves and throw them away
2. Pull the others one by one then wash and well drain them in a cloth
3. Put them in the bowl with a few slices of beet-root and one or two hard-boiled eggs cut in quarters
4. Add two teaspoonfuls of chopped chervil, one of tarragon, and one of chives or young onions
5. Sprinkle over with your fingers half a teaspoonful of salt and one or two pinches of pepper
6. Sprinkle two or three tablespoonfuls of the best olive oil and one and-a-half of wine vinegar or the juice of a lemon
7. Turn the salad in the bowl with a spoon and fork until well mixed and serve

title: Tarhhctna Chbrbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds and a half of crumbs of French rolls
- three-quarters of a pound of curd
- broth
- bread
- fresh butter
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Put two pounds and a half of crumbs of French rolls in a stone mortar, with three-quarters of a pound of curd
2. Pound them well together, and let it remain for one night
3. Put it in a stewpan, and moisten it by degrees with as much broth as is required by bruising and beating it up smooth with a wooden spoon
4. Set it on a moderate fire, and let it simmer for about an hour
5. Take it off, and pour it into a tureen, and serve it with bread cut into small dice and fried in fresh butter
6. The addition of a small quantity of cream or milk will render it still more pleasant

title: Tarhhctna Chorbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds and a half of crumbs of French rolls
- three-quarters of a pound of curd
- broth
- bread
- fresh butter
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Put two pounds and a half of crumbs of French rolls in a stone mortar, with three-quarters of a pound of curd
2. Pound them well together, and let it remain for one night
3. Put it in a stewpan, and moisten it by degrees with as much broth as is required by bruising and beating it up smooth with a wooden spoon
4. Set it on a moderate fire, and let it simmer for about an hour
5. Take it off, and pour it into a tureen, and serve it with bread cut into small dice and fried in fresh butter
6. The addition of a small quantity of cream or milk will render it still more pleasant

title: Tartik Pilaví
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three dozen of small scallops
- six or seven ounces of fresh butter
- two or three finely-chopped onions
- a pound of the best rice, well washed
- salt
- pepper
- half a teacup of the best olive-oil
- water

## Directions

1. Procure two or three dozen of small scallops, scrape and well wash them, and open the shells without separating one from the other
2. Put six or seven ounces of fresh butter in a pan, with two or three finely-chopped onions
3. Set it on the fire, and fry them partly brown
4. Add a pound of the best rice, well washed, a little salt and pepper, and stir it with a wooden spoon for ten minutes or so
5. Stuff the scallops with the rice, and place them carefully in a convenient saucepan, with half a teacup of the best olive-oil
6. Cover them with water, put a plate over upside downwards, put the lid on, and let it simmer on a moderate fire until there is no liquid remaining
7. Arrange them in a dish, and serve, hot or cold

title: Tartik Pilayi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three dozen of small scallops
- six or seven ounces of fresh butter
- two or three finely-chopped onions
- a pound of the best rice, well washed
- a little salt and pepper
- half a teacup of the best olive-oil
- water

## Directions

1. Procure two or three dozen of small scallops, scrape and well wash them, and open the shells without separating one from the other
2. Put six or seven ounces of fresh butter in a pan, with two or three finely-chopped onions
3. Set it on the fire, and fry them partly brown
4. Add a pound of the best rice, well washed, a little salt and pepper, and stir it with a wooden spoon for ten minutes or so
5. Stuff the scallops with the rice, and place them carefully in a convenient saucepan, with half a teacup of the best olive-oil
6. Cover them with water, put a plate over upside downwards, put the lid on, and let it simmer on a moderate fire until there is no liquid remaining
7. Arrange them in a dish, and serve, hot or cold

title: Tatar-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- fried minced mutton or beef
- water
- scalded butter
- curd
- pepper

## Directions

1. Make some paste, as No. 83; roll out as many pieces as are wanted to the thickness of a sixpence
2. Cut them in checks or square pieces, about two inches long and two broad
3. Lay half a teaspoonful of fried minced mutton or beef on one corner of each piece, and fold the other half over, thus forming it of a triangular shape
4. Press the two edges with your fingers to make the paste stick
5. When the whole is ready, put on the fire a saucepan nearly full of water
6. When boiling, throw in the pieces of paste, and let them boil until nicely cooked, then drain them, and dish them up
7. Sprinkle a little scalded butter over, with some nice curd, and a pinch of pepper, and serve hot

title: Tatar-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- fried minced mutton or beef
- water
- scalded butter
- nice curd
- a pinch of pepper

## Directions

1. Make some paste as No. 83
2. Roll out as many pieces as are wanted to the thickness of a sixpence
3. Cut them in checks or square pieces, about two inches long and two broad
4. Lay half a teaspoonful of fried minced mutton or beef on one corner of each piece, and fold the other half over, thus forming it of a triangular shape
5. Press the two edges with your fingers to make the paste stick
6. When the whole is ready, put on the fire a saucepan nearly full of water
7. When boiling, throw in the pieces of paste, and let them boil until nicely cooked
8. Drain them, and dish them up
9. Sprinkle a little scalded butter over, with some nice curd, and a pinch of pepper, and serve hot

title: Tavuk-Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- water
- salt
- onions
- fresh butter
- pepper
- paste

## Directions

1. Put a nice and well-cleaned fowl in a saucepan with some water and a little salt
2. Set it on the fire, and boil it till sufficiently tender
3. Take the fowl out and when cold, remove the meat from the bones, and chop it up fine
4. Stew one or two finely-chopped onions in fresh butter until quite brown, but not burnt
5. Mix them well with the fowl and add a little salt and pepper
6. Butter a baking-tin, in which place ten very fine layers of paste one over the other, sprinkling a little butter between each one
7. Lay the minced fowl all over
8. Cover it with the same number of pastes as before
9. Cut it in four crossways, pour a little hot fresh butter over
10. Put it in the oven, bake it a nice brown, and serve hot

title: Tawshan Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- wine vinegar
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Procure a nice hare, clean it well, cut it into pieces, each about the size of a kidney
2. Put them in cold water, and let them remain for two or three hours
3. Take them out, and dry them in a cloth, season with sufficient salt and pepper, and let them remain for twenty minutes or so
4. Pass them on the skewers one by one, put them before a moderate fire
5. While they are turning, peel five or six cloves of garlic, and well pound them in a mortar with a little salt
6. Add a small teacup of wine vinegar, stir it well, pour it in a small saucepan, add a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
7. Place the saucepan on a moderate charcoal fire, and stir well with a wooden spoon until the butter is melted
8. Take off, and keep it hot

title: Tawshan Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- salt
- pepper
- cloves of garlic
- wine vinegar
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Procure a nice hare, clean it well, cut it into pieces, each about the size of a kidney
2. Put them in cold water, and let them remain for two or three hours
3. Take them out, and dry them in a cloth
4. Season with sufficient salt and pepper, and let them remain for twenty minutes or so
5. Pass them on the skewers one by one
6. Put them before a moderate fire
7. While they are turning, peel five or six cloves of garlic, and well pound them in a mortar with a little salt
8. Add a small teacup of wine vinegar, stir it well, pour it in a small saucepan, add a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
9. Place the saucepan on a moderate charcoal fire, and stir well with a wooden spoon until the butter is melted
10. Take off, and keep it hot

title: Tawshan Ycihnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- water
- wine vinegar
- garlic
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Cut a hare in pieces about the size of kidneys, wash them well, and let them remain for one or two hours, or until thoroughly cleansed from blood
2. Place them in a stewpan with five or six onions sliced thin, and sufficient salt and pepper
3. Cover them with water, set it on a charcoal fire till the hare is half cooked
4. Strain the liquor into a basin, and take out the onions
5. Put the liquor in a stewpan with a teacup and a half of wine vinegar, and eight or ten cloves of garlic peeled
6. Put it on the fire, and let it boil until the liquor is reduced to half
7. Fry the onions in fresh butter, and put them over the hare with the liquor
8. Set it on the fire, and let it simmer until a nice thick gravy is formed at the bottom
9. Dish it up, and serve

title: Tawuh Ydhni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- mutton
- water
- salt
- fresh butter
- onions
- chick-peas
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut a well-cleaned fowl in two lengthways
2. Place it in a stewpan with two or three pounds of the middle of a neck of mutton
3. Cover them with water, put in a little salt
4. Set it on the fire, and let it boil till the fowl is half cooked, and skim it well
5. Take the fowl out and fry it a nice colour in fresh butter
6. Place it in a stewpan with six or eight sliced onions fried in butter, a pint and a quarter of chick-peas, previously boiled a little soft and skinned, two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar, and the liquor in which the fowl is boiled
7. Add sufficient salt and pepper

title: Tawuk Kyul-Basstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- fowls
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- broth

## Directions

1. Split one or two nice fowls lengthways, and remove the whole of the bones with a sharp knife
2. Cut the flesh in four or five pieces as you fancy, and beat the thicker parts even with a rolling pin
3. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt and pepper, and a little cinnamon, and let them remain for one or two hours
4. Place them on a gridiron, set it on a moderate charcoal fire, and constantly turn them with a pair of tongs
5. When nicely browned, lay them in a stewpan, and cover them with broth
6. Put the pan on a moderate charcoal fire, and let simmer until tender
7. Dish up, and serve hot

title: Tawuk Pilawi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two nice chickens
- water
- a little salt
- three pints of chicken broth
- a pound and a half of rice, well washed
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic
- two or three pieces of cinnamon
- half a teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Place one or two nice chickens in a saucepan, and nearly fill it with water, add a little salt, and set it on the fire.
2. When boiling, skim and simmer until the chickens are tender.
3. Take them out, place them in a dish, and keep them hot.
4. Measure three pints of the broth of the chickens, and pour it in a pan, to which add a pound and a half of rice, well washed, a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic, two or three pieces of cinnamon, and half a teaspoonful of salt.
5. Put the pan on the fire with the cover over, and let it boil gently until the liquor is absorbed.
6. Take it off, stir it once round with a fork, and keep it on the hob with the cover over for fifteen or twenty minutes.
7. Take out the pieces of cinnamon.
8. Dish up the rice, in the centre of which place the chickens, cut in pieces, and serve very hot.

title: Tawuk Suyu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three well-cleaned fowls
- sufficient salt
- one or two onions
- half a stick of celery
- five or six pints of water

## Directions

1. Put the fowls in a stewpan with sufficient salt, one or two onions cut cross-wise, half a stick of celery, and five or six pints of water
2. Put the pan on the fire
3. As soon as boiling commences, skim it well
4. Let it simmer till the fowls are quite tender
5. Pass the broth through a sieve

title: Tbwuk Pilbwi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two nice chickens
- water
- a little salt
- three pints of the broth of the chickens
- a pound and a half of rice, well washed
- a quarter of a pound of fresh butter
- half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic
- two or three pieces of cinnamon
- half a teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Place one or two nice chickens in a saucepan, and nearly fill it with water, add a little salt, and set it on the fire.
2. When boiling, skim and simmer until the chickens are tender.
