3. Take them out, place them in a dish, and keep them hot.
4. Measure three pints of the broth of the chickens, and pour it in a pan.
5. Add a pound and a half of rice well washed, a quarter of a pound of fresh butter, half a dozen pieces of gum-mastic, two or three pieces of cinnamon, and half a teaspoonful of salt.
6. Put the pan on the fire with the cover over, and let it boil gently until the liquor is absorbed.
7. Take it off, stir it once round with a fork, and keep it on the hob with the cover over for fifteen or twenty minutes.
8. Take out the pieces of cinnamon.
9. Dish up the rice, in the centre of which place the chickens cut in pieces, and serve very hot.

title: Tciwuk Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- flour
- paper

## Directions

1. Clean a chicken, or as many as are required
2. Sprinkle sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon over, and let it remain for one or two hours
3. Put it on the spit, and place it before the fire
4. When the gravy begins to drop, sprinkle a little flour over, and continue the same till the chicken is just upon being done
5. Wrap it up with some thin paper, and place it again before the fire for twenty minutes or so
6. Take the paper off, and serve hot

title: Tciwuk Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean a chicken, or as many as are required
2. Sprinkle sufficient salt, pepper, and cinnamon over, and let it remain for one or two hours
3. Put it on the spit, and place it before the fire
4. When the gravy begins to drop, sprinkle a little flour over, and continue the same till the chicken is just upon being done
5. Wrap it up with some thin paper, and place it again before the fire for twenty minutes or so
6. Take the paper off, and serve hot

title: Tdss Kebdbi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- leg or loin of mutton
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- mixed spice
- broth
- rice

## Directions

1. Cut sufficient quantity of a leg or loin of mutton in pieces the size of small walnuts
2. Wash them with cold water, and dry them in a cloth
3. Sprinkle two or three finely-chopped onions over, with sufficient salt, pepper, and mixed spice
4. Rub them well in the meat, and let it remain covered for about two hours
5. Put the pieces of meat in a convenient basin, and place it upside downwards in the centre of a clean baking-tin an inch and a half deep, and the bottom of which must be strong enough to bear the heat of the fire
6. Place a trevet on a charcoal fire, and over which set the baking-tin for about an hour and a half
7. When the gravy has run out and is absorbed, pour in the baking-tin a pint and a half of broth No. 1 or No. 2, and add three-quarters of a pound of the best rice, well washed
8. Spread it even around the basin
9. When the broth is absorbed, and the rice is hard, sprinkle some more broth over
10. Each grain should be soft, and well separated
11. When done, take the baking-tin off the fire, and just at the time it is wanted, remove the basin from the centre of it, and serve as it is, with a napkin pinned round the baking-tin

title: Tdss Kebdbi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a leg or loin of mutton
- two or three finely-chopped onions
- salt
- pepper
- mixed spice
- a pint and a half of broth No. 1 or No. 2
- three-quarters of a pound of the best rice

## Directions

1. Cut sufficient quantity of a leg or loin of mutton in pieces the size of small walnuts
2. Wash them with cold water, and dry them in a cloth
3. Sprinkle two or three finely-chopped onions over, with sufficient salt, pepper, and mixed spice
4. Rub them well in the meat, and let it remain covered for about two hours
5. Put the pieces of meat in a convenient basin, and place it upside downwards in the centre of a clean baking-tin an inch and a half deep, and the bottom of which must be strong enough to bear the heat of the fire
6. Place a trevet on a charcoal fire, and over which set the baking-tin for about an hour and a half
7. When the gravy has run out and is absorbed, pour in the baking-tin a pint and a half of broth No. 1 or No. 2, and add three-quarters of a pound of the best rice, well washed
8. Spread it even around the basin
9. When the broth is absorbed, and the rice is hard, sprinkle some more broth over
10. Each grain should be soft, and well separated
11. When done, take the baking-tin off the fire, and, just at the time it is wanted, remove the basin from the centre of it, and serve as it is, with a napkin pinned round the baking-tin

title: Tdwuh Ydhni
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- mutton
- water
- salt
- fresh butter
- onions
- chick-peas
- wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut a well-cleaned fowl in two lengthways
2. Place it in a stewpan with two or three pounds of the middle of a neck of mutton, cover them with water, put in a little salt, set it on the fire, and let it boil till the fowl is half cooked, and skim it well
3. Take the fowl out and fry it a nice colour in fresh butter
4. Place it in a stewpan with six or eight sliced onions fried in butter, a pint and a quarter of chick-peas, previously boiled a little soft and skinned, two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar, and the liquor in which the fowl is boiled, add sufficient salt

title: Tdwuh Ydhnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one well-cleaned fowl
- two or three pounds of the middle of a neck of mutton
- water
- salt
- fresh butter
- six or eight onions
- a pint and a quarter of chick-peas
- two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut a well-cleaned fowl in two lengthways
2. Place it in a stewpan with two or three pounds of the middle of a neck of mutton
3. Cover them with water, put in a little salt
4. Set it on the fire and let it boil till the fowl is half cooked, and skim it well
5. Take the fowl out and fry it a nice colour in fresh butter
6. Place it in a stewpan with six or eight sliced onions fried in butter, a pint and a quarter of chick-peas previously boiled a little soft and skinned, two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar, and the liquor in which the fowl is boiled
7. Add sufficient salt and pepper
8. Put it on a slow charcoal fire and let it simmer until it forms a nice thick gravy at the bottom of the pan, and the peas are tender
9. Dish it up tastefully and serve

title: Tdwuh Ydhnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a well-cleaned fowl
- two or three pounds of the middle of a neck of mutton
- water
- salt
- fresh butter
- six or eight onions
- a pint and a quarter of chick-peas
- two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut a well-cleaned fowl in two lengthways
2. Place it in a stewpan with two or three pounds of the middle of a neck of mutton
3. Cover them with water, put in a little salt
4. Set it on the fire and let it boil till the fowl is half cooked
5. Skim it well
6. Take the fowl out and fry it a nice colour in fresh butter
7. Place it in a stewpan with six or eight sliced onions fried in butter
8. Add a pint and a quarter of chick-peas, previously boiled a little soft and skinned
9. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of wine vinegar and the liquor in which the fowl is boiled
10. Add sufficient salt and pepper
11. Put it on a slow charcoal fire and let it simmer until it forms a nice thick gravy at the bottom of the pan and the peas are tender
12. Dish it up tastefully and serve

title: Teke Salatassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- prawns
- parsley
- salt
- lemon juice
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Take off the skins and the heads of three or four dozen prawns
2. Arrange them in a dish with a handful or two of picked parsley
3. Sprinkle a pinch or two of salt over, with the juice of one or two lemons
4. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of the best oil, and serve

title: Teke Salatası
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- prawns
- parsley
- salt
- lemon juice
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Take off the skins and the heads of three or four dozen prawns
2. Arrange them in a dish with a handful or two of picked parsley
3. Sprinkle a pinch or two of salt over with the juice of one or two lemons and two or three tablespoonfuls of the best oil
4. Serve

title: Tekir-Betlighi Pulalcissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight red mullets
- two or three onions
- mixed spice
- salt
- pepper
- juice of one or two lemons
- yolks of two or three eggs
- water
- a quarter of a pint of olive oil
- chopped parsley
- half a pint of water
- half a teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Take out the gills and the insides of seven or eight red mullets, and wash them
2. Peel two or three onions, and well pound them in a mortar
3. Add a little mixed spice, salt, and pepper, the juice of one or two lemons, the yolks of two or three eggs, and a little water, and beat them up well together with a wooden spoon
4. Pour it over the mullets, and let them remain for about two hours
5. Put a quarter of a pint of olive oil in a flat stewpan, with a plate of chopped parsley
6. Put it on the fire, and just scald it
7. Add half a pint of water, half a teaspoonful of salt
8. When it boils, lay the mullets in with the ingredients, and let it simmer for fifteen or twenty minutes
9. Dish up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Tekir-Betlighi Puldlcissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight red mullets
- two or three onions
- mixed spice
- salt
- pepper
- juice of one or two lemons
- yolks of two or three eggs
- water
- a quarter of a pint of olive oil
- chopped parsley
- half a pint of water
- half a teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Take out the gills and the insides of seven or eight red mullets, and wash them
2. Peel two or three onions, and well pound them in a mortar
3. Add a little mixed spice, salt, and pepper, the juice of one or two lemons, the yolks of two or three eggs, and a little water, and beat them up well together with a wooden spoon
4. Pour it over the mullets, and let them remain for about two hours
5. Put a quarter of a pint of olive oil in a flat stewpan, with a plate of chopped parsley
6. Put it on the fire, and just scald it
7. Add half a pint of water, half a teaspoonful of salt
8. When it boils, lay the mullets in with the ingredients, and let it simmer for fifteen or twenty minutes
9. Dish up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Tenjere Kyulbassiissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- rump steak or veal
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- wine vinegar
- cinnamon
- cummin
- broth

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of rump steak or veal in slices the size of a hand, and about half an inch thick
2. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper over
3. Place them on a gridiron, set it on a moderate charcoal fire, and frequently turn them
4. When nicely browned, place them in a stewpan with a few cloves of garlic peeled and a half or a tea-cup of wine vinegar, a little cinnamon and cummin
5. Cover them with broth
6. Put the pan with the cover over on a moderate charcoal fire and let it simmer till tender
7. Serve

title: Tenjere Kyulbassussi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- rump steak or veal
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- wine vinegar
- cinnamon
- cummin
- broth
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Cut as much as is wanted of rump steak or veal in slices the size of a hand, and about half an inch thick
2. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper over, place them on a gridiron, set it on a moderate charcoal fire, and frequently turn them
3. When nicely browned, place them in a stewpan with a few cloves of garlic peeled, and a half or a tea-cup of wine vinegar, a little cinnamon and cummin
4. Cover them with broth, put the pan with the cover over on a moderate charcoal fire, and let it simmer till tender
5. Serve hot

title: Terbiyeli Jigher Chbrbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb's or sheep's lights
- salt
- water
- broth
- yolks of two or three eggs
- juice of one or two lemons or wine vinegar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Get a lamb's or sheep's lights and cut it into small dice
2. Put them in a stewpan with a little salt, and a small quantity of water
3. Put it on the fire until the lights are nearly cooked
4. Add three or four pints of broth, and let it simmer till the pieces of lights are quite done
5. Beat up the yolks of two or three eggs in a small saucepan, with the juice of one or two lemons, or with a little wine vinegar, and stir it on a slow fire with a wooden spoon till a little thick
6. Take it off and pour it gradually into the soup, stirring it quickly at the same time, to prevent the eggs from curdling
7. Pour the soup into a tureen, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Terbiyeli Jigher Chorbassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb's or sheep's lights
- salt
- water
- broth
- yolks of two or three eggs
- juice of one or two lemons or wine vinegar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Get a lamb's or sheep's lights and cut it into small dice
2. Put them in a stewpan with a little salt, and a small quantity of water
3. Put it on the fire until the lights are nearly cooked
4. Add three or four pints of broth, and let it simmer till the pieces of lights are quite done
5. Beat up the yolks of two or three eggs in a small saucepan, with the juice of one or two lemons, or with a little wine vinegar
6. Stir it on a slow fire with a wooden spoon till a little thick
7. Take it off and pour it gradually into the soup, stirring it quickly at the same time, to prevent the eggs from curdling
8. Pour the soup into a tureen, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Terbiyyeli Kyufte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three pounds of raw mutton
- two or three onions
- salt
- pepper
- water
- yolks of three or four eggs
- juice of a lemon or two

## Directions

1. Mince very fine two or three pounds of the best part of raw mutton, with two or three onions peeled, and a little salt and pepper
2. Make it in balls the size of nuts
3. Put them in a saucepan half full of water, set it on the fire until done
4. Beat up the yolks of three or four eggs, with the juice of a lemon or two, and a little salt
5. Pour it in a saucepan, put it on a slow fire, and keep stirring until it begins to thicken
6. Add three or four tablespoonfuls (one at a time) of the liquor in which the balls of meat have been boiling
7. Stir it for two or three minutes longer, then dish the meat up, pour this sauce over

title: Terbiyyeli Kyufte
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three pounds of raw mutton
- two or three onions peeled
- salt
- pepper
- water
- yolks of three or four eggs
- juice of a lemon or two

## Directions

1. Mince very fine two or three pounds of the best part of raw mutton, with two or three onions peeled, and a little salt and pepper
2. Make it in balls the size of nuts
3. Put them in a saucepan half full of water, set it on the fire until done
4. Beat up the yolks of three or four eggs, with the juice of a lemon or two, and a little salt
5. Pour it in a saucepan, put it on a slow fire, and keep stirring until it begins to thicken
6. Add three or four tablespoonfuls (one at a time) of the liquor in which the balls of meat have been boiling
7. Stir it for two or three minutes longer, then dish the meat up, pour this sauce

title: Tertibi Digher
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and a quarter of fresh butter
- six eggs
- two pounds and a half of the best honey or powdered sugar
- about two pounds and a half of nice sago
- five or six ounces of loaf-sugar
- about three pints of water
- half a teacup of rose-water
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare a pound and a quarter of fresh butter, break six eggs over, and beat up well together
2. Add gradually two pounds and a half of the best honey or powdered sugar, previously made into a syrup, and beat them up well
3. Add by degrees about two pounds and a half of nice sago, bringing it to the consistency of a thick batter
4. Pour it in a rather deep baking-tin, previously buttered
5. Bake it a nice brown in a hot oven
6. Dissolve five or six ounces of loaf-sugar in about three pints of water, with half a teacup of rose-water, and keep it lukewarm
7. When the pudding is nicely done, pour it all over, and put it again in the oven
8. When the whole of the liquid is absorbed, take it out, cut it up in any shape you fancy, dish up, and serve with powdered sugar over

title: Thtlu Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five eggs
- tablespoonful of milk
- dessertspoonful of powdered sugar
- salt
- three or four ounces of fresh butter
- powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Break five eggs in a basin, and beat them up
2. Add a tablespoonful of milk, a dessertspoonful of powdered sugar, a little salt, and beat them well up
3. Put three or four ounces of fresh butter in the frying-pan, pour in the eggs, and fry a nice colour
4. Serve with powdered sugar over

title: Thtlu Khyghana
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five eggs
- a tablespoonful of milk
- a dessertspoonful of powdered sugar
- a little salt
- three or four ounces of fresh butter
- powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Break five eggs in a basin, and beat them up
2. Add a tablespoonful of milk, a dessertspoonful of powdered sugar, a little salt, and beat them well up
3. Put three or four ounces of fresh butter in the frying-pan, pour in the eggs, and fry a nice colour
4. Serve with powdered sugar over

title: Thwuh Ghyuhssu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three chickens
- five pints and a half of nice milk
- three-quarters of a pound of rice flour
- water
- three-quarters of a pound of powdered sugar
- one or two tablespoonfuls of rose-water
- almond oil
- powdered cinnamon

## Directions

1. Boil two or three chickens.
2. When done, cut off the breasts, remove the bones and the skins from them, and pound the meat in a stone mortar, or pull it in bits like fine threads.
3. Pour in a stewpan five pints and a half of nice milk, put it on the fire.
4. While boiling, put in a basin three-quarters of a pound of rice flour, which mix with some of the boiling milk and some water, to form a batter.
5. Add this to the milk and boil it until it becomes a smooth substance, but not too thick.
6. Add the pounded breasts of chickens with three-quarters of a pound of powdered sugar, and one or two tablespoonfuls of rose-water.
7. Keep stirring, and at the same time beating it up on a slow fire until it forms a rather thick glutinous substance.
8. Remove it from the fire, stir it for a few minutes longer.
9. Fill a mould previously oiled with almond oil, and place it in cool place.
10. When ready, dip the mould into warm water, and turn it out on to a dish.
11. Sprinkle a little powdered cinnamon over, and serve.
12. Rinse the pounded breasts of the chickens once or twice in cold water before you add them to the milk and ground rice.

title: Thwuk Dolmdssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three chickens
- salt
- the livers, hearts, and gizzards of the chickens
- fresh butter
- three-quarters of a pound of well-washed rice
- two or three tablespoonfuls of pistachios
- a tablespoonful of currants
- pepper
- a little cinnamon
- a small teacup of hot water
- broth

## Directions

1. Get two or three chickens and well clean them
2. Sprinkle a little salt over and let them remain for ten minutes or so
3. Chop up the livers, hearts, and the gizzards very fine
4. Place them in a frying-pan with sufficient fresh butter
5. Add three-quarters of a pound of well-washed rice, two or three tablespoonfuls of pistachios, a tablespoonful of currants, sufficient salt and pepper, and a little cinnamon
6. Stir round for a few minutes
7. Add a small teacup of hot water and keep stirring till the water is absorbed and the rice partly soft
8. Stuff the chickens with it and sew them up
9. Fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
10. Place them in a stewpan and nearly cover them with broth
11. Put the pan on a charcoal fire with the cover over and let them simmer till the chickens are quite tender
12. Dish up and serve

title: Thwuk Dolmessi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three chickens
- salt
- chicken livers, hearts, and gizzards
- fresh butter
- three-quarters of a pound of well-washed rice
- two or three tablespoonfuls of pistachios
- a tablespoonful of currants
- pepper
- cinnamon
- a small teacup of hot water
- broth

## Directions

1. Get two or three chickens, well clean them
2. Sprinkle a little salt over, and let them remain for ten minutes or so
3. Chop up the livers, hearts, and the gizzards, very fine
4. Place them in a frying-pan with sufficient fresh butter
5. Add three-quarters of a pound of well-washed rice, two or three tablespoonfuls of pistachios, a tablespoonful of currants, sufficient salt and pepper, and a little cinnamon
6. Stir round for a few minutes
7. Add a small teacup of hot water, and keep stirring till the water is absorbed and the rice partly soft
8. Stuff the chickens with it, sew them up, and fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
9. Place them in a stewpan, nearly cover them with broth
10. Put the pan on a charcoal fire with the cover over, and let them simmer till the chickens are quite tender
11. Dish up, and serve

title: Timur-Hindi Shurubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces of tamarinds
- two pints and a gill of water
- three pounds of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Put six ounces of tamarinds in an earthen pan with two pints and a gill of water
2. Set it to boil until it is reduced to a pint and a half
3. Take it off and pass the liquor through a sieve into a stewpan, add three pounds of powdered sugar, and boil it for half a minute
4. Take it off
5. When nearly cold, pass it through a clean cloth, bottle, and cork it up till wanted

title: Top-Marul Salatassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage-lettuces
- beet-root
- eggs, hard-boiled
- chervil, chopped
- tarragon
- chives or young onions
- salt
- pepper
- olive oil
- wine vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Procure two or three cabbage-lettuces
2. Remove the outer leaves and throw them away
3. Pull the others one by one
4. Wash and well drain them in a cloth
5. Put them in the bowl, with a few slices of beet-root, one or two hard-boiled eggs cut in quarters
6. Add two teaspoonfuls of chopped chervil, one of tarragon, and one of chives or young onions
7. Sprinkle over with your fingers half a teaspoonful of salt, one or two pinches of pepper
8. Add two or three tablespoonfuls of the best olive oil, and one and-a-half of wine vinegar, or the juice of a lemon
9. Turn the salad in the bowl with a spoon and fork until well mixed, and serve

title: Turk Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- pound of flour
- salt
- eggs
- water
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put on the dresser a pound of flour, make a hole in the centre, in which put a teaspoonful of salt
2. Break in two eggs, mix all together
3. Add sufficient water, and form it a softish flexible paste
4. Divide it in two, and roll them out to the size of the baking-tin you intend to use
5. Sprinkle some melted fresh butter over, and fold over the edges of the paste so as to hide the butter
6. Throw a little flour both under and over, and roll them out again as before
7. Repeat the same five or six times more
8. Butter the baking-tin, in which lay one of the two pieces of paste
9. Lay any preparation you choose all over it, half an inch thick, and cover it with the other paste
10. Make about fifteen deep holes in the paste here and there with the point of a knife
11. Bake it a nice brown on both sides
12. When done, cut it in diamonds, dish up tastefully, and serve hot

title: Uskkumru Tāwassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen middle-sized mackerel
- salt
- two or three onions
- pepper
- mixed spice
- juice of two or three lemons
- yolks of three or four eggs
- hot olive oil
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take out the insides and the gills of half a dozen middle-sized mackerel, wash well, and sprinkle them with a little salt, and let them remain twenty minutes or so.
2. Peel two or three onions, and well pound them in a mortar, to which add sufficient salt and pepper, mixed spice, the juice of two or three lemons, and the yolks of three or four eggs, and well beat them up.
3. Pour it into a pie-dish, roll the mackerel in it, and let them remain for about two hours.
4. Fry them a nice brown in hot olive oil, turning them over.
5. When done, take them out with a slice and let them drain well, dish them up tastefully, squeeze a lemon over, if approved of, and serve hot.

title: Uskumru Tâvâssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- mixed spice
- lemons
- eggs
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Take out the insides and the gills of half a dozen middle-sized mackerel, wash well, and sprinkle them with a little salt, and let them remain twenty minutes or so
2. Peel two or three onions, and well pound them in a mortar, to which add sufficient salt and pepper, mixed spice, the juice of two or three lemons, and the yolks of three or four eggs, and well beat them up
3. Pour it into a pie-dish, roll the mackerel in it, and let them remain for about two hours
4. Fry them a nice brown in hot olive oil, turning them over
5. When done, take them out with a slice and let them drain well, dish them up tastefully, squeeze a lemon over, if approved of, and serve hot

title: Ussakumru Balıglı Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mackerel
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- fennel
- bay-leaf

## Directions

1. Get as many fresh mackerel as are required, cut off the point of the head
2. If large, divide each one in three pieces; if small, in two pieces
3. Wash and dry them in a cloth
4. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt and pepper, and a handful or two of chopped parsley mixed with fennel, and let them remain for an hour
5. Put them on skewers, with a bay-leaf between each piece, and cook them before a moderate fire, turning them until nicely browned

title: Usscumru Balighindan Papas Yahnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen mackerel
- water
- three or four onions
- olive oil
- a handful of picked parsley
- a little fennel
- half a teacup of wine vinegar
- two or three teacups of water
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- one or two tomatoes
- a tablespoonful of preserved tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Take out the inside of half a dozen mackerel and wash them
2. Boil them in water for one or two minutes
3. When cold, remove the bones
4. Cut three or four onions in slices and fry them partly brown in olive oil
5. Lay the half on the bottom of a stewpan with a handful of picked parsley mixed with a little fennel
6. Pour in what oil is left in the frying-pan
7. Place the pieces of mackerel over
8. Put the other half of the onions with the same quantity of parsley and fennel on the top
9. Add half a teacup of wine vinegar and two or three teacups of the water in which the fish is boiled
10. Add sufficient salt and pepper
11. Put the pan on a moderate charcoal fire with the lid on and let it simmer gently for about fifteen or twenty minutes until the fish is nicely done
12. Dish it up, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Usshumru Balighi Tereturu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel, fresh
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and boil three or four very fresh mackerel
2. When cold, remove the heads and extract all the bones
3. Arrange the fish in a dish
4. Dress it as No. 203
5. Sprinkle a little cayenne pepper over, and serve

title: UssJcumru Balighindan Papdss Yahnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen mackerel
- water
- three or four onions
- olive oil
- a handful of picked parsley
- a little fennel
- half a teacup of wine vinegar
- two or three teacups of the water in which the fish is boiled
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- one or two tomatoes
- a tablespoonful of preserved tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Take out the inside of half a dozen mackerel and wash them
2. Boil them in water for one or two minutes
3. When cold, remove the bones
4. Cut three or four onions in slices and fry them partly brown in olive oil
5. Lay half on the bottom of a stewpan with a handful of picked parsley mixed with a little fennel and pour in what oil is left in the fryingpan
6. Place the pieces of mackerel over and then the other half of the onions with the same quantity of parsley and fennel on the top
7. Add half a teacup of wine vinegar, two or three teacups of the water in which the fish is boiled, sufficient salt and pepper
8. Put the pan on a moderate charcoal fire with the lid on and let it simmer gently until the fish is nicely done, which will take about fifteen or twenty minutes
9. Dish it up, sprinkle a little cinnamon over, and serve

title: Ussk-umru-Balighi Kyulbasstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four mackerel
- salt
- olive oil
- parsley
- onions
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean three or four mackerel, cut off the points of the heads
2. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt, and let them remain for about an hour
3. Rub a gridiron with some olive oil, lay the mackerel over
4. Broil gently on a charcoal fire
5. When done, dish it up, place some chopped parsley mixed with finely-sliced onions over
6. Serve hot
7. A lemon squeezed over the fish will improve the flavour

title: Usskumru Balighi Kebabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mackerel
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- fennel
- bay-leaf

## Directions

1. Cut off the point of the head of the mackerel
2. If large, divide each one in three pieces; if small, in two pieces
3. Wash and dry them in a cloth
4. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt and pepper, and a handful or two of chopped parsley mixed with fennel
5. Let them remain for an hour
6. Put them on skewers, with a bay-leaf between each piece
7. Cook them as above

title: Ussukmru Balghi Terethuru
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and boil three or four very fresh mackerel
2. When cold, remove the heads and extract all the bones
3. Arrange the fish in a dish and dress it as No. 203
4. Sprinkle a little cayenne pepper over and serve

title: Ussukumru-Balighi Kyulbasstissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four mackerel
- salt
- olive oil
- parsley
- onions
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean three or four mackerel, cut off the points of the heads
2. Sprinkle them with sufficient salt, and let them remain for about an hour
3. Rub a gridiron with some olive oil, lay the mackerel over
4. Broil gently on a charcoal fire
5. When done, dish it up
6. Place some chopped parsley mixed with finely-sliced onions over
7. Serve hot, with a lemon squeezed over the fish

title: Uzuin Turshussu
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- ten or twelve pounds of black or white grapes
- mustard-seeds

## Directions

1. Pick out the best bunches of grapes
2. Squeeze the juice from the bruised and small ones
3. Pass it through a sieve into a stewpan free from smell
4. Set it to boil for a few minutes
5. Wash the bunches you have selected
6. Lay two or three of them on the bottom of a stone jar
7. Sprinkle some mustard-seeds over, then grapes, again mustard-seeds over, then grapes, continuing so till the whole of the grapes are laid
8. Pour the boiled grape-juice over
9. Cover the jar air-tight and let it remain for thirty or forty days before using

title: Vishna Ekmeghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of loaf sugar
- a pint of water
- two pounds of morella cherries
- two French penny rolls
- yolks of two eggs
- eight tablespoonfuls of milk
- hot fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put a pound of loaf sugar into a stewpan with a pint of water, put it on the fire
2. When boiling, skim it, then add two pounds of morella cherries with the stalks taken off and the stones taken out
3. Stir with a wooden spoon, and boil them until getting tender
4. Cut two French penny rolls lengthwise in eight slices
5. Put the yolks of two eggs in a basin, with eight tablespoonfuls of milk, and beat it up well
6. Dip quickly each slice in it, and fry it a nice colour on both sides in hot fresh butter
7. When all done, lay them in a convenient pie-dish, then put the cherries over with the syrup

title: Vishna Kdshabi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of fresh morella cherries
- a pound and a quarter of crushed loaf sugar
- two or three pints of water
- a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or rose
- three or four pieces of ice

## Directions

1. Pick out the large cherries and squeeze the juice from the small ones through a cloth into a saucepan
2. Add a pound and a quarter of crushed loaf sugar and boil it for one minute
3. Put the large cherries into a stewpan with two or three pints of water and set it to boil until the cherries are tender
4. Take it off and let it partly cool
5. Take the cherries out carefully, without bruising them
6. Put about two pints of the water in which the cherries are boiled in a glass bowl
7. Sweeten it with the syrup which you have prepared, according to palate
8. Add the cherries, with a few drops of the essence of orange-flower or rose, and three or four pieces of ice
9. Serve

title: Vishna Shier Ubi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- morella cherries
- sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Squeeze as many pounds of morella cherries as are wanted, and pass the juice through a cloth into a basin, and leave it for twenty-four hours
2. Strain it gently into another basin, and throw the residue away
3. Put the juice in a very clean stewpan, with double the quantity of sifted sugar
4. Stir till the sugar is dissolved, and set it to boil
5. As soon as it bubbles up take it off, and pass it through a sieve or cloth
6. When cold, bottle and cork it up until wanted

title: Viskna Recheli
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds morella cherries
- 4 pounds white sugar
- 1 pint of water

## Directions

1. Take off the stalks and extract the stones of the cherries
2. Put 4 pounds of white sugar in a stewpan with a pint of water
3. Put it on the fire until it begins to thicken
4. Add the cherries and stir with a wooden spoon
5. Boil till rather thick
6. Try a little on a plate to see if it sets
7. When cold, fill the reserve jars or glass bowls with covers
8. Let them remain in a cool place

title: Viskna Recheli
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of morella cherries
- four pounds of white sugar
- one pint of water

## Directions

1. Take off the stalks and extract the stones of two pounds of morella cherries.
2. Put four pounds of white sugar in a stewpan with a pint of water.
3. Put it on the fire until it begins to thicken.
4. Add the cherries and stir with a wooden spoon.
5. Boil till rather thick.
6. Try a little on a plate.
7. If it sets, take it off.
8. When cold, fill the reserve jars or glass bowls with covers.
9. Let them remain in a cool place.

title: Yaghsiz Balik Yahnissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven red mullets
- salt
- three pints of water
- three or four onions
- three or four heads of garlic
- two or three onions
- two or three handfuls of parsley
- pepper
- half a lemon

## Directions

1. Get six or seven red mullets, take out the insides, well clean them, and sprinkle some salt over
2. Put three pints of water in a saucepan with three or four onions cut in four crossways and three or four heads of garlic with only the outer skins peeled
3. Put it on the fire and let boil until the water is reduced to about one pint
4. Take the onions and the garlic out, throw the onions away, but squeeze the garlic and add the pulp to the liquor
5. Peel two or three onions and chop them very fine with two or three handfuls of parsley
6. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper over and well rub them
7. Stuff the fish with it and lay it carefully in a stewpan
8. Pour the liquor over, set it on a charcoal fire and let it simmer till the fish is nicely cooked
9. Dish it up, squeeze half a lemon over, and serve hot

title: Yagissiz Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two fat fowls
- water
- sufficient salt
- two measures of liquor to one of well-washed rice
- two or three pieces of cinnamon
- a few pieces of gum-mastic

## Directions

1. Put one or two fat fowls in a saucepan nearly full of water, and sufficient salt, and put it on the fire.
2. When boiling, skim and simmer until the meat separates from the bones.
3. Take it off, and pass the whole through a clean cloth or sieve by pressing and squeezing it with your hand, and cast the residue away.
4. Put in the saucepan two measures of the liquor to one of well-washed rice, add two or three pieces of cinnamon, and a few pieces of gum-mastic.
5. Set it on a charcoal fire till the liquor is absorbed.
6. Take it off, and place it on the hob, stir it once round with a fork, and let it remain covered for fifteen minutes or so.
7. Take out the pieces of cinnamon, dish up, and serve hot.

title: Yalcinji-Bahk-Dholmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen rather thin and long egg-plants
- olive oil or fresh butter
- four or five ounces
- three or four onions
- half a pound of well-washed rice
- half a teacup of hot water
- half a white plain biscuit
- two ounces of skinned pistachios
- one ounce of currants
- salt
- pepper
- mixed spice
- rib-bones of mutton

## Directions

1. Peel egg-plants very thin in spots here and there (imitating the fish scales)
2. Cut off the tops, as large as a florin, and take out the whole of the inside, leaving only sufficient to bear stuffing
3. Throw them in salt water for ten or fifteen minutes
4. Put four or five ounces of nice olive oil or fresh butter in a saucepan, and set it on the fire
5. When hot, add three or four onions chopped fine, and begin stirring it with a wooden spoon
6. When on the point of browning, add half a pound of well-washed rice, and keep stirring
7. As soon as the rice begins to brown, pour in half a teacup of hot water, and continue stirring
8. When the water is absorbed, add another half a teacup of water, and keep stirring
9. When the rice is partly soft, add the half of a white plain biscuit, well powdered, two ounces of skinned pistachios, one of currants, sufficient salt, pepper, and a little mixed spice; stir well; then take it off
10. When partly cold, stuff the egg-plants with it, and cover them with the tops contrariwise
11. Cover the bottom of a saucepan with some rib-bones of mutton, and place the egg-plants over
12. Cover them with water, set the pan on the fire with the lid over, and let it simmer till the egg-plants are partly soft
13. Take them out and fry them a nice colour in hot olive oil or fresh butter
14. Dish them up tastefully, and serve

title: Yalcinji-Ddlmci
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight dozens of young vine leaves
- three-quarters of a pint of olive oil
- two pounds and a half of finely chopped onions
- two pounds and a half of the best rice
- two teacupfuls of water
- one or two teaspoonfuls of powdered mint
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- rib-bones of mutton
- eight or ten sour plums

## Directions

1. Procure seven or eight dozens of young vine leaves, and scald them
2. Let them drain in a colander
3. Pour in a saucepan three-quarters of a pint of olive oil and place it on the fire
4. When hot, put in two pounds and a half of finely chopped onions, and stir until they begin to brown
5. Add two pounds and a half of the best rice, well cleaned, and keep stirring
6. When the rice is on the point of browning, add two teacupfuls of water, and continue stirring gently till the water is absorbed
7. Take it off, add one or two teaspoonfuls of powdered mint, a little salt, pepper, and cinnamon, and well stir it
8. Spread the vine leaves out, and put half a tablespoonful of the prepared rice in each one
9. Fold it about the length of your thumb, but a little thicker, and squeeze it gently in your hand, so that it may not open while cooking
10. Cover the bottom of a saucepan with rib-bones of mutton, and over which place the stuffed leaves one by one
11. Arrange between them eight or ten sour plums
12. Press them with a plate, then nearly cover them with water, and set the pan on the fire
13. When boiling, let it simmer until there is no liquid remaining
14. Take one out and try if the rice is soft; if not, add some more water
15. When soft, and each grain is separate, dish them up tastefully, and serve

title: Yalcinji-Dolmci
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight dozens of young vine leaves
- three-quarters of a pint of olive oil
- two pounds and a half of finely chopped onions
- two pounds and a half of the best rice
- two teacupfuls of water
- one or two teaspoonfuls of powdered mint
- salt
- pepper
- cinnamon
- rib-bones of mutton
- eight or ten sour plums

## Directions

1. Procure seven or eight dozens of young vine leaves, and scald them
2. Let them drain in a colander
3. Pour in a saucepan three-quarters of a pint of olive oil; place it on the fire
4. When hot, put in two pounds and a half of finely chopped onions, and stir until they begin to brown
5. Add two pounds and a half of the best rice, well cleaned, and keep stirring
6. When the rice is on the point of browning, add two teacupfuls of water, and continue stirring gently till the water is absorbed
7. Take it off, add one or two teaspoonfuls of powdered mint, a little salt, pepper, and cinnamon, and well stir it
8. Spread the vine leaves out, and put half a tablespoonful of the prepared rice in each one, fold it about the length of your thumb, but a little thicker, and squeeze it gently in your hand, so that it may not open while cooking
9. Cover the bottom of a saucepan with rib-bones of mutton, and over which place the stuffed leaves one by one, arranging, at the same time, between them eight or ten sour plums
10. When the whole is laid, press them with a plate, then nearly cover them with water, and set the pan on the fire
11. When boiling, let it simmer until there is no liquid remaining
12. Take one out and try if the rice is soft; if not, add some more water
13. When soft, and each grain is separate, dish them up tastefully, and serve

title: Yaldnji-Bahk-Dbhnassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen rather thin and long egg-plants
- four or five ounces of nice olive oil or fresh butter
- three or four onions
- half a pound of well-washed rice
- half a white plain biscuit
- two ounces of skinned pistachios
- one ounce of currants
- salt
- pepper
- mixed spice
- rib-bones of mutton
- water

## Directions

1. Peel egg-plants very thin in spots here and there (imitating the fish scales)
2. Cut off the tops, as large as a florin, and take out the whole of the inside, leaving only sufficient to bear stuffing
3. Throw them in salt water for ten or fifteen minutes
4. Put four or five ounces of nice olive oil or fresh butter in a saucepan, and set it on the fire
5. When hot, add three or four onions chopped fine, and begin stirring it with a wooden spoon
6. When on the point of browning, add half a pound of well-washed rice, and keep stirring
7. As soon as the rice begins to brown, pour in half a teacup of hot water, and continue stirring
8. When the water is absorbed, add another half a teacup of water, and keep stirring
9. When the rice is partly soft, add the half of a white plain biscuit well powdered, two ounces of skinned pistachios, one of currants, sufficient salt, pepper, and a little mixed spice
10. Stir well and take it off
11. When partly cold, stuff the egg-plants with it, and cover them with the tops contrariwise
12. Cover the bottom of a saucepan with some rib-bones of mutton, and place the egg-plants over
13. Cover them with water, set the pan on the fire with the lid over, and let it simmer till the egg-plants are partly soft
14. Take them out and fry them a nice colour in hot olive oil or fresh butter
15. Dish them up tastefully, and serve

title: Ydghsiz Kaddyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- crumpets
- milk
- powdered sugar
- clotted cream

## Directions

1. Lay four or five crumpets in a rather deep baking-tin
2. Pour over them half a pint of milk
3. Sprinkle powdered sugar over
4. Lay a layer of clotted cream
5. Lay an equal number of crumpets over
6. Pour half a pint more milk, then sugar
7. Put it on a charcoal fire and let it boil for a little while
8. Take it off just before the cream is melted
9. When rather cold, dish them up carefully
10. Shake some powdered sugar over and serve

title: Ydghsiz Usscumru Pulalcissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight mackerel
- picked parsley
- powdered gum-mastic
- salt
- pepper
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take out the insides and the gills of seven or eight mackerel, wash them, and cut off the points of the heads and the tails
2. Lay some picked parsley on the bottom of a stewpan, place half of the fish over, then sprinkle a little powdered gum-mastic over, some parsley again
3. Lay the other half of the fish over, gum-mastic again, then parsley on the top
4. Add sufficient salt and pepper, and three-quarters of a pint of water
5. Put the pan on a charcoal fire, and let simmer gently for twenty minutes or so
6. Dish it up, squeeze half or the whole of a lemon over, and serve

title: Ydghsiz Usskumru Pulalcissi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight mackerel
- picked parsley
- powdered gum-mastic
- salt
- pepper
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- half or whole of a lemon

## Directions

1. Take out the insides and the gills of seven or eight mackerel, wash them, and cut off the points of the heads and the tails
2. Lay some picked parsley on the bottom of a stewpan, place half of the fish over
3. Sprinkle a little powdered gum-mastic over, some parsley again
4. Lay the other half of the fish over, gum-mastic again, then parsley on the top
5. Add sufficient salt and pepper, and three-quarters of a pint of water
6. Put the pan on a charcoal fire, and let simmer gently for twenty minutes or so
7. Dish it up, squeeze half or the whole of a lemon over, and serve

title: Ydissi Kaddyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- crumpets
- loaf sugar
- water
- eggs
- butter
- pistachios or almonds
- essence of rose or orange-flower

## Directions

1. Get six or seven crumpets
2. Put a pound of loaf sugar into a saucepan, with two pints of water
3. Set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
4. Keep it boiling gently by the side of the fire
5. Beat up five or six eggs in a basin
6. Dip the crumpets one after the other in the eggs
7. Fry them a nice brown on both sides in scalded fresh butter
8. Throw them in the syrup
9. Take them out instantly and dish them up
10. Sprinkle between each one some pounded pistachios or almonds
11. Put one or two drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower in a tea-cup of water
12. Pour it over the crumpets
13. Put it in the oven or on a slow charcoal fire for five minutes or so
14. Then serve

title: Ydssi Kaddyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- crumpets
- loaf sugar
- water
- eggs
- butter
- pistachios or almonds
- essence of rose or orange-flower

## Directions

1. Get six or seven crumpets
2. Put a pound of loaf sugar into a saucepan, with two pints of water
3. Set it to boil until it becomes a syrup
4. Keep it boiling gently by the side of the fire
5. Beat up five or six eggs in a basin
6. Dip the crumpets one after the other in the egg mixture
7. Fry them a nice brown on both sides in scalded fresh butter
8. Throw them in the syrup
9. Take them out instantly and dish them up
10. Sprinkle between each one some pounded pistachios or almonds
11. Put one or two drops of the essence of rose or orange-flower in a tea-cup of water
12. Pour it over the crumpets
13. Put it in the oven or on a slow charcoal fire for five minutes or so
14. Then serve

title: Yhghssiz Pilaw
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two fat fowls
- water
- sufficient salt
- two measures of the liquor to one of well-washed rice
- two or three pieces of cinnamon
- a few pieces of gum-mastic

## Directions

1. Put one or two fat fowls in a saucepan nearly full of water, and sufficient salt, and put it on the fire.
2. When boiling, skim and simmer until the meat separates from the bones.
3. Take it off, and pass the whole through a clean cloth or sieve by pressing and squeezing it with your hand, and cast the residue away.
4. Put in the saucepan two measures of the liquor to one of well-washed rice, add two or three pieces of cinnamon, and a few pieces of gum-mastic.
5. Set it on a charcoal fire till the liquor is absorbed.
6. Take it off, and place it on the hob, stir it once round with a fork, and let it remain covered for fifteen minutes or so.
7. Take out the pieces of cinnamon, dish up, and serve hot.

title: Yimurta Lkmmssi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of flour
- salt
- water
- eggs
- cream

## Directions

1. Put a pound of flour in a stewpan with a small teaspoonful of salt
2. Mix with water to form a smooth batter
3. Set it on a charcoal fire and stir it till it becomes thick
4. Take it off and let it cool
5. When cold, beat it up with eggs until it becomes a smooth thick batter
6. Proceed exactly as No. 161
7. Dish up and serve with cream over, if approved of

title: Yimurta Lâkmassi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of flour
- salt
- water
- eggs
- cream

## Directions

1. Put a pound of flour in a stewpan with a small teaspoonful of salt
2. Mix with water to form a smooth batter
3. Set it on a charcoal fire and stir it till it becomes thick
4. Take it off and let it cool
5. When cold, beat it up with eggs until it becomes a smooth thick batter
6. Proceed exactly as No. 161
7. Dish up and serve with cream over, if approved of

title: Yoghurt
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four pints of pure milk
- two tablespoonfuls of old curd

## Directions

1. Put four pints of pure milk into a particularly clean stewpan
2. Put it on a charcoal fire and let it bubble once
3. Take it off and pour it into a basin
4. When cold enough to bear your finger in it, add two tablespoonfuls of old curd
5. Stir it well with the handle of a wooden spoon
6. Put the basin on some straw in a warm corner of the pantry
7. Cover the basin all over with a piece of flannel three or four thicknesses
8. Let it remain for ten or twelve hours
9. Serve as it is when nicely set with the top covered with cream

title: Yoghurtlu-Sha Ighdm
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five turnips
- salt
- flour
- fresh butter
- half a pint of broth (No. 1)
- curd
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel four or five turnips, cut them in slices about a quarter or a half an inch thick
2. Boil them in water with a little salt, partly tender, and let them drain
3. When free from water, dip them in flour, and fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
4. Lay them in a baking-pan or a pie-dish that will bear fire
5. Pour half a pint of broth (No. 1) over the turnips
6. Set the pan on a moderate charcoal fire, and let it simmer for eight or ten minutes
7. Take it off, and lay some curd over the turnips, over which sprinkle a little hot fresh butter, and a pinch of pepper, and serve hot

title: Yoghurtlu-Sha Ighdm
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five turnips
- a little salt
- flour
- fresh butter
- half a pint of broth (No. 1)
- curd
- a pinch of pepper

## Directions

1. Peel four or five turnips, cut them in slices about a quarter or a half an inch thick
2. Boil them in water with a little salt, partly tender, and let them drain
3. When free from water, dip them in flour, and fry them a nice brown in fresh butter
4. Lay them in a baking-pan or a pie-dish that will bear fire
5. Pour half a pint of broth (No. 1) over the turnips
6. Set the pan on a moderate charcoal fire, and let it simmer for eight or ten minutes
7. Take it off, and lay some curd over the turnips, over which sprinkle a little hot fresh butter, and a pinch of pepper, and serve hot

title: Yufkali Kaddyif
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- puff-paste
- vermicelli
- butter
- syrup

## Directions

1. Roll out very thin six pieces of puff-paste
2. Lay three of them on the bottom of a well-buttered baking-tin, one over the other
3. Lay some vermicelli (No. 149) over, about half or three-quarters of an inch thick
4. Pour five or six tablespoonfuls of scalded fresh butter all over
5. Cover it with the other three pieces of paste
6. Prick it here and there with a carving-fork
7. Bake it a nice brown on both sides
8. When done pour some syrup over as No. 149
9. Let it remain covered for ten minutes or so
10. Cut it in diamonds, or any other fanciful shape you please
11. Dish up tastefully and serve

title: Yufkâ Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- salt
- water
- eggs
- broth
- fresh butter
- fried minced meat
- mutton or beef
- onions

## Directions

1. Place two or three pounds of flour on the table-dresser, or in a pan, make a hole in the centre, put in a little salt, and sufficient water, and stir it well up with a wooden spoon
2. Throw a little flour over, and knead well with the hands until it forms a nice paste
3. Divide it into small pieces (say eight or ten), and roll them out thin, about the thickness of a sixpence, and the size of a baking-tin intended for laying them in
4. Bake them soft, one by one, on a hot stove or a baking-sheet, which is placed over a trevet on a wood or charcoal fire
5. When done nicely on both sides, beat up two or three eggs in a basin, with a teacup of broth
6. Grease the baking-tin with fresh butter, and lay in half the pieces of paste one over the other, sprinkling some of the beaten-up egg, and fresh butter between each one with a quill or a paste-brush
7. Lay some fried minced meat all over, half an inch thick, which is prepared from raw mutton or beef, and then cover it with the remaining pieces of paste, as above
8. Egg and butter over, place it in the oven until nicely browned on both sides
9. When done, cut it in diamonds, or any other shape, dish up, and serve hot

title: Yufkā Bureghi
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three pounds of flour
- salt
- water
- two or three eggs
- teacup of broth
- fresh butter
- fried minced meat

## Directions

1. Place two or three pounds of flour on the table-dresser, or in a pan, make a hole in the centre, put in a little salt, and sufficient water, and stir it well up with a wooden spoon.
2. Throw a little flour over, and knead well with the hands until it forms a nice paste.
3. Divide it into small pieces (say eight or ten), and roll them out thin, about the thickness of a sixpence, and the size of a baking-tin intended for laying them in.
4. Bake them soft, one by one, on a hot stove (such as crumpets are baked on), or a baking-sheet, which is placed over a trevet on a wood or charcoal fire.
5. When done nicely on both sides, beat up two or three eggs in a basin, with a teacup of broth (No. 1 or No. 2).
6. Grease the baking-tin with fresh butter, and lay in half the pieces of paste one over the other, sprinkling some of the beaten-up egg, and fresh butter between each one with a quill or a paste-brush.
7. Lay some fried minced meat all over, half an inch thick, which is prepared from raw mutton or beef.
8. Cover it with the remaining pieces of paste, as above with egg and butter over.
9. Place it in the oven until nicely browned on both sides.
10. When done, cut it in diamonds, or any other shape, dish up, and serve hot.

title: Zubdayi Khunkyari
author: Turabi Efendi
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six ounces of powdered sugar
- three-quarters of a teacup of rose-water
- a little musk
- two or three small glasses of maraschino
- an ounce and a half of melted isinglass
- a pint of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Put in a basin five or six ounces of powdered sugar, three-quarters of a teacup of rose-water, mixed with a little musk, two or three small glasses of maraschino, an ounce and a half of melted isinglass
2. Stir it well, then add a pint of whipped cream
3. Stir it again three or four times, put it in a mould, and set it on ice until firm
4. Turn it out and serve
